name: ABCUBKR
label: Unused Commitments To Provide Liquidity To Conduit Structures by the bank
definition: ( YTD, % ) Conduits Sponsored by the Bank - Unused Commitments to Provide Liquidity to Conduit Structures: Asset-Backed Commercial Paper Conduits Sponsored By The Bank, A Bank Affiliate, Or The Bank's Holding Company.

name: ABCUOTH
label: Unused Commitments To Provide Liquidity, Unrelated Institutions
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Conduits Sponsored by Other Unrelated Institutions Unused Commitments To Provide Liquidity To Conduit Structures: Asset-Backed Commercial Paper Conduits Sponsored By Other Unrelated Institutions.

name: ABCUOTHR
label: Unused Commitments To Provide Liquidity, Unrelated Institutions
definition: ( YTD, % ) Conduits Sponsored by Other Unrelated Institutions Unused Commitments To Provide Liquidity To Conduit Structures: Asset-Backed Commercial Paper Conduits Sponsored By Other Unrelated Institutions.

name: ABCXBK
label: Maximum Credit Exposure Asset-Backed Commercial Paper Conduits Sponsored By Bank
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Conduits Sponsored by the Bank Maximum Amount Of Credit Exposure Arising From Credit Enhancements Provided To Conduit Structures In The Form Of Standby Letters Of Credit, Subordinate Securities, And Other Enhancements - Assets-Backed Commercial Paper Conduits Sponsored By The Bank, A Bank Affiliate Or The Bank's Holding Company.

name: ABCXBKR
label: Maximum Credit Exposure Asset-Backed Commercial Paper Conduits Sponsored By Bank
definition: ( YTD, % ) Conduits Sponsored by the Bank Maximum Amount Of Credit Exposure Arising From Credit Enhancements Provided To Conduit Structures In The Form Of Standby Letters Of Credit, Subordinate Securities, And Other Enhancements - Assets-Backed Commercial Paper Conduits Sponsored By The Bank, A Bank Affiliate Or The Bank's Holding Company.

name: ABCXOTH
label: Maximum Credit Exposure Asset-Backed Commercial Paper Conduits Sponsored By Unrelated Institutions
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Conduits Sponsored by Other Unrelated Institutions Maximum Amount Of Credit Exposure Arising From Credit Enhancements Provided To Conduit Structures In The Form Of Standby Letters Of Credit, Subordinate Securities, And Other Enhancements - Assets-Backed Commercial Paper Conduits Sponsored By Other Unrelated Institutions.

name: ABCXOTHR
label: Maximum Credit Exposure Asset-Backed Commercial Paper Conduits Sponsored By Unrelated Institutions
definition: ( YTD, % ) Conduits Sponsored by Other Unrelated Institutions Maximum Amount Of Credit Exposure Arising From Credit Enhancements Provided To Conduit Structures In The Form Of Standby Letters Of Credit, Subordinate Securities, And Other Enhancements - Assets-Backed Commercial Paper Conduits Sponsored By Other Unrelated Institutions.

name: ACQ_CERT
label: Acquiring FDIC Certificate #
definition: Acquiring Institution's unique number assigned by the FDIC used to identify institutions and for the issuance of insurance certificates.

name: ACQ_CHANGECODE
label: Acquiring Activity Event Code
definition: Acquiring Institution Activity Event Code

name: ACQ_CHARTAGENT
label: Acquiring Chartering Agency
definition: Acquiring Institution Chartering Agency - State and Federal, Comptroller of the Currency - Chartering authority for nationally chartered commercial banks and for federally chartered savings associations (The Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) before 7/21/11) State (includes U.S. Territories) - Chartering authority for institutions that are not chartered by the OCC or OTS

name: ACQ_CHARTER
label: Acquiring OCC Charter Number
definition: Acquiring Institution's unique number assigned by the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) used to identify institutions it has chartered and regulates (i.e. national banks).

name: ACQ_CLASS
label: Acquiring Class Designation
definition: Acquiring Institution's class designation code indicating the major groupings of institutions. N = FDIC Insured National Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; SM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; NM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, or a Mutual Commercial Bank; SI = FDIC Insured Stock and Mutual Savings Bank; MI = Mutually Insured; SB = FDIC Insured Savings Bank; SL = FDIC Insured Savings and Loan Association; OI = Other FDIC Insured Institution (International Banking Act); CU = Credit Union; NC = Non-insured Institution; NS = Non-insured Savings Association

name: ACQ_CLCODE
label: Acquiring Numeric Class Code
definition: Acquiring Institution's numeric code which identifies the major and minor categories of an institution.

name: ACQ_CNTYNAME
label: Acquiring County Name
definition: County where the acquiring institution is physically located (abbreviated if the county name exceeds 16 characters).

name: ACQ_CNTYNUM
label: Acquiring County Number
definition: The federal information processing standards (FIPS) numeric code of the acquiring institution.

name: ACQ_FDICREGION
label: Acquiring Supervisory Region Number
definition: A two-digit number indicating the FDIC Supervisory Division or Region of the acquiring institution. 02 = New York; 05 = Atlanta; 09 = Chicago; 11 = Kansas City; 13 = Dallas; 14 = San Francisco; 16 = Office of Complex Financial Institutions (CFI)

name: ACQ_FDICREGION_DESC
label: Acquiring Supervisory Region Description
definition: The description of the FDIC supervisory region of the acquiring institution.

name: ACQ_INSAGENT1
label: Acquiring Insurance Fund Membership
definition: The insurance fund membership of the acquiring institution; Deposit Insurance Fund (DIF), Bank Insurance Fund (BIF), Savings Association Insurance Fund (SAIF)

name: ACQ_INSAGENT2
label: Acquiring Secondary Insurance Fund
definition: The secondary insurance fund of the acquiring institution. As a result of the establishment of a single Deposit Insurance Fund (DIF) effective April 1, 2006, the secondary insurance fund is no longer applicable. Prior to April 1, 2006 secondary funds included both Bank Insurance Fund (BIF) and Savings Association Insurance Fund (SAIF)

name: ACQ_INSTNAME
label: Acquiring Institution Name
definition: Name of the Acquiring Institution

name: ACQ_MADDR
label: Acquiring Mailing Street Address
definition: Street address of the acquiring institution's or one of its branches' mailing address.

name: ACQ_MADDR2
label: Acquiring Mailing Street Address Line 2
definition: Street address line 2 of the acquiring institution's or one of its branches' mailing address.

name: ACQ_MCITY
label: Acquiring Mailing City
definition: City of the acquiring institution's or one of its branches' mailing address.

name: ACQ_MSTALP
label: Acquiring Mailing State Abbreviation
definition: State abbreviation of the acquiring institution's or one of its branches' mailing address.

name: ACQ_MSTATE
label: Acquiring Mailing State
definition: State of the acquiring institution's or one of its branches' mailing address.

name: ACQ_MZIP5
label: Acquiring Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code representing physical location of the institution or its branch office.

name: ACQ_MZIP5_RAW
label: Acquiring 5-digit Zip Code
definition: The 5-digit postal code of the acquiring institution's mailing address.

name: ACQ_MZIPREST
label: Acquiring Zip Code Extension
definition: The 4-digit zip code extension of the acquiring institution's mailing address.

name: ACQ_ORG_EFF_DTE
label: Acquiring Instituion's Effective Date
definition: Acquiring Institution's Effective Date

name: ACQ_PADDR
label: Acquiring Physical Street Address
definition: Street address at which the acquiring institution or one of its branches is physically located.

name: ACQ_PADDR2
label: Acquiring Physical Street Address Line 2
definition: Street address line 2 at which the acquiring institution or one of its branches is physically located.

name: ACQ_PCITY
label: Acquiring Physical City
definition: City in which an acquiring institution's main office or one of its branches is physically located.

name: ACQ_PSTALP
label: Acquiring Physical State Alpha Code
definition: State in which the acquiring institution's main office or one if its branches are physically located. The FDIC Act defines state as any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, and any territory of the United States, Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, the Virgin Island, and the Northern Mariana Islands.

name: ACQ_PZIP5
label: Acquiring Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code representing physical location of the acquiring institution or one of its branch offices.

name: ACQ_PZIP5_RAW
label: Acquiring Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code representing physical location of the institution or its branch office.

name: ACQ_PZIPREST
label: Acquiring Zip Code Extension
definition: The 4-digit zip code extension of the acquiring institution's physical location.

name: ACQ_REGAGENT
label: Acquiring Chartering Agency
definition: Acquiring Institution Chartering Agency - State and Federal, Comptroller of the Currency - Chartering authority for nationally chartered commercial banks and for federally chartered savings associations (The Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) before 7/21/11) State (includes U.S. Territories) - Chartering authority for institutions that are not chartered by the OCC or OTS

name: ACQ_TRUST
label: Acquiring Trust Power
definition: Trust power status of the acquiring institution.

name: ACQ_UNINUM
label: Acquiring Unique FDIC Number
definition: The acquiring institution's unique FDIC identifier number for holding companies, banks, branches and nondeposit subsidiaries. This value maps to the main office for the acquiring Institution in a merger, acquisition, etc.

name: ACTIVE
label: Institution Status
definition: A number indicating the status of an institution. 1='Institutions that are currently open and insured by the FDIC'; 0='Institution closed or not insured by FDIC'

name: ACT_EVT1_NUM
label: Activity Event Number
definition: A number corresponding to an event that causes changes to entity activities or ownership over time.

name: ACT_EVT2_NUM
label: Activity Event Number
definition: A number corresponding to an event that causes changes to entity activities or ownership over time.

name: ACT_EVT3_NUM
label: Activity Event Number
definition: A number corresponding to an event that causes changes to entity activities or ownership over time.

name: ACT_EVT4_NUM
label: Activity Event Number
definition: A number corresponding to an event that causes changes to entity activities or ownership over time.

name: ACT_EVT5_NUM
label: Activity Event Number
definition: A number corresponding to an event that causes changes to entity activities or ownership over time.

name: ACT_EVT_DESC
label: Activity Event Description
definition: The description of a code that identifies an event that causes changes to entity activities or ownership over time.

name: ACT_EVT_NUM
label: Activity Event Number
definition: A number indicating a change/event to an institution's activities or ownership over time.

name: ADDNONII
label: Additional Non-Interest Income
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Includes the following noninterest income: Investment banking, advisory, brokerage, and underwriting; Venture capital revenue; Net Servicing fees; Net securitization income; Insurance commission fees and income; Net gains (losses) on sales of loans; Net gains (losses) on sales of real estate owned; Net gains (losses) on sales of other assets (excluding securities); and Other noninterest income. RIS definition: YTD - ADDNONII  = NONII - SUM(IFIDUC, ISERCHG, IGLTRAD)

name: ADDNONIIQ
label: Additional Non-Interest Income
definition: ( Qtly, $ ) Includes the following noninterest income: Investment banking, advisory, brokerage, and underwriting; Venture capital revenue; Net Servicing fees; Net securitization income; Insurance commission fees and income; Net gains (losses) on sales of loans; Net gains (losses) on sales of real estate owned; Net gains (losses) on sales of other assets (excluding securities); and Other noninterest income. RIS definition: YTD - ADDNONII  = NONII - SUM(IFIDUC, ISERCHG, IGLTRAD)

name: ADDNONIIQR
label: Additional Non-Interest Income
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Includes the following noninterest income: Investment banking, advisory, brokerage, and underwriting; Venture capital revenue; Net Servicing fees; Net securitization income; Insurance commission fees and income; Net gains (losses) on sales of loans; Net gains (losses) on sales of real estate owned; Net gains (losses) on sales of other assets (excluding securities); and Other noninterest income. RIS definition: YTD - ADDNONII  = NONII - SUM(IFIDUC, ISERCHG, IGLTRAD)

name: ADDNONIIR
label: Additional Non-Interest Income
definition: ( YTD, % ) Includes the following noninterest income: Investment banking, advisory, brokerage, and underwriting; Venture capital revenue; Net Servicing fees; Net securitization income; Insurance commission fees and income; Net gains (losses) on sales of loans; Net gains (losses) on sales of real estate owned; Net gains (losses) on sales of other assets (excluding securities); and Other noninterest income. RIS definition: YTD - ADDNONII  = NONII - SUM(IFIDUC, ISERCHG, IGLTRAD)

name: ADDNONINTEXP
label: Additional Non-Interest Expense
definition: ( YTD, $ ) This includes all other operating expenses of the institution. These may consist of net (gains) or losses on OREO, loans sales, fixed assets sales, amortization of intangible assets, or other itemized expenses. Beginning in June 1996, this does not include losses on asset sales for TFR reporters. Such gains (losses) are included net in noninterest income. RIS definition: YTD - ADDNONINTEXP  = EOTHNINT + EAMINTAN (>2000) or ADDNONINTEXP =EOTHNINT

name: ADDNONINTEXPQ
label: Additional Non-Interest Expense
definition: ( Qtly, $ ) This includes all other operating expenses of the institution. These may consist of net (gains) or losses on OREO, loans sales, fixed assets sales, amortization of intangible assets, or other itemized expenses. Beginning in June 1996, this does not include losses on asset sales for TFR reporters. Such gains (losses) are included net in noninterest income. RIS definition: YTD - ADDNONINTEXP  = EOTHNINT + EAMINTAN (>2000) or ADDNONINTEXP =EOTHNINT

definition: ( Qtly, % ) This includes all other operating expenses of the institution. These may consist of net (gains) or losses on OREO, loans sales, fixed assets sales, amortization of intangible assets, or other itemized expenses. Beginning in June 1996, this does not include losses on asset sales for TFR reporters. Such gains (losses) are included net in noninterest income. RIS definition: YTD - ADDNONINTEXP  = EOTHNINT + EAMINTAN (>2000) or ADDNONINTEXP =EOTHNINT

name: ADDNONINTEXPR
label: Additional Non-Interest Expense
definition: ( YTD, % ) This includes all other operating expenses of the institution. These may consist of net (gains) or losses on OREO, loans sales, fixed assets sales, amortization of intangible assets, or other itemized expenses. Beginning in June 1996, this does not include losses on asset sales for TFR reporters. Such gains (losses) are included net in noninterest income. RIS definition: YTD - ADDNONINTEXP  = EOTHNINT + EAMINTAN (>2000) or ADDNONINTEXP =EOTHNINT

name: ADDRESBR
label: ADDRESBR
definition: Physical location of the branch

name: ADDRESS
label: Street Address
definition: The street address in which an institution or branch office is physically located.

name: ADDRESS2
label: Street Address Line 2
definition: The street address line 2 in which an institution or branch office is physically located.

name: ALLOTHL
label: All other liabilities
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The bank's liability on acceptances executed and outstanding, interest and other expenses accrued and unpaid (including accrued income taxes payable), net deferred tax liabilities and minority interest in consolidated subsidiaries.    Reporting notes: Beginning March 2009, non-controlling (minority) interests in consolidated subsidiaries are reported as part of not of equity capital and not included in this item. As of 1997, limited-life preferred stock and related surplus is reported as part of subordinated debt. As 1994, revaluation losses on assets held in trading accounts are included in trading liabilities.   RIS Definition: ALLOTHL= SUM(OLMIN, ACEPT, LLPFDSTK, 0)

name: ALLOTHLR
label: All other liabilities
definition: ( YTD, % ) The bank's liability on acceptances executed and outstanding, interest and other expenses accrued and unpaid (including accrued income taxes payable), net deferred tax liabilities and minority interest in consolidated subsidiaries.    Reporting notes: Beginning March 2009, non-controlling (minority) interests in consolidated subsidiaries are reported as part of not of equity capital and not included in this item. As of 1997, limited-life preferred stock and related surplus is reported as part of subordinated debt. As 1994, revaluation losses on assets held in trading accounts are included in trading liabilities.   RIS Definition: ALLOTHL= SUM(OLMIN, ACEPT, LLPFDSTK, 0)

name: AOA
label: All Other Assets
definition: ( YTD, $ ) All other assets include investments in unconsolidated subsidiaries, customers' liability on acceptances outstanding, income earned not collected on loans, net deferred tax assets, excess residential mortgage servicing fees receivable and other assets. Reporting notes: Beginning in June 2009, includes direct and indirect investments in real estate ventures for FFIEC CALL Reporters. RIS definition: IDOA = SUM(OA, INVSUB, CUSLI) or >=0906 IDOA = SUM(OA, INVSUB, CUSLI, INVSUORE

name: AOAR
label: All Other Assets
definition: ( YTD, % ) All other assets include investments in unconsolidated subsidiaries, customers' liability on acceptances outstanding, income earned not collected on loans, net deferred tax assets, excess residential mortgage servicing fees receivable and other assets. Reporting notes: Beginning in June 2009, includes direct and indirect investments in real estate ventures for FFIEC CALL Reporters. RIS definition: IDOA = SUM(OA, INVSUB, CUSLI) or >=0906 IDOA = SUM(OA, INVSUB, CUSLI, INVSUORE

name: ASCEAUTO
label: Bank Asset Sales Auto Loans
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Auto Loans - Maximum Amount Of Credit Exposure Arising From Recourse Or Other Seller-Provided Credit Enhancement For Assets That Were Sold And Not Securitized - Auto Loans.

name: ASCECI
label: Bank Asset Sales C&I Loans
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Commercial & Industrial Loans - Maximum Amount Of Credit Exposure Arising From Recourse Or Other Seller-Provided Credit Enhancement For Assets That Were Sold And Not Securitized - Commercial And Industrial Loans.

name: ASCECONS
label: Bank Asset Sales Other Consumer Loans
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Other Consumer Loans - Maximum Amount Of Credit Exposure Arising From Recourse Or Other Seller-Provided Credit Enhancement For Assets That Were Sold And Not Securitized - Other Consumer Loans.

name: ASCECRCD
label: Bank Asset Sales Credit Cards Receivables
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Credit Cards Receivables - Maximum Amount Of Credit Exposure Arising From Recourse Or Other Seller-Provided Credit Enhancement For Assets That Were Sold And Not Securitized - Credit Cards Receivables.

name: ASCEHEL
label: Bank Asset Sales Home Equity Lines
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Home Equity Lines - Maximum Amount Of Credit Exposure Arising From Recourse Or Other Seller-Provided Credit Enhancement For Assets That Were Sold And Not Securitized - Home Equity Lines.

name: ASCEOTH
label: Maximum Amount of Credit Exposure, Bank Asset Sales All Other Loans &  All Leases
definition: ( YTD, $ ) All Other Loans and All Leases - Maximum Amount Of Credit Exposure Arising From Recourse Or Other Seller-Provided Credit Enhancement For Assets That Were Sold And Not Securitized - All Other Loans, Other Assets and Leases. Beginning June 2018, all other loans also includes home equity lines, credit card receivables, auto loans, other consumer loans and commercial and industrial loans

name: ASCEOTHR
label: Maximum Amount of Credit Exposure, Bank Asset Sales All Other Loans &  All Leases
definition: ( YTD, % ) All Other Loans and All Leases - Maximum Amount Of Credit Exposure Arising From Recourse Or Other Seller-Provided Credit Enhancement For Assets That Were Sold And Not Securitized - All Other Loans, Other Assets and Leases. Beginning June 2018, all other loans also includes home equity lines, credit card receivables, auto loans, other consumer loans and commercial and industrial loans

name: ASCERES
label: Maximum Amount of Credit Exposure, Bank Asset Sales 1-4 Family Residential Loans
definition: ( YTD, $ ) 1-4 Family Residential Loans - Maximum Amount Of Credit Exposure Arising From Recourse Or Other Seller-Provided Credit Enhancement For Assets That Were Sold And Not Securitized - 1-4 Family Residential Loans.

name: ASCERESR
label: Maximum Amount of Credit Exposure, Bank Asset Sales 1-4 Family Residential Loans
definition: ( YTD, % ) 1-4 Family Residential Loans - Maximum Amount Of Credit Exposure Arising From Recourse Or Other Seller-Provided Credit Enhancement For Assets That Were Sold And Not Securitized - 1-4 Family Residential Loans.

name: ASDRAUTO
label: Assets Sold With Recourse: Auto Loans Before June 2018
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Auto Loans Assets Sold With Recourse Or Other Seller-Provided Credit Enhancements And Not Securitized - Auto Loans. This line item ends March 2018, institutions now report any of these in All Other Loans and All Leases.

name: ASDRCI
label: Assets Sold With Recourse: C&I Loans Before June 2018
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Commercial & Industrial Loans Assets Sold With Recourse Or Other Seller-Provided Credit Enhancements And Not Securitized - Commercial And Industrial Loans. This line item ends March 2018, institutions now report any of these in All Other Loans and All Leases.

name: ASDRCONS
label: Assets Sold With Recourse: Other Consumer Loans Before June 2018
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Other Consumer Loans Assets Sold With Recourse Or Other Seller-Provided Credit Enhancements And Not Securitized - Other Consumer Loans. This line item ends March 2018, institutions now report any of these in All Other Loans and All Leases.

name: ASDRCRCD
label: Assets Sold With Recourse: Credit Cards Receivables Before June 2018
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Credit Cards Receivables Assets Sold With Recourse Or Other Seller-Provided Credit Enhancements And Not Securitized - Credit Cards Receivables. This line item ends March 2018, institutions now report any of these in All Other Loans and All Leases.

name: ASDRHEL
label: Assets Sold With Recourse: Home Equity Lines Before June 2018
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Home Equity Lines Assets Sold With Recourse Or Other Seller-Provided Credit Enhancements And Not Securitized - Home Equity Lines. This line item ends March 2018, institutions now report any of these in All Other Loans and All Leases.

name: ASDROTH
label: Assets Sold With Recourse All Other Loans and All Leases
definition: ( YTD, $ ) All Other Loans and All Leases Assets Sold With Recourse Or Other Seller-Provided Credit Enhancements And Not Securitized - All Other Loans, Other Assets and Leases. Beginning June 2018, all other loans also includes Home equity lines, credit card receivables, auto loans, other consumer loans and commercial and industrial loans.

name: ASDROTHR
label: Assets Sold With Recourse All Other Loans and All Leases
definition: ( YTD, % ) All Other Loans and All Leases Assets Sold With Recourse Or Other Seller-Provided Credit Enhancements And Not Securitized - All Other Loans, Other Assets and Leases. Beginning June 2018, all other loans also includes Home equity lines, credit card receivables, auto loans, other consumer loans and commercial and industrial loans.

name: ASDRRES
label: Assets Sold With Recourse 1-4 Family Residential Loans
definition: ( YTD, $ ) 1-4 Family Residential Loans Assets Sold With Recourse Or Other Seller-Provided Credit Enhancements And Not Securitized - 1-4 Family Residential Loans.

name: ASDRRESR
label: Assets Sold With Recourse 1-4 Family Residential Loans
definition: ( YTD, % ) 1-4 Family Residential Loans Assets Sold With Recourse Or Other Seller-Provided Credit Enhancements And Not Securitized - 1-4 Family Residential Loans.

name: ASSET
label: ASSET
definition: Total assets of the institution as of June 30.   This amount is repeated for every location of the institution.  Be sure to use the assets only once for each institution, otherwise duplication of the assets when summarizing by institution.  Please note that assets are not available by location.  They are only reported at the institution level on the quarterly Call Reports and are assigned to the headquarter's location.

name: ASSET2
label: Average Assets, Quarterly
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The two period average of the total assets shown on the balance sheet. The calculation uses this quarter's and last quarter's values. An example calculation for September would be (June assets + September assets) / 2. The denominator for quarterly income as a percent of average assets.

name: ASSET5
label: Average Total Assets
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Year-to-date average of the total assets represented on the balance sheet. Used as the denominator for year-to-date income as a percent of average assets. The number of quarterly values used in the calculation depends on the date of the data. year-to-date averages: March reporting period - (December assets + March assets) / 2 June reporting period - (December assets + March assets + June assets) / 3 September reporting period - (December assets + March assets + June assets + September assets) / 4 December reporting period - (Previous December assets + March assets + June assets + September assets + December assets) / 5 Quarterly averages: All reporting periods – (Previous quarter assets + current quarter assets) / 2

name: ASSETFOR
label: Total Assets in Foreign Offices
definition: ( Qtly, % ) The sum of all assets held in foreign offices owned by the institution including cash, loans, securities, bank premises and other assets. This total does not include off-balance-sheet accounts. Available for Call Reporters only.

name: ASSETR
label: Total Assets
definition: ( YTD, % ) The sum of all assets owned by the institution including cash, loans, securities, bank premises and other assets. This total does not include off-balance-sheet accounts.

name: ASSTLT
label: Long-Term Assets (5+ Years)
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Loans and debt securities with remaining maturities or repricing intervals of over five years. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: ASSTLTR
label: Long-Term Assets (5+ Years)
definition: ( YTD, % ) Loans and debt securities with remaining maturities or repricing intervals of over five years. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: ASTEMPM
label: Assets Per Employee ($Millions)
definition: ( YTD and Qtly, % ) Total assets in millions of dollars as a percent of the number of full-time equivalent employees.

name: AVASSETJ
label: Adjusted Average Assets for Leverage Capital Purposes
definition: ( YTD, $ ) As of March 2015, total average assets for leverage capital purposes according to the amended Market Risk Capital Rule. (see: FDIC Financial Institutions Letter FIL-24-1012 ) Advanced approaches institutions begin reporting a new calculation for this item beginning March 31, 2014 on schedule RC-R Part I.B.  All institutions began the new schedule RC-R Part I and Part II as of March 31, 2015. Prior to the new RC-R requirements, average assets-adjusted based on the risk-based capital definitions for prompt corrective action (PCA) and includes:  Call Reporters: Average assets (from schedule RC-K) - disallowed intangible assets - unrealized loss on equity securities - unrealized holding gains or losses on available-for-sale securities according to FASB 115 - adjustments for financial subsidiaries (Beginning in March 2001) _x000D_Thrift Financial Reporters (before March 2012):  Adjusted tangible assets (from schedule CCR) - unrealized holding gains or losses on available-for-sale securities adjusted according to FASB 115

name: AVASSETJR
label: Adjusted Average Assets for Leverage Capital Purposes
definition: ( YTD, % ) As of March 2015, total average assets for leverage capital purposes according to the amended Market Risk Capital Rule. (see: FDIC Financial Institutions Letter FIL-24-1012 ) Advanced approaches institutions begin reporting a new calculation for this item beginning March 31, 2014 on schedule RC-R Part I.B.  All institutions began the new schedule RC-R Part I and Part II as of March 31, 2015. Prior to the new RC-R requirements, average assets-adjusted based on the risk-based capital definitions for prompt corrective action (PCA) and includes:  Call Reporters: Average assets (from schedule RC-K) - disallowed intangible assets - unrealized loss on equity securities - unrealized holding gains or losses on available-for-sale securities according to FASB 115 - adjustments for financial subsidiaries (Beginning in March 2001) _x000D_Thrift Financial Reporters (before March 2012):  Adjusted tangible assets (from schedule CCR) - unrealized holding gains or losses on available-for-sale securities adjusted according to FASB 115

name: AVMMLF
label: Quarterly average amount of assets purchased under the MMLF and excluded from “Total assets for the leverage ratio.”
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The quarterly average amount of assets purchased under the Money Market Mutual Fund Liquidity Facility(MMLF) that are included as a deduction for leverage ratio purposes and thus are excluded from total assets when calculating the leverage ratio. (Reported on Schedule RC-R Part I, item 29, “LESS: Other deductions from (additions to) and excluded from “Total assets for the leverage ratio” reported in Schedule RC-R, Part I, item 30.) to prevent the disruption in the money markets from destabilizing the financial system, the board of governors of the federal reserve system authorized the federal reserve bank of Boston on March 19, 2020, to establish the MMLF pursuant to section 13(3) of the federal reserve act (12 u.s.c. 343(3)). under the MMLF, the federal reserve bank of Boston will extend nonrecourse loans to eligible borrowers to purchase eligible assets from money market mutual funds, which will be posted as collateral to the Federal Reserve Bank of Boston. Data begins on June 30, 2020.

name: AVMMLFR
label: Quarterly average amount of assets purchased under the MMLF and excluded from “Total assets for the leverage ratio.”
definition: ( YTD, % ) The quarterly average amount of assets purchased under the Money Market Mutual Fund Liquidity Facility(MMLF) that are included as a deduction for leverage ratio purposes and thus are excluded from total assets when calculating the leverage ratio. (Reported on Schedule RC-R Part I, item 29, “LESS: Other deductions from (additions to) and excluded from “Total assets for the leverage ratio” reported in Schedule RC-R, Part I, item 30.) to prevent the disruption in the money markets from destabilizing the financial system, the board of governors of the federal reserve system authorized the federal reserve bank of Boston on March 19, 2020, to establish the MMLF pursuant to section 13(3) of the federal reserve act (12 u.s.c. 343(3)). under the MMLF, the federal reserve bank of Boston will extend nonrecourse loans to eligible borrowers to purchase eligible assets from money market mutual funds, which will be posted as collateral to the Federal Reserve Bank of Boston. Data begins on June 30, 2020.

name: AVPPPPLG
label: Quarterly average amount of PPP loans pledged to the PPPLF and excluded from “Total assets for the leverage ratio.”

name: BRO
label: Total brokered deposits
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total brokered deposits held in domestic offices. Brokered deposits represent funds which the reporting bank obtains, directly or indirectly, by or through any deposit broker for deposit into one or more deposit accounts. Thus, brokered deposits include both those in which the entire beneficial interest in a given bank deposit account or instrument is held by a single depositor and those in which the deposit broker sells participations in a given bank deposit account or instrument to one or more investors. Fully Insured brokered deposits are brokered deposits that are issued in denominations of $100,000

name: BROINS
label: Fully Insured Brokered Deposits
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Brokered deposits held in domestic offices issued in denominations of less than $100,000, or in denominations of $100,000 or more and participated out by the broker in shares of less than $100,000note: although standard FDIC insurance coverage was temporarily raised from $100,000 to $250,000 in October 2008, institutions are required to report this item based on the $100,000 coverage limit through December 2009.

name: BROINSR
label: Fully Insured Brokered Deposits
definition: ( YTD, % ) Brokered deposits held in domestic offices issued in denominations of less than $100,000, or in denominations of $100,000 or more and participated out by the broker in shares of less than $100,000note: although standard FDIC insurance coverage was temporarily raised from $100,000 to $250,000 in October 2008, institutions are required to report this item based on the $100,000 coverage limit through December 2009.

name: BROR
label: Total brokered deposits
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total brokered deposits held in domestic offices. Brokered deposits represent funds which the reporting bank obtains, directly or indirectly, by or through any deposit broker for deposit into one or more deposit accounts. Thus, brokered deposits include both those in which the entire beneficial interest in a given bank deposit account or instrument is held by a single depositor and those in which the deposit broker sells participations in a given bank deposit account or instrument to one or more investors. Banks with domestic offices only and total assets less than $1 billion. (3/2017 - 6/2019) For further information on FFIEC Call Reports , click here. For further information on Thrift Financial Reports, click here.

name: CALLRPT
label: Call Reporters
definition: FFIEC Call Reports are filed by all Insured commercial banks (national banks, state member banks, state nonmember banks) and by state-chartered savings banks that are supervised by the FDIC. Call Reports are quarterly reports of condition and income based on information as of the close of business on the last calendar day of each calendar quarter (March 31, June 30, September 30, and December 31). Specific reporting requirements for each institution depend upon its asset size and whether it has any foreign offices. for further information on call reports, click here. all Insured institutions are required to file a call report as of the March 2012 reporting cycle when thrift financial reports were phased out as a result of Dodd frank legislation. Insured savings institutions that were supervised by the office of thrift supervision reported similar information in quarterly thrift financial reports (TFRs). for further information on Thrift Financial Reports, click here.

name: CB
label: Community Bank
definition: FDIC Community banks are identified based on criteria defined in the FDIC community banking study. using detailed balance sheet and geographic data, the study defines community banks primarily in terms of their traditional relationship banking and limited geographic scope of operations. Summary of FDIC Research Definition of Community Banking Organizations Community banks are designated at the level of the banking. (All charters under designated holding companies are considered community banking charters.) Exclude: Any organization with: - No loans or no core deposits - Foreign Assets ≥10% of total assets - More than 50% of assets in certain specialty banks, including: credit card specialists consumer nonbank banks1 industrial loan companies trust companies bankers' banks Include: All remaining banking organizations with: - Total assets 2 - Total assets ≥indexed size threshold, where: Loan to assets > 33% Core deposits to assets > 50% More than 1 office but no more than the indexed maximum number of offices.3 Number of large MSAs with offices ≤2 Number of states with offices ≤3 No single office with deposits > indexed maximum branch deposit size.4 1 Consumer nonbank banks are financial institutions with limited charters that can make commercial loans or take deposits, but not both. 2 Asset size threshold indexed to equal $250 million in 1985 and $1 billion in 2010. 3 Maximum number of offices indexed to equal 40 in 1985 and 75 in 2010. 4 Maximum branch deposit size indexed to equal $1.25 billion in 1985 and $5 billion in 2010.

name: CBLRIND
label: Community Bank Leverage Ratio Election (1 is Yes)
definition: ( YTD and Qtly, % ) Beginning in March 2020, 1 means YES, the institution has elected the community bank Leverage ratio (CBLR) framework for the quarter. the community bank leverage ratio (cblr) framework is an optional framework designed to reduce burden by removing the requirements for calculating and reporting risk-based capital ratios for qualifying community banking organizations that opt into the framework. The framework provides a simple measure of capital adequacy for qualifying community banking organizations, consistent with section 201 of the Economic Growth, Regulatory Relief and Consumer Protection Act. The calculation of the Leverage Ratio is the percent of Tier 1 capital divided average total consolidated assets. Qualifying community banking organizations that elect to use the CBLR framework and that maintain a leverage ratio of greater than 9 percent are considered to have satisfied the risk-based and leverage capital requirements in the generally applicable capital rule. In addition, these institutions are considered to have met the well-capitalized ratio requirements for section 38 of the Federal Deposit Insurance Act. If needed, there is a two-quarter grace period to meet the qualifying criteria again if an institution maintains a leverage ratio of 8 percent or greater. Temporarily under the Coronavirus Aid, Relief, and Economic Security Act , as of June 2020 a banking organization with a leverage ratio of 8 percent or greater (and that meets the other existing qualifying criteria) may elect to use the community bank leverage ratio framework. If needed, there is a two-quarter grace period to meet the qualifying criteria again if an institution maintains a leverage ratio of 7 percent or greater. There are transition rules in place through the beginning of 2022 to return to the Leverage Ratio requirement to greater than 9 percent.

name: CBSA
label: Core Based Statistical Area Name
definition: The name associated with the numeric code that the U.S. Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget assigns for the CBSA. The 2000 standards provide that each CBSA must contain at least one urban area of 10,000 or more population. Metropolitan and Micropolitan statistical areas are two categories of core based statistical areas. If an institution is not defined as a CBSA, the value of the field will be zero. For more information see: http: //www.census.gov/population/www/estimates/metroarea.html

name: CBSA_DIV
label: Metropolitan Divisions Name
definition: The name associated with the numeric code given by the US Census Bureau office of Management and Budget (2000 standards) that represents the core based statistical division (CBSADIV). A metropolitan division is a county or group of counties within a core based statistical area that contains a core with a population of at least 2.5 million. A CBSA metropolitan division consists of one or more main/secondary counties that represent an employment center or centers, plus adjacent counties associated with the main county or counties through commuting ties. If an institution is not defined as a CBSA division the value of the field will be zero.

name: CBSA_DIV_FLG
label: Metropolitan Divisions Flag
definition: A flag (1=Yes) indicating member of a Core Based Statistical Division as defined by the US Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget.

name: CBSA_DIV_NAMB
label: CBSA_DIV_NAMB
definition: The name that corresponds to a code for a county or group of counties within a Core Based Statistical Area that contains a core with a population of at least 2.5 million. A Metropolitan Division consists of one or more main/secondary counties that represent an employment center or centers, plus adjacent counties associated with the main county or counties through commuting ties.

name: CBSA_DIV_NO
label: Metropolitan Divisions Number
definition: The numeric code given by the US Census Bureau office of Management and Budget that represents the core based statistical division (CBSADIV)under the year 2000 standards. A metropolitan division is a county or group of counties within a core based statistical area that contains a core with a population of at least 2.5 million. A CBSA metropolitan division consists of one or more main/secondary counties that represent an employment center or centers, plus adjacent counties associated with the main county or counties through commuting ties. If an institution is not defined as a CBSA division the value of the field will be zero.

name: CBSA_METRO
label: Metropolitan Division Number
definition: The numeric code for the new Metropolitan Statistical Areas (MSAs) are based on the 2000 Census. These areas correspond to the state / county / CBSA relationships as defined by the Census Bureau. The new MSAs reflect only the CBSA names and codes for metropolitan areas (CBSA_METRO). Micropolitan Areas and Metropolitan Divisions are excluded for publication purposes.

name: CBSA_METRO_FLG
label: Metropolitan Division Flag
definition:
A flag used to indicate whether an institution is in a metropolitan statistical area. The US Census bureau office of Management and Budget defines the metropolitan statistical area. A core based statistical area associated with at least one urbanized area that has a population of at least 50,000. The metropolitan statistical area comprises the central county or counties containing the core, plus adjacent outlying counties having a high degree of social and economic integration with the central county as measured through commuting. _x000D_0 = institution is not in a metropolitan statistical area or; 
1 = institution is in a metropolitan statistical area;_x000D_For more information see: http: //www.census.gov/population/www/estimates/metroarea.html

name: CBSA_METRO_NAME
label: Metropolitan Division Name
definition: The new Metropolitan Statistical Areas (MSAs) are based on the 2000 Census. These areas correspond to the state / county / CBSA relationships as defined by the Census Bureau. The new MSAs reflect only the CBSA names and codes for metropolitan areas (CBSA_METRO_NAME). Micropolitan Areas and Metropolitan Divisions are excluded for publication purposes.

name: CBSA_MICRO_FLG
label: Micropolitan Division Flag
definition: A flag (0=No or 1=Yes) used to indicate whether an branch is in a Metropolitan Statistical Area as defined by the US Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget

name: CBSA_NO
label: Core Based Statistical Areas
definition: Numeric code of the Core Based Statistical Area (CBSA) as defined by the US Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget.

name: CERT
label: FDIC Certificate #
definition: A unique number assigned by the FDIC used to identify institutions and for the issuance of insurance certificates.

name: CERTCONS
label: Directly owned by another bank (CERT)
definition: FDIC certificate number of the parent bank or savings institution with which the reported institution s financial data has been consolidated. Beginning in March 1997, both the Thrift Financial Reports and Call Reports are completed on a fully consolidated basis. Previously, the consolidation of subsidiary depository institutions was prohibited. Now, parent institutions are required to file consolidated reports, while their subsidiary financial institutions are still required to file separate reports. Click on the certificate number to identify the parent bank or thrift.

name: CFPBENDDTE

name: CFPBFLAG
label: CFPB Flag
definition: A flag that indicates secondary supervision by CFPB ('0' - not supervised by CFPB, '1'- secondarily supervised by CFPB) If applicable, also currently supervised by The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB). the cfpb has supervisory authority regarding specific federal consumer protection laws for banks, thrifts, and credit unions with assets over $10 billion, as well as their affiliates. The CFPB also has supervisory authority over nonbank mortgage originators and servicers, payday lenders, and private student lenders of all sizes. The Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) provides the current list of banks subject to their specific supervisory authority to the FDIC each quarter. We complete additional validations on FDIC regulated institutions. The current information is then updated, stored on, and retrieved from the FDIC's Structure Information Management System. As a result, there may be differences in the lists related to timing. For further information on the current and historical lists of CFPB supervised institutions, click here.

name: CHUS
label: Balances due from depository institutions in US
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Cash balances due from depository institutions in U.S. include all interest-bearing and noninterest-bearing balances whether in the form of demand, savings or time balances, including certificates of deposit but excluding certificates of deposit held for trading. Beginning in 2001, this item is not reported by FFIEC Call filers with total assets of less than $300 million.

name: CHUSFBK
label: U.S. Branches of Foreign Banks Balances Due
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Hba

name: CHUSR
label: Balances due from depository institutions in US
definition: ( YTD, % ) Cash balances due from depository institutions in U.S. include all interest-bearing and noninterest-bearing balances whether in the form of demand, savings or time balances, including certificates of deposit but excluding certificates of deposit held for trading. Beginning in 2001, this item is not reported by FFIEC Call filers with total assets of less than $300 million.

name: CITY
label: City
definition: City in which an institution's headquarters or one of its branches is physically located. Either the entire name or part of the name of a specific city may be entered to produce an Institution List.

name: CITY2BR
label: CITY2BR
definition: Central city based on the reported ZIP code of the branch

name: CITYBR
label: CITYBR
definition: Reported city in which the branch is located

name: CITYHCR
label: City of High Holder
definition: City in which the headquarters of the institution's regulatory high holder are physically located.

name: CLASS
label: Bank Charter Class
definition: A code indicating the major groupings of institutions. N = FDIC Insured National Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; SM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; NM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, or a Mutual Commercial Bank; SI = FDIC Insured Stock and Mutual Savings Bank; MI = Mutually Insured; SB = FDIC Insured Savings Bank; SL = FDIC Insured Savings and Loan Association; OI = Other FDIC Insured Institution (International Banking Act); CU = Credit Union; NC = Non-insured Institution; NS = Non-insured Savings Association

name: CLCODE
label: Class Numeric Code

name: CMSA
label: Consolidated Metropolitan Statistical Area
definition: ( Qtly, % ) The Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) Consolidated Metropolitan Statistical Area (CMSA) code is a number representing the institution location. CMSA consists of two or more contiguous Metropolitan Statistical Areas (MSA) with a combined population of over 1 Million. Note: If an institution is not located in a CMSA, the value of the field will be zeroes.

name: CNTRYNA
label: CNTRYNA
definition: The country name where the headquarters of the institution is chartered.

name: CNTRYNAB
label: CNTRYNAB
definition: Country name in which the branch is located.

name: CNTYNAMB
label: CNTYNAMB
definition: County name in which the branch is located.

name: CNTYNAME
label: County
definition: The county name in which an institution or branch office is physically located.

name: CNTYNUM
label: County Number
definition: The Federal Information Processing System (FIPS) number corresponding to the county in which a physical address is located.

name: CNTYNUMB
label: CNTYNUMB
definition: County number that corresponds to the county in which the branch is located.

name: CONSERVE
label: Conservatorship
definition: A flag (Y=yes;N=no) that indicates if an institution is being operated in government conservatorship.

name: CONSOLD
label: CONSOLD
definition: When BRCENM = C, consolidated, this is the office number (brnum) in which the deposits are reported.  Both branches should be within the same state and county.

name: COREDEP

name: COREDEPR

name: COUNTY
label: County
definition: County where the institution is physically located (abbreviated if the county name exceeds 16 characters).
