definition: ( Qtly, $ ) Total recoveries of loans secured by 1-4 family residential properties (which are held in domestic offices) that have been credited to the allowance for loan and lease losses established by the institution.

name: CRRERESQR
label: Secured By 1-4 Family Residential Properties
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total recoveries of loans secured by 1-4 family residential properties (which are held in domestic offices) that have been credited to the allowance for loan and lease losses established by the institution.

name: CRRERESR
label: Secured By 1-4 Family Residential Properties
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total recoveries of loans secured by 1-4 family residential properties (which are held in domestic offices) that have been credited to the allowance for loan and lease losses established by the institution.

name: CRRERS2Q
label: Secured By Junior Liens
definition: ( Qtly, $ ) Total recoveries of closed-end loans secured by junior liens 1-4 family residential properties (which are held in domestic offices) that have been credited to the allowance for loan and lease losses established by the institution. Prior to 2004, this item is not available for TFR Reporters .

name: CRRERS2QR
label: Secured By Junior Liens
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total recoveries of closed-end loans secured by junior liens 1-4 family residential properties (which are held in domestic offices) that have been credited to the allowance for loan and lease losses established by the institution. Prior to 2004, this item is not available for TFR Reporters .

name: CRRERSF2
label: Secured By Junior Liens
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total recoveries of closed-end loans secured by junior liens 1-4 family residential properties (which are held in domestic offices) that have been credited to the allowance for loan and lease losses established by the institution. Prior to 2004, this item is not available for TFR Reporters .

name: CRRERSF2R
label: Secured By Junior Liens
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total recoveries of closed-end loans secured by junior liens 1-4 family residential properties (which are held in domestic offices) that have been credited to the allowance for loan and lease losses established by the institution. Prior to 2004, this item is not available for TFR Reporters .

name: CRRERSFM
label: Secured By First Liens
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total recoveries of closed-end loans secured by first liens 1-4 family residential properties (which are held in domestic offices) that have been credited to the allowance for loan and lease losses established by the institution. Prior to 2004, this item is not available for TFR Reporters .

name: CRRERSFMR
label: Secured By First Liens
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total recoveries of closed-end loans secured by first liens 1-4 family residential properties (which are held in domestic offices) that have been credited to the allowance for loan and lease losses established by the institution. Prior to 2004, this item is not available for TFR Reporters .

name: CRRERSFQ
label: Secured By First Liens
definition: ( Qtly, $ ) Total recoveries of closed-end loans secured by first liens 1-4 family residential properties (which are held in domestic offices) that have been credited to the allowance for loan and lease losses established by the institution. Prior to 2004, this item is not available for TFR Reporters .

name: CRRERSFQR
label: Secured By First Liens
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total recoveries of closed-end loans secured by first liens 1-4 family residential properties (which are held in domestic offices) that have been credited to the allowance for loan and lease losses established by the institution. Prior to 2004, this item is not available for TFR Reporters .

name: CSA
label: Combined Statistical Area Name (Branch)
definition: Name of the Combined Statistical Area (CSA) as defined by the US Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget

name: CSABR
label: CSABR
definition: The identifying code of a geographic entity consisting of two or more adjacent Core Based Statistical Areas (CBSAs) with employment interchange measures of at least 15. Pairs of CBSAs with employment interchange measures of at least 25 combine automatically. Pairs of CBSAs with employment interchange measures of at least 15, but less than 25, may combine if local opinion in both areas favors combination.

name: CSANAMBR
label: CSANAMBR
definition: The name of a geographic entity consisting of two or more adjacent Core Based Statistical Areas (CBSAs) with employment interchange measures of at least 15. Pairs of CBSAs with employment interchange measures of at least 25 combine automatically. Pairs of CBSAs with employment interchange measures of at least 15, but less than 25, may combine if local opinion in both areas favors combination.

name: CSA_FLG
label: Combined Statistical Area Flag  (Branch)
definition: Flag (1=Yes) indicating member of a Combined Statistical Area (CSA) as defined by the US Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget

name: CSA_NO
label: Combined Statistical Area Number (Branch)
definition: Numeric code of the Combined Statistical Area (CSA) as defined by the US Census Bureau Office of Management and Budget

name: CSTRPT
label: Retrieve Custom Reports
definition: ( Qtly, % ) To retrieve Custom Reports enter your e-mail address in the box provided and select Lookup. Reports saved on the SDI system and referenced by that address will be listed below by name. Use the radio button to select a report to be retrieved or deleted. Modify an existing report by retrieving and then selecting View Custom Report on the create or modify page. Use the Create button to make a new report. For more help use to create a custom report .

name: CTDERBEN
label: Credit Derivatives: Bank Is Beneficiary
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Notional amount of credit derivatives on which the reporting institution has obtained a guarantee against credit losses from other parties. Credit derivatives are off-balance sheet arrangements that allow one party (the beneficiary) to transfer the credit risk of an asset to another party (the guarantor).

name: CTDERBENR
label: Credit Derivatives: Bank Is Beneficiary
definition: ( YTD, % ) Notional amount of credit derivatives on which the reporting institution has obtained a guarantee against credit losses from other parties. Credit derivatives are off-balance sheet arrangements that allow one party (the beneficiary) to transfer the credit risk of an asset to another party (the guarantor).

name: CTDERGTY
label: Credit Derivatives: Bank Is Guarantor
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Notional amount of credit derivatives on which the institution has extended credit protection to another party. Credit derivatives are off-balance sheet arrangements that allow one party (the beneficiary) to transfer the credit risk of an asset to another party (the guarantor).

name: CTDERGTYR
label: Credit Derivatives: Bank Is Guarantor
definition: ( YTD, % ) Notional amount of credit derivatives on which the institution has extended credit protection to another party. Credit derivatives are off-balance sheet arrangements that allow one party (the beneficiary) to transfer the credit risk of an asset to another party (the guarantor).

name: CURRENTLY
label: Currently – institution Status
definition: Based on the most current demographic information, the selections are: active – the institution is currently conducting business as an FDIC Insured institution under the legal name displayed. An empty field indicates a change in status. Select the link on the name for more information. Changes in status include, but are not limited to: - institutions that have changed their legal name - institutions that have changed the location of their headquarters - institutions that have merged with other institutions - institutions that have been acquired by other institutions - institutions that are no longer in business - institutions that are no longer Insured by the FDIC.

name: DATEUPDT
label: Last update
definition: The date of the last data update.

name: DDT
label: Demand Deposits
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total demand deposits included in transaction accounts held in domestic offices. prior to 2004, This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: DDTR
label: Demand Deposits
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total demand deposits included in transaction accounts held in domestic offices. prior to 2004, This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: DEMOGRAPHICAODS
label: Demographic information
definition: Reflects both locational detail (name, city, state, etc.) and operating status (active, inactive, bank class, etc.) for each institution and is provided at each quarter's end. To incorporate recent status changes, demographic data are also updated weekly and referred to as most current in ID.

name: DENOVO
label: Denovo Institution
definition: A flag used to indicate whether an institution is a new institution (not a recharter). This flag is set quarterly. For instance, if REPDTE is 3/31/98 and DENOVO equals 1, the institution was a denovo during the first quarter of 1998.

name: DEP
label: Total deposits
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The sum of all deposits including demand deposits, money market deposits, other savings deposits, time deposits and deposits in foreign offices.

name: DEPACCEPT_WEBSITES_M_OVER
label: Deposit Accepting Websites
definition: ( Qtly, % ) In addition to the primary corporate website, Uniform Resource Locators (URLs) of all other public-facing internet websites that the bank uses to accept or request deposits from the public, if any

name: DEPCERTF
label: Certified and Official Checks Foreign
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Certified and official checks held in foreign offices.

name: DEPCSBQ
label: Estimated Assessable Deposits
definition: ( YTD, $ ) An average quarterly deposit assessment base used for computation of the deposit insurance assessment of the institution. It is an estimated calculation, which will not match every institution’s official FDIC assessment statement. Some institutions have additional adjustments based on specific deposit categories. For more information on the insurance premium assessment process (including quarterly Financing Corporation (FICO) rates, assessment rates and general questions regarding invoice computations), please contact the FDIC via our Toll-free number: 800-759-6596 or our Email address: assessments@FDIC.gov

name: DEPCSBQR
label: Estimated Assessable Deposits
definition: ( YTD, % ) An average quarterly deposit assessment base used for computation of the deposit insurance assessment of the institution. It is an estimated calculation, which will not match every institution’s official FDIC assessment statement. Some institutions have additional adjustments based on specific deposit categories. For more information on the insurance premium assessment process (including quarterly Financing Corporation (FICO) rates, assessment rates and general questions regarding invoice computations), please contact the FDIC via our Toll-free number: 800-759-6596 or our Email address: assessments@FDIC.gov

name: DEPDASQR
label: Domestic Deposits to Total Assets
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total domestic office deposits as a percent of total assets.

name: DEPDASTR
label: Domestic Deposits to Total Assets
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total domestic office deposits as a percent of total assets.

name: DEPDOM
label: Deposits held in domestic offices
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The sum of all domestic office deposits (as of June 30), including demand deposits, money market deposits, other savings deposits and time deposits.

name: DEPDOMR
label: Deposits held in domestic offices
definition: ( YTD, % ) The sum of all domestic office deposits, including demand deposits, money market deposits, other savings deposits and time deposits.

name: DEPFBKF
label: Banks in foreign countries
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Deposits of foreign banks (including U.S. branches and agencies of foreign banks) held in foreign offices.

name: DEPFBKFR
label: Banks in foreign countries
definition: ( YTD, % ) Deposits of foreign banks (including U.S. branches and agencies of foreign banks) held in foreign offices.

name: DEPFGOVF
label: Foreign governments and official institutions
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Deposits of foreign governments and official institutions (including foreign central banks) held in foreign offices.

name: DEPFGOVFR
label: Foreign governments and official institutions
definition: ( YTD, % ) Deposits of foreign governments and official institutions (including foreign central banks) held in foreign offices.

name: DEPFOR
label: Deposits held in foreign offices
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The sum of all foreign office deposits, including demand deposits, money market deposits, other savings deposits and time deposits. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: DEPFORR
label: Deposits held in foreign offices
definition: ( YTD, % ) The sum of all foreign office deposits, including demand deposits, money market deposits, other savings deposits and time deposits. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: DEPI
label: Total Interest-Bearing Deposits
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Interest-bearing deposits (includes interest-bearing deposits in foreign offices).Represents any deposit in domestic and foreign offices on which the banks pays or accrues interest.

name: DEPIDOM
label: Interest-Bearing Deposits in Domestic Offices
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The sum of interest-bearing time and savings deposits held in domestic offices. This item is not available for Thrift Financial Report filers having both assets less than $300 million and risk-based capital ratios if 12 percent or more.

name: DEPIDOMR
label: Interest-Bearing Deposits in Domestic Offices
definition: ( YTD, % ) The sum of interest-bearing time and savings deposits held in domestic offices. This item is not available for Thrift Financial Report filers having both assets less than $300 million and risk-based capital ratios if 12 percent or more.

name: DEPIFOR
label: Interest-Bearing Deposits in Foreign Offices
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The sum of interest-bearing time and savings deposits held in foreign offices. represents any deposit in foreign offices, whether demand, savings or time, on which the bank pays or accrues interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters, The last TFR was filed in December 2011.

name: DEPIFORR
label: Interest-Bearing Deposits in Foreign Offices
definition: ( YTD, % ) The sum of interest-bearing time and savings deposits held in foreign offices. represents any deposit in foreign offices, whether demand, savings or time, on which the bank pays or accrues interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters, The last TFR was filed in December 2011.

name: DEPINS
label: Estimated Insured Deposits
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The estimated amount of FDIC Insured deposits in domestic offices and in Insured branches of Puerto Rico and US territories and possessions. notes: (1) as of July 21, 2010, the standard maximum deposit insurance amount was permanently raised to $250,000. (2) Beginning December 31, 2010, through December 31, 2012, includes the unlimited insurance coverage of noninterest-bearing transaction accounts. all noninterest-bearing transaction accounts are fully insured, regardless of the balance of the account, at all FDIC-Insured institutions. (3) Beginning September 2009, amounts for non-retirement accounts are reported based on the temporary increase to the standard insurance limit to $250,000. (4) Prior to September 2009, amounts for non-retirement accounts were reported based on the standard insurance limit of $100,000. (5) Beginning in 2006, retirement accounts are Insured to $250,000 and reporting was phased in at different times during the year by banks, thrifts and IBAs. (6) Prior to March 2002, reported for domestic offices only. (7) This calculation uses the self-reported estimate of uninsured deposits filed by institutions that are greater than $1 billion.

name: DEPINSR
label: Estimated Insured Deposits
definition: ( YTD, % ) The estimated amount of FDIC Insured deposits in domestic offices and in Insured branches of Puerto Rico and US territories and possessions. notes: (1) as of July 21, 2010, the standard maximum deposit insurance amount was permanently raised to $250,000. (2) Beginning December 31, 2010, through December 31, 2012, includes the unlimited insurance coverage of noninterest-bearing transaction accounts. all noninterest-bearing transaction accounts are fully insured, regardless of the balance of the account, at all FDIC-Insured institutions. (3) Beginning September 2009, amounts for non-retirement accounts are reported based on the temporary increase to the standard insurance limit to $250,000. (4) Prior to September 2009, amounts for non-retirement accounts were reported based on the standard insurance limit of $100,000. (5) Beginning in 2006, retirement accounts are Insured to $250,000 and reporting was phased in at different times during the year by banks, thrifts and IBAs. (6) Prior to March 2002, reported for domestic offices only. (7) This calculation uses the self-reported estimate of uninsured deposits filed by institutions that are greater than $1 billion.

name: DEPIPCCF
label: Deposits: Individuals, Partnerships and Corporations
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total transaction and nontransaction accounts of Individuals, partnerships and corporations (including all certified and official checks) held in foreign offices. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011. Beginning in March 2017 only reported by banks with Assets of $10 billion or more.

name: DEPIPCCFR
label: Deposits: Individuals, Partnerships and Corporations
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total transaction and nontransaction accounts of Individuals, partnerships and corporations (including all certified and official checks) held in foreign offices. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011. Beginning in March 2017 only reported by banks with Assets of $10 billion or more.

name: DEPIPCF
label: Deposits: Individuals, Partnerships, and Corporations Foreign
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Deposits of Individuals, partnerships, and corporations held in foreign offices.

name: DEPLGAMT
label: Deposits: Amount ($) Deposit Accounts of More Than $100,000
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Amount of deposit accounts of more than $100,000 held in domestic offices and in Insured branches in Puerto Rico and U.S. territories and possessions note: 1.) Beginning September 2009, institutions are required to report most deposits on the $250,000 threshold levels. 2.)beginning June 2006, excludes retirement accounts for Call Reporters. 3.) Beginning September 2006, excludes retirement accounts for Insured U.S. Branches of a foreign chartered institutions (IBAs). 4.) Beginning December 2006, excludes retirement accounts for TFR Reporters.

name: DEPLGAMTR
label: Deposits: Amount ($) Deposit Accounts of More Than $100,000
definition: ( YTD, % ) Amount of deposit accounts of more than $100,000 held in domestic offices and in Insured branches in Puerto Rico and U.S. territories and possessions note: 1.) Beginning September 2009, institutions are required to report most deposits on the $250,000 threshold levels. 2.)beginning June 2006, excludes retirement accounts for Call Reporters. 3.) Beginning September 2006, excludes retirement accounts for Insured U.S. Branches of a foreign chartered institutions (IBAs). 4.) Beginning December 2006, excludes retirement accounts for TFR Reporters.

name: DEPLGB
label: Number (#) of Deposit Accounts More Than $100,000
definition: ( YTD, $ and % ) Number of individual deposit accounts of more than $100,000 held in domestic offices and Insured U.S. branches of a foreign chartered institutions (IBAs). As of March 1991, this item is reported on a quarterly basis and is listed as memoranda only. beginning June 2006, excludes retirement accounts for call reporters and beginning September 2006, excludes retirement accounts for ibas.

name: DEPLGRA
label: Amount ($) of Retirement Deposit Accounts of More Than $250,000
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The total amount of retirement deposit accounts (demand, savings, and time) of more than $250,000 held in domestic offices and Insured branches in US territories.

name: DEPLGRAR
label: Amount ($) of Retirement Deposit Accounts of More Than $250,000
definition: ( YTD, % ) The total amount of retirement deposit accounts (demand, savings, and time) of more than $250,000 held in domestic offices and Insured branches in US territories.

name: DEPLGRN
label: Number (#) of Retirement Deposit Accounts of More Than $250,000
definition: ( YTD, $ and % ) The total number of retirement deposit accounts (demand, savings, and time) with a balance on the report date of more than $250,000 held in domestic offices and Insured branches in US territories.

name: DEPLSNB
label: Deposits Obtained Via List Services, Yet Not Brokered
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Estimated amount of deposits obtained through the use of deposit listing services that are not brokered. Available as of March 2011

name: DEPLSNBR
label: Deposits Obtained Via List Services, Yet Not Brokered
definition: ( YTD, % ) Estimated amount of deposits obtained through the use of deposit listing services that are not brokered. Available as of March 2011

name: DEPNIDOM
label: Noninterest-Bearing Deposits in Domestic Offices
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The sum of total demand deposits and noninterest-bearing time and savings deposits held in domestic offices. Prior to 2004, this item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: DEPNIDOMR
label: Noninterest-Bearing Deposits in Domestic Offices
definition: ( YTD, % ) The sum of total demand deposits and noninterest-bearing time and savings deposits held in domestic offices. Prior to 2004, this item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: DEPNIFOR
label: Noninterest-Bearing Deposits in Foreign Offices
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The sum of total demand deposits and noninterest-bearing time and savings deposits held in foreign offices. represents any deposit in foreign offices on which the bank does not pay or accrue interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: DEPNIFORR
label: Noninterest-Bearing Deposits in Foreign Offices
definition: ( YTD, % ) The sum of total demand deposits and noninterest-bearing time and savings deposits held in foreign offices. represents any deposit in foreign offices on which the bank does not pay or accrue interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: DEPOTHF
label: All Other Deposits (Foreign Offices Only)
definition: ( Qtly, % ) All other transaction and nontransaction accounts held in foreign offices.

name: DEPR
label: Total deposits
definition: ( YTD, % ) The sum of all deposits including demand deposits, money market deposits, other savings deposits, time deposits and deposits in foreign offices.

name: DEPSMAMT
label: Amount ($) Deposit Accounts of $100,000 Or Less
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Amount of deposit accounts of $100,000 or less held in domestic offices and in Insured branches in Puerto Rico and U.S. territories and possessions beginning September 2009, institutions are required to report most deposits on the $250,000 threshold levels. beginning June 2006, excludes retirement accounts for Call Reporters. Beginning September 2006, excludes retirement accounts for Insured U.S. branches of a foreign chartered institutions (IBA). Beginning December 2006, excludes retirement accounts for TFR Reporters.

name: DEPSMAMTR
label: Amount ($) Deposit Accounts of $100,000 Or Less
definition: ( YTD, % ) Amount of deposit accounts of $100,000 or less held in domestic offices and in Insured branches in Puerto Rico and U.S. territories and possessions beginning September 2009, institutions are required to report most deposits on the $250,000 threshold levels. beginning June 2006, excludes retirement accounts for Call Reporters. Beginning September 2006, excludes retirement accounts for Insured U.S. branches of a foreign chartered institutions (IBA). Beginning December 2006, excludes retirement accounts for TFR Reporters.

name: DEPSMB
label: Number (#) of Deposit Accounts of $ 100,000 Or Less (June Only for Call)
definition: ( YTD, $ and % ) Number of deposit accounts of $ 100,000 or less held in domestic offices. This is listed as a memoranda only. beginning September 2009, institutions are required to report most deposits on the $250,000 threshold levels. Beginning June 2006, excludes retirement accounts for Call Reporters. Call Reporters provide this item only on the June Call report. TFR Reporters provide this item each quarter.

name: DEPSMRA
label: Amount ($) of Retirement Deposit Accounts of $250,000 Or Less
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The balance of all retirement deposit accounts (demand, savings, and time) with a balance of $250,000 or less held in domestic offices and Insured branches in US territories. Retirement deposit accounts that are eligible for $250,000 in deposit insurance coverage are deposits made in connection with the following types of retirement plans: Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs), including traditional and Roth IRAs; Simplified Employee Pension (SEP) plans; Section 457 deferred compensation plans; Self-directed Keogh (HR 10) plans; and Self-directed defined contribution plans, which are primarily 401(k) plan accounts.  The term ‘‘self-directed’’ means that the plan participants have the right to direct how their funds are invested, including the ability to direct that the funds be deposited at an FDIC-Insured institution. Retirement deposit accounts exclude Coverdell Education Savings Accounts, formerly known as Education IRAs.  Notice: Insured deposit amounts or deposit thresholds reflect permanent insurance limits in effect as of the report date, not withstanding temporary increases currently in effect.

name: DEPSMRAR
label: Amount ($) of Retirement Deposit Accounts of $250,000 Or Less
definition: ( YTD, % ) The balance of all retirement deposit accounts (demand, savings, and time) with a balance of $250,000 or less held in domestic offices and Insured branches in US territories. Retirement deposit accounts that are eligible for $250,000 in deposit insurance coverage are deposits made in connection with the following types of retirement plans: Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs), including traditional and Roth IRAs; Simplified Employee Pension (SEP) plans; Section 457 deferred compensation plans; Self-directed Keogh (HR 10) plans; and Self-directed defined contribution plans, which are primarily 401(k) plan accounts.  The term ‘‘self-directed’’ means that the plan participants have the right to direct how their funds are invested, including the ability to direct that the funds be deposited at an FDIC-Insured institution. Retirement deposit accounts exclude Coverdell Education Savings Accounts, formerly known as Education IRAs.  Notice: Insured deposit amounts or deposit thresholds reflect permanent insurance limits in effect as of the report date, not withstanding temporary increases currently in effect.

name: DEPSMRN
label: Number (#) of Retirement Deposit Accounts of $250,000 Or Less
definition: ( YTD, $ and % ) Total number of retirement deposit accounts (demand, savings, and time) with a balance on the report date of $250,000 or less, held in domestic offices and in Insured branches in US territories. important note: for call reporters and iba filers, this item is reported only on the June call report.

name: DEPSUM
label: DEPSUM
definition: Total deposits of the institution as of June 30.

name: DEPSUMBR
label: DEPSUMBR
definition: Branch office deposits as of June 30

name: DEPTRNA
label: Amount ($) - Non Interest Bearing Accounts of More Than $250,000
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Amount of non-interest-bearing transaction accounts of more than $250,000 held in domestic offices and in Insured branches in Puerto Rico and US territories and possessions. this item is reported by Insured institutions that are participating in the FDIC transaction account guarantee program.

name: DEPTRNN
label: Number (#) of Non Interest Bearing Accounts of More Than $250,000
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Number of non-interest-bearing transaction accounts of more than $250,000 held in domestic offices and in Insured branches in Puerto Rico and US Territories and possessions. this item is reported by Insured institutions that are participating in the FDIC transaction account guarantee program.

name: DEPTRNNJ
label: Average # of Non Interest Bearing Trans Accts > $250,000
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Average daily number of noninterest-bearing transaction accounts of more than $250,000 held in domestic offices and in Insured branches in Puerto Rico and US territories and possessions. note: 1. this item is filed by Insured institutions that elected to participate in the transaction account guarantee program. 2. prior to September 2010, this item is reported as the number of noninterest-bearing transaction accounts of more than $250,000 held in domestic offices and in Insured branches in Puerto Rico and US territories and possessions at the end of the reporting period.

name: DEPUNA
label: Estimated uninsured deposits in domestic offices in insured branches in U.S. territories and possessions
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Estimated amount of uninsured deposits in domestic offices and in insured branches in Puerto Rico and U.S. territories and possessions reported by the institution.  Please refer to the FFIEC CALL, TFR (before 2012) , and FFIEC 002  instructions regarding insurance limit changes.  Reporting Notes: Beginning September 2019 this item is required to be reported by FFIEC CALL form 051 filers with $1 billion or more in total assets.  Other CALL form 51 reporters do not file this item.   Beginning in March 2006, this item is reported by banks and thrifts with $1 billion or more in total assets.   Prior to March 2002, reported for domestic offices only

name: DEPUNAR
label: Estimated uninsured deposits in domestic offices in insured branches in U.S. territories and possessions
definition: ( YTD, % ) Estimated amount of uninsured deposits in domestic offices and in insured branches in Puerto Rico and U.S. territories and possessions reported by the institution.  Please refer to the FFIEC CALL, TFR (before 2012) , and FFIEC 002  instructions regarding insurance limit changes.  Reporting Notes: Beginning September 2019 this item is required to be reported by FFIEC CALL form 051 filers with $1 billion or more in total assets.  Other CALL form 51 reporters do not file this item.   Beginning in March 2006, this item is reported by banks and thrifts with $1 billion or more in total assets.   Prior to March 2002, reported for domestic offices only

name: DEPUSBKF
label: Deposits: Commercial Banks and Other Depository Institutions in U.S.
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Deposits of U.S. banks (including IBFs and foreign branches of U.S. banks) and other depository institutions in the U.S that are held in foreign offices.

name: DEPUSBKFR
label: Deposits: Commercial Banks and Other Depository Institutions in U.S.
definition: ( YTD, % ) Deposits of U.S. banks (including IBFs and foreign branches of U.S. banks) and other depository institutions in the U.S that are held in foreign offices.

name: DEPUSMF
label: Deposits: U.S Government, States, and Political Subdivisions
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Deposits of U.S. government, states and political subdivisions in the U.S. that are held in foreign offices.

name: DEPUSMFR
label: Deposits: U.S Government, States, and Political Subdivisions
definition: ( YTD, % ) Deposits of U.S. government, states and political subdivisions in the U.S. that are held in foreign offices.

name: DIVISIONB
label: DIVISIONB
definition: A county or group of counties within a Core Based Statistical Area that contains a core with a population of at least 2.5 million. A Metropolitan Division consists of one or more main/secondary counties that represent an employment center or centers, plus adjacent counties associated with the main county or counties through commuting ties.

name: DOCKET
label: OTS Docket Number
definition: An identification number assigned to institutions chartered by the Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) or members of the Federal Housing Finance Board (FHFB) and formerly by the Federal Home Loan Bank (FHLB) Board. The value is '00000' for institutions not members of the FHFB.

name: DRAG
label: Loans to Finance Agricultural Production and Other Loans to Farmers
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total loans to finance agricultural production and other loans to farmers that have been charged-off and debited to the allowance for loan and lease losses established by the institution. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: DRAGQ
label: Loans to Finance Agricultural Production and Other Loans to Farmers
definition: ( Qtly, $ ) Total loans to finance agricultural production and other loans to farmers that have been charged-off and debited to the allowance for loan and lease losses established by the institution. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: DRAGQR
label: Loans to Finance Agricultural Production and Other Loans to Farmers
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total loans to finance agricultural production and other loans to farmers that have been charged-off and debited to the allowance for loan and lease losses established by the institution. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: DRAGR
label: Loans to Finance Agricultural Production and Other Loans to Farmers

name: ELNANTR
label: Loan and Lease Loss Provision to Net Charge-Offs
definition: ( YTD, % ) Provision for loan losses as a percent of total net charge-offs on a consolidated basis. Beginning March 2019, if an institution adopts CECL (ASU2016-13), this item represents the provision for credit losses on loans and leases held for investment in the numerator. If the denominator is less than or equal to zero, then ratio is shown as 'NA.'

name: ELNATQ
label: Provision For Credit Losses
definition: ( Qtly, $ ) The amount needed to make the allowance for loan and lease losses adequate to absorb expected loan and lease losses (based upon management's evaluation of the bank's current loan and lease portfolio). call reporters: prior to 2001 and after 2002, an allowance for transfer risk is also included to cover losses on international assets. Additionally, from 1997 to 2000, includes provision for credit losses on off-balance sheet credit exposures. TFR Reporters: Reflects net provision for losses on interest-bearing assets.

name: ELNATQR
label: Loan and Lease Loss Provision to Net Charge-Offs
definition: ( Qtly, % ) The amount needed to make the allowance for loan and lease losses adequate to absorb expected loan and lease losses (based upon management's evaluation of the bank's current loan and lease portfolio). call reporters: prior to 2001 and after 2002, an allowance for transfer risk is also included to cover losses on international assets. Additionally, from 1997 to 2000, includes provision for credit losses on off-balance sheet credit exposures. TFR Reporters: Reflects net provision for losses on interest-bearing assets.

name: ELNATR
label: Provision For Credit Losses
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The amount needed to make the allowance for loan and lease losses adequate to absorb expected loan and lease losses (based upon management's evaluation of the bank's current loan and lease portfolio). call reporters: prior to 2001 and after 2002, an allowance for transfer risk is also included to cover losses on international assets. Additionally, from 1997 to 2000, includes provision for credit losses on off-balance sheet credit exposures. TFR Reporters: Reflects net provision for losses on interest-bearing assets.

name: ELNATRR
label: Provision For Credit Losses
definition: ( YTD, % ) The amount needed to make the allowance for loan and lease losses adequate to absorb expected loan and lease losses (based upon management's evaluation of the bank's current loan and lease portfolio). call reporters: prior to 2001 and after 2002, an allowance for transfer risk is also included to cover losses on international assets. Additionally, from 1997 to 2000, includes provision for credit losses on off-balance sheet credit exposures. TFR Reporters: Reflects net provision for losses on interest-bearing assets.

name: ELNATRY
label: Credit Loss Provision to Assets
definition: ( YTD, % ) The annualized provision for loans and lease losses as a percent of average total assets on a consolidated basis. For institutions that have adopted CECL methodology (ASU 2016-13), this item represents provisions for all credit losses on a consolidated basis. For institutions that have not adopted CECL methodology (ASU 2016-13), this item represents the provision for loan and lease losses.

name: ELNATRYQ
label: Credit Loss Provision to Assets
definition: ( Qtly, % ) The annualized provision for loans and lease losses as a percent of average total assets on a consolidated basis. For institutions that have adopted CECL methodology (ASU 2016-13), this item represents provisions for all credit losses on a consolidated basis. For institutions that have not adopted CECL methodology (ASU 2016-13), this item represents the provision for loan and lease losses.

name: ELNLOSF
label: Provision for Credit Losses in Foreign Offices
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total loans secured by 1-4 family residential properties (which are held in domestic offices) that have been charged-off and debited to the allowance for loan and lease losses established by the institution.

name: ENCEAUTOR

name: ENCECI

definition: ( YTD, $ ) For institutions that have adopted CECL methodology (ASU 2016-13), this item represents provisions for all credit losses on a consolidated basis. For institutions that have not adopted CECL methodology (ASU 2016-13), this item represents the provision for loan and lease losses.

Also beginning June 2018  All other loans also includes home equity lines and  credit card receivables for institutions filing a FFIEC Call Report 31 .

Also beginning June 2018  All other loans also includes home equity lines and  credit card receivables for institutions filing a FFIEC Call Report 31 .

name: EPREMAGG
label: Premises and Equipment Expense
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Expenses of bank premises and fixed assets, net of rental income. Excludes salaries and employee benefits and mortgage interest.

name: EPREMAGGR
label: Premises and Equipment Expense
definition: ( YTD, % ) Expenses of bank premises and fixed assets, net of rental income. Excludes salaries and employee benefits and mortgage interest.

name: EPREMAGQ
label: Premises and Equipment Expense
definition: ( Qtly, $ ) Expenses of bank premises and fixed assets, net of rental income. Excludes salaries and employee benefits and mortgage interest.

name: EPREMAGQR
label: Premises and Equipment Expense
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Expenses of bank premises and fixed assets, net of rental income. Excludes salaries and employee benefits and mortgage interest.

name: EQ
label: Equity capital
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total bank equity capital (includes preferred and common stock, surplus and undivided profits).

name: EQ5
label: Average Equity
definition: ( Qtly, % ) The average of total equity capital (includes preferred and common stock, surplus and undivided profits). Averages are calculated as follows: Year-to-date averages: March reporting period - (December equity + March equity) / 2 June reporting period - (December equity + March equity + June equity) / 3 September reporting period - (December equity + March equity + June equity + September equity) / 4 December reporting period - (Previous December equity + March equity + June equity + September equity + December equity) / 5 Quarterly averages: All reporting periods – (Previous quarter equity + current quarter equity) / 2

name: EQCACCHG
label: Cumulative Effect of Changes in Accounting Principles from Prior Years
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Cumulative effect of changes in accounting principles adopted during the calendar year-to-date reporting period that were applied retroactively and for which prior years financial statements were restated. Institutions with total assets of less than $100 million file these data only on the December Call Report.

name: EQCACCOR
label: Corrections of Material Accounting Errors from Prior Years
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Corrections of material accounting errors from the prior years which were not corrected for the period in which the errors were made. Institutions with total assets of less than $100 million file these data only on the December Call Report. This item is not available for TFR Reporters, which ended in Dec. 2011.

name: EQCADJ
label: Adjustments from Amended Reports of Income, Net
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Net equity capital adjustments from amended reports of income of the institution that were made for the previous calendar year-end. Institutions with total assets of less than $100 million file these data only on the December Call Report. This item is not available for TFR Reporters, which ended in Dec. 2011.

name: EQCBEG
label: Total Equity Capital, Balance At Previous Year-End
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Beginning balance of the total equity capital of an institution at previous calendar year-end.

name: EQCBHCTR
label: Other Transactions With Parent Holding Company
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Other transactions involving the institution's parent holding company which affect equity capital directly such as capital contributions other than those for stock or dividends to the holding company in the form of property rather than cash. Institutions with total assets of less than $100 million file these data only on the December Call Report.   Prior to 2004, This item is not available for TFR Reporters, which ended in Dec. 2011.

name: EQCBHCTRR
label: Other Transactions With Parent Holding Company
definition: ( YTD, % ) Other transactions involving the institution's parent holding company which affect equity capital directly such as capital contributions other than those for stock or dividends to the holding company in the form of property rather than cash. Institutions with total assets of less than $100 million file these data only on the December Call Report.   Prior to 2004, This item is not available for TFR Reporters, which ended in Dec. 2011.

name: EQCCOMPI
label: Other Comprehensive Income
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Other comprehensive income The bank’s other comprehensive income for the calendar year-to-date. Other comprehensive income includes: The change during the calendar year-to-date in net unrealized holding gains (losses) on the bank’s available-for-sale securities. The change during the calendar year-to-date in the bank’s accumulated net gains (losses) on cash flow hedges. On the FFIEC 031 only, the increase or decrease during the calendar year-to-date in the bank’s cumulative foreign currency translation adjustments and qualifying foreign currency transaction gains and losses, net of applicable income taxes, if any. The change during the calendar year-to-date in any minimum pension liability adjustment recognized in accordance with FASB Statement No. 87, Employers’ Accounting for Pensions. Prior to 2004, This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: EQCCOMPIR
label: Other Comprehensive Income
definition: ( YTD, % ) Other comprehensive income The bank’s other comprehensive income for the calendar year-to-date. Other comprehensive income includes: The change during the calendar year-to-date in net unrealized holding gains (losses) on the bank’s available-for-sale securities. The change during the calendar year-to-date in the bank’s accumulated net gains (losses) on cash flow hedges. On the FFIEC 031 only, the increase or decrease during the calendar year-to-date in the bank’s cumulative foreign currency translation adjustments and qualifying foreign currency transaction gains and losses, net of applicable income taxes, if any. The change during the calendar year-to-date in any minimum pension liability adjustment recognized in accordance with FASB Statement No. 87, Employers’ Accounting for Pensions. Prior to 2004, This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: EQCDIV

name: EQCDIVC

name: EQCDIVCR

name: EQCDIVNTINC

name: EQCDIVQR

name: EQCMRG

definition: ( YTD, $ ) Changes that occur as a result of pooling of interest, purchase acquisition, or push down accounting. Institutions with total assets of less than $100 million file these data only on the December Call Report. This item is not available for TFR Reporters, which ended in Dec. 2011.

name: EQCMRGR

name: EQCOTHIN

name: EQCPREV

name: EQCREST

definition: ( YTD, $ ) The sum of all corrections, net of applicable income taxes, resulting from material accounting errors which were made in prior years' Reports of Condition and Income and not corrected by the filing of an amended report for the period in which the error was made. Also includes the cumulative effect, net of applicable income taxes, of those changes in accounting principles adopted during the calendar year-to-date reporting period that were applied retroactively and for which prior years' financial statements were restated. prior to 2004, This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: EQCRESTR
label: Restatements from Amended Reports of Income, Net
definition: ( YTD, % ) The sum of all corrections, net of applicable income taxes, resulting from material accounting errors which were made in prior years' Reports of Condition and Income and not corrected by the filing of an amended report for the period in which the error was made. Also includes the cumulative effect, net of applicable income taxes, of those changes in accounting principles adopted during the calendar year-to-date reporting period that were applied retroactively and for which prior years' financial statements were restated. prior to 2004, This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: EQCS
label: Common Stock
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Common stock issued by the institution.

name: EQCSR
label: Common Stock
definition: ( YTD, % ) Common stock issued by the institution.

name: EQCSTKRX
label: Net Additions to Capital Stock
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Sale, conversion, acquisition, or retirement of capital stock, net. Included are the sale of the institution's perpetual preferred stock or common stock; exercise of stock options, conversion of convertible debt, limited life preferred stock, or perpetual preferred stock into common stock or perpetual preferred; redemption and retirement of perpetual preferred stock or common stock; treasury stock transactions; and capital related transactions involving the institution's employee stock ownership plan. Institutions with total assets of less than $100 million file these data only on the December Call Report.

name: EQCSTKRXR
label: Net Additions to Capital Stock
definition: ( YTD, % ) Sale, conversion, acquisition, or retirement of capital stock, net. Included are the sale of the institution's perpetual preferred stock or common stock; exercise of stock options, conversion of convertible debt, limited life preferred stock, or perpetual preferred stock into common stock or perpetual preferred; redemption and retirement of perpetual preferred stock or common stock; treasury stock transactions; and capital related transactions involving the institution's employee stock ownership plan. Institutions with total assets of less than $100 million file these data only on the December Call Report.

name: EQCSTKX
label: Treasury Stock Transactions, Net
definition: ( Qtly, % ) The change in the bank’s total equity capital from the acquisition (without retirement) and resale or other disposal of the bank's own perpetual preferred stock or common stock, i.e., treasury stock. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: EQCSXQ
label: Net Additions to Capital Stock
definition: ( Qtly, $ ) Sale, conversion, acquisition, or retirement of capital stock, net. Included are the sale of the institution's perpetual preferred stock or common stock; exercise of stock options, conversion of convertible debt, limited life preferred stock, or perpetual preferred stock into common stock or perpetual preferred; redemption and retirement of perpetual preferred stock or common stock; treasury stock transactions; and capital related transactions involving the institution's employee stock ownership plan. Institutions with total assets of less than $100 million file these data only on the December Call Report.

name: EQCSXQR
label: Net Additions to Capital Stock
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Sale, conversion, acquisition, or retirement of capital stock, net. Included are the sale of the institution's perpetual preferred stock or common stock; exercise of stock options, conversion of convertible debt, limited life preferred stock, or perpetual preferred stock into common stock or perpetual preferred; redemption and retirement of perpetual preferred stock or common stock; treasury stock transactions; and capital related transactions involving the institution's employee stock ownership plan. Institutions with total assets of less than $100 million file these data only on the December Call Report.

name: EQCTRSTX
label: Net Treasury Stock Transactions
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The net treasury stock transaction on a consolidated basis. The change in the bank’s total equity capital during the calendar year to date from the acquisition (without retirement) and resale or other disposal of the bank's own perpetual preferred stock or common stock, i.e., treasury stock transactions.

name: EQCTRSTXR
label: Net Treasury Stock Transactions
definition: ( YTD, % ) The net treasury stock transaction on a consolidated basis. The change in the bank’s total equity capital during the calendar year to date from the acquisition (without retirement) and resale or other disposal of the bank's own perpetual preferred stock or common stock, i.e., treasury stock transactions.

name: EQCUGL
label: Changes in Net Unrealized Holding Gains (Losses), Available-For-Sale Securities
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Changes in net unrealized holding gains (losses) on available-for-sale securities on a consolidated basis. Call Reporters less than $100 million report this data only in December.

name: EQCUREAL
label: Changes in Net Unrealized Loss on Marketable Equity Securities Prior To 1994
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Changes in net unrealized loss on marketable equity securities on a consolidated basis for time periods prior to 1994. Call Reporters, less than $100 million only report this information in December.

name: EQPP
label: Perpetual Preferred Stock
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Perpetual preferred stock is preferred stock that does not have a stated maturity date or that cannot be redeemed at the option of the holder. It includes those issues of preferred stock that automatically convert into common stock at a stated date.

name: EQPPR
label: Perpetual Preferred Stock
definition: ( YTD, % ) Perpetual preferred stock is preferred stock that does not have a stated maturity date or that cannot be redeemed at the option of the holder. It includes those issues of preferred stock that automatically convert into common stock at a stated date.

name: EQQR
label: Equity Capital to Assets
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total equity capital as a percent of total assets.

name: EQR
label: Equity capital
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total bank equity capital (includes preferred and common stock, surplus and undivided profits).

name: EQSUR
label: Surplus
definition: ( YTD, $ ) That portion of an institution's capital received for shares of stock sold in excess of par value (excludes all surplus related to preferred stock).

name: EQSURR
label: Surplus
definition: ( YTD, % ) That portion of an institution's capital received for shares of stock sold in excess of par value (excludes all surplus related to preferred stock).

name: EQTOT
label: Total Equity Capital
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total equity capital on a consolidated basis note: 1) beginning March 2009, includes the non-controlling (minority) interests in consolidated subsidiaries for CALL report and TFR filers.

name: EQTOTR
label: Total Equity Capital
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total equity capital on a consolidated basis note: 1) beginning March 2009, includes the non-controlling (minority) interests in consolidated subsidiaries for CALL report and TFR filers.

name: EQUP
label: Undivided Profits
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Retained earnings from bank operations, net unrealized holding gains (losses) on available-for-sale securities, and cumulative foreign currency translation adjustments.

name: EQUPTOT
label: Undivided Profits
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Undivided profits, capital reserves, net unrealized holdings gains (losses) on available-for-sale securities, other equity capital components, and accumulated gains (losses) on cash flow hedges.

name: EQUPTOTR
label: Undivided Profits
definition: ( YTD, % ) Estimated amount of uninsured deposits in domestic offices and in insured branches in Puerto Rico and U.S. territories and possessions reported by the institution.  Please refer to the FFIEC CALL, TFR (before 2012) , and FFIEC 002  instructions regarding insurance limit changes.  Reporting Notes: Beginning September 2019 this item is required to be reported by FFIEC CALL form 051 filers with $1 billion or more in total assets.  Other CALL form 51 reporters do not file this item.   Beginning in March 2006, this item is reported by banks and thrifts with $1 billion or more in total assets.   Prior to March 2002, reported for domestic offices only

name: EQV
label: Equity Capital to Assets
definition: ( YTD, % ) Estimated amount of uninsured deposits in domestic offices and in insured branches in Puerto Rico and U.S. territories and possessions reported by the institution.  Please refer to the FFIEC CALL, TFR (before 2012) , and FFIEC 002  instructions regarding insurance limit changes.  Reporting Notes: Beginning September 2019 this item is required to be reported by FFIEC CALL form 051 filers with $1 billion or more in total assets.  Other CALL form 51 reporters do not file this item.   Beginning in March 2006, this item is reported by banks and thrifts with $1 billion or more in total assets.   Prior to March 2002, reported for domestic offices only

name: ERNAST
label: Earning Assets
definition: ( YTD, $ ) All loans and other investments that earn interest or dividend income.

name: ERNAST5
label: Average Earning Assets
definition: ( Qtly, % ) The average of all loans and other investments that earn interest or dividends. Averages are calculated as follows: Year-to-date averages: March reporting period - (December earning assets + March earning assets) / 2 June reporting period - (December earning assets + March earning assets + June earning assets) / 3 September reporting period - (December earning assets + March earning assets + June earning assets + September earning assets) / 4 December reporting period - (Previous December earning assets + March earning assets + June earning assets + September earning assets + December earning assets) / 5 Quarterly averages: All reporting periods – (Previous quarter earning assets + current quarter earning assets) / 2

name: ERNASTR
label: Earning Assets to total Assets
definition: ( YTD and Qtly, % ) Interest earning assets as a percent of total assets.

name: ESAL
label: Salaries and Employee Benefits
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Salaries and employee benefits expense.

name: ESALQ
label: Salaries and Employee Benefits
definition: ( Qtly, $ ) Salaries and employee benefits expense.

name: ESALQR
label: Salaries and Employee Benefits
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Salaries and employee benefits expense.

name: ESALR
label: Salaries and Employee Benefits
definition: ( YTD, % ) Salaries and employee benefits expense.

name: ESCROW
label: ESCROW
definition: Escrow deposits report on the Thrift Financial Reports.  These are included in the total domestic deposits on the Call Reports.

name: ESTABLISHED
label: Established Date
definition: ( Qtly, % ) After Date/Before Date - The date on which the institution began operations.

name: ESTINS
label: % Insured (Estimated)
definition: ( YTD, % ) Other comprehensive income The bank’s other comprehensive income for the calendar year-to-date. Other comprehensive income includes: The change during the calendar year-to-date in net unrealized holding gains (losses) on the bank’s available-for-sale securities. The change during the calendar year-to-date in the bank’s accumulated net gains (losses) on cash flow hedges. On the FFIEC 031 only, the increase or decrease during the calendar year-to-date in the bank’s cumulative foreign currency translation adjustments and qualifying foreign currency transaction gains and losses, net of applicable income taxes, if any. The change during the calendar year-to-date in any minimum pension liability adjustment recognized in accordance with FASB Statement No. 87, Employers’ Accounting for Pensions. Prior to 2004, This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: ESTINSR
label: % Insured (Estimated)
definition: ( YTD, % ) Cash dividends paid on common and preferred stock.

name: ESTYMD
label: Established Date
definition: The date on which the institution or branch began operations.

name: ESUBND
label: Subordinated Notes and Debenture
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total interest expense on subordinated notes and debentures.

name: ETTLOTMG
label: Demand Notes, US Treasury & Other
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total interest expense on demand notes issued to the US Treasury, other borrowed money and interest on mortgage indebtedness and obligations under capitalized leases on a consolidated basis.

name: EXTRA
label: Discontinued Operations (Extraordinary Gains
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Discontinued operations, net of applicable income taxes on a consolidated basis. Accounting rule change as of March 2016, now only includes Discontinued Operations Expense and not Extraordinary Gains (Net of Taxes).

name: EXTRAQ
label: Discontinued Operations (Extraordinary Gains
definition: ( Qtly, $ ) Discontinued operations, net of applicable income taxes on a consolidated basis. Accounting rule change as of March 2016, now only includes Discontinued Operations Expense and not Extraordinary Gains (Net of Taxes).

name: EXTRAQR
label: Discontinued Operations (Extraordinary Gains
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Discontinued operations, net of applicable income taxes on a consolidated basis. Accounting rule change as of March 2016, now only includes Discontinued Operations Expense and not Extraordinary Gains (Net of Taxes).

name: EXTRAR
label: Discontinued Operations (Extraordinary Gains
definition: ( YTD, % ) Discontinued operations, net of applicable income taxes on a consolidated basis. Accounting rule change as of March 2016, now only includes Discontinued Operations Expense and not Extraordinary Gains (Net of Taxes).

name: FAQ_E4
label: Statistics on Depository Institutions (Sdi): Frequently Asked Questions
definition: ( Qtly, % ) How do I create a report in SDI? Create a report in SDI by selecting 'Create or Modify Reports' from the top menu. Select the amount of columns that you wish to include in the report (from two to four). Enter your criteria for the report. You can analyze any combination of single FDIC-Insured institutions or bank holding companies, custom peer groups of FDIC-Insured institutions or bank holding companies, or standard peer groups of FDIC-Insured institutions. After completing the selection criteria, press 'Next' to continue processing. You will be at the 'Confirmation Report'. Confirm all column selections and modify the report date and/or income basis (year-to-date or quarterly) for any column. Then: Select from the 'Report Selection' box any of the predefined reports or choose 'Custom Report' to create a report of specific items of your choice. Decide to view or download the report. If 'View' is selected, you can choose to present information in dollar amounts or in percentage amounts. Press 'Next' If any of the predefined reports have been selected, the report will appear. If 'Custom Report' has been selected, you will be prompted to select those items that you wish to include in the report. After you select and review the items, press 'Add More' to include more items or 'Next' to generate the report. How are custom peer groups defined in SDI? Custom peer groups are user-defined groups of FDIC-Insured institutions or bank holding companies*. The user makes the decision of which banking entities to include in the custom peer group. SDI will then generate financial reports that calculate the financial information reported by the individual members of the peer group. These financial reports will display the peer group information in aggregate. Custom peer groups can consist of: a) the criteria used to create the peer group (unlimited membership), or b) individual institutions or bank holding companies up to up to a limit of 50 members. *Financial data for bank holding companies represent combined amounts for all FDIC-Insured bank and thrift subsidiaries, and do not reflect nondeposit subsidiaries or parent companies. Account balances have not been adjusted for intra-company transactions. Additional holding company information can be found at the website for the Federal Reserve System--National Information Center (NIC). What are standard peer groups? Standard peer groups are pre-defined and pre-calculated groups of FDIC-Insured institutions. These standard peer groups are used in FDIC publications such as the FDIC's Statistics on Banking. When possible, standard peer groups should be used instead of custom groups for faster processing. The available standard peer groups are included in the following table: Table of Standard Peer Groups What are Standard Reports? Standard Reports consist of pre-selected columns based on many of the standard peer groups that are used in FDIC publications (e.g. the FDIC's Statistics on Banking). First time users are encouraged to view the Standard Reports which may or may not meet their immediate need. These reports also provide the user with an idea of the amount of information and flexibility that is provided in SDI. To access a Standard Report, simply press the 'Run Report' option for the standard report that you wish to retrieve. The 'Assets and Liabilities' report is the default. Access any of the available SDI reports by choosing from the drop-down menu. Modify the column selections by choosing 'Create or Modify Reports' and changing the criteria. Available Standard reports I want to analyze data from SDI in a different software package. Can I download data from SDI? If so, how do I do it? Yes, you can download and save information provided by SDI. In fact, there are three distinctly different types of data download in SDI. Download data after column selections have been made. (Note: this download returns a maximum of 200 records) To download: Download larger amounts (up to 2.5 megabytes) of data without being restricted by the column selections. To download: Download more than 2.5 megabytes of data. To download: Select 'SDI Map, Definitions, and Data Download' download from the top menu bar. Select the 'Data Download' button and press 'Next'. Select the items that you wish to download from any of the available reports in SDI. Press 'Add More' or 'Next' to continue. A page titled 'Download Selection Criteria' will appear. Generate the list of institutions that you wish to download by entering criteria in the selection boxes that are provided. The selection boxes are divided into basic and advanced options. If entries are placed in multiple boxes, the criteria is treated as an 'and' condition. After all selection criteria has been entered the user must press 'Find.' A Download Selection Criteria Confirmation screen will appear. If your request results in a file estimated to be larger than 2.5 MB, you will be notified that the request can be processed during off-peak hours if you choose to continue with the request. Press 'Proceed' to continue. Enter your email address where prompted and press 'Process'. A pop-up screen will inform you that your request has been accepted, and that you will notified via e-mail upon completion of the request. You will receive an e-mail after the request is processed. The e-mail will include the file’s name and a link to access it. Please note that the file will be in compressed format (.ZIP). You will need to have a file compressor/de-compressor (e.g. WinZip, PKZip) to open the underlying data files of your request, which will be in comma separated value (.CSV) format. I have successfully created a custom peer group. Can I save this group for future use? Yes. I have successfully created a custom report. Can I save this report for future use? Yes.

name: FASOTH
label: Outstanding Principal Balance of Assets Transferred
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Outstanding principal balance of other financial assets transferred with recourse that has been treated as sold for Call Report purposes.

name: FASOTHRC
label: Amount of Recourse Exposure other financial assets transferred
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Amount of recourse exposure on other financial assets transferred to others that have been treated as sold for Call Report purposes.

name: FDICDBS
label: FDIC Region Number
definition:
The FDIC Office assigned to the geographic area. The eight FDIC Regions and their respective states are: 
Boston - Connecticut, Maine, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, Rhode Island, Vermont 
New York - Delaware, District of Columbia, Maryland, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Puerto Rico, U.S. Virgin Islands 
Atlanta - Alabama, Florida, Georgia, North Carolina, South Carolina, Virginia, West Virginia 
Memphis - Arkansas, Kentucky, Louisiana, Mississippi, Tennessee 
Chicago - Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Ohio, Wisconsin 
Kansas City - Iowa, Kansas, Minnesota, Missouri, Nebraska, North Dakota, South Dakota 
Dallas - Colorado, New Mexico, Oklahoma, Texas 
San Francisco - Alaska, American Samoa, Arizona, California, Guam, Hawaii, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, Oregon, States of Micronesia, Utah, Washington, Wyoming

name: FDICNAME
label: FDICNAME
definition: The name of the FDIC Regional Office that services the institution.

name: FDICREGION
label: Supervisory Region
definition: A two-digit number indicating the FDIC Supervisory Division or Region. 02 = New York; 05 = Atlanta; 09 = Chicago; 11 = Kansas City; 13 = Dallas; 14 = San Francisco; 16 = Office of Complex Financial Institutions (CFI)

name: FDICREGION_DESC
label: Supervisory Region Description
definition: A description associated with the name of an FDIC supervisory region

name: FDICREGN
label: FDIC Supervisory Region
definition: The supervisory FDIC office assigned to the institution. The eight FDIC Supervisory Regions and their respective states are: Boston - Connecticut, Maine, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, Rhode Island, Vermont New York - Delaware, District of Columbia, Maryland, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Puerto Rico, U.S. Virgin Islands Atlanta - Alabama, Florida, Georgia, North Carolina, South Carolina, Virginia, West Virginia Memphis - Arkansas, Kentucky, Louisiana, Mississippi, Tennessee Chicago - Illinois, Indiana, Michigan, Ohio, Wisconsin Kansas City - Iowa, Kansas, Minnesota, Missouri, Nebraska, North Dakota, South Dakota Dallas - Colorado, New Mexico, Oklahoma, Texas San Francisco - Alaska, American Samoa, Arizona, California, Guam, Hawaii, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, Oregon, States of Micronesia, Utah, Washington, Wyoming

name: FDICSUPV
label: Federal Reserve District
definition: The supervisory FDIC office assigned to the institution. There are twelve Federal Reserve Districts, with two Districts serving one state in some instances. The list of Federal Reserve Districts and their respective states are as follows: Boston - Connecticut, Maine, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, Rhode Island, Vermont New York - Connecticut, New Jersey, New York, Puerto Rico U.S. Virgin Islands Philadelphia - Delaware, New Jersey, Pennsylvania Cleveland - Kentucky, Ohio, Pennsylvania, West Virginia Richmond - Maryland, North Carolina, South Carolina, Virginia, West Virginia Atlanta - Alabama, Florida, Georgia, Louisiana, Mississippi, Tennessee Chicago - Illinois, Indiana, Iowa, Michigan, Wisconsin St. Louis - Arkansas, Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Mississippi, Missouri, Tennessee Minneapolis - Michigan, Minnesota, Montana, North Dakota, South Dakota, Wisconsin Kansas City - Colorado, Kansas, Missouri, Nebraska, New Mexico, Oklahoma, Wyoming Dallas - Louisiana, New Mexico, Texas San Francisco> - Alaska, American Samoa, Arizona, California, Guam, Hawaii, Idaho, Nevada, Oregon, States of Micronesia, Utah, Washington

name: FED
label: Federal Reserve ID Number
definition:
A number used to identify the Federal Reserve district in which the institution is located. 
01 - Boston, 
02 - New York,
03 - Philadelphia, 
04 - Cleveland,
05 - Richmond,
06 - Atlanta,
07 - Chicago,
08 - St. Louis,
09 - Minneapolis,
10 - Kansas city,
11 - Dallas,
12 - San Francisco

name: FEDCHRTR
label: Federal charter flag
definition: A flag used to indicate whether the institution is chartered by an agent of the federal government.

name: FEDNAME
label: FEDNAME
definition: The name that identifies the Federal Reserve District where the institution is located.

name: FED_RSSD
label: Federal Reserve ID Number
definition: A unique number assigned by the Federal Reserve board as the entity's unique identifier

name: FI_EFFDATE
label: Last Structure Change Effective Date
definition: A date indicating when an institution's change/event is effective.

name: FI_UNINIM
label: FDIC's unique number
definition: FDIC's unique identifier number for holding companies, banks, branches and nondeposit subsidiaries. This value maps the branch back to the parent financial institution.

name: FI_UNINUM
label: FDIC UNINUM of the Owner Institution
definition: This is the FDIC UNINUM of the institution that owns the branch.  A UNINUM is a unique sequentially number added to the FDIC database for both banks and  branches.  There is no pattern imbedded within the number.  The FI_UNINUM is updated with every merger or purchase of branches to reflect the most current owner.

name: FLDOFF
label: FDIC Field Office
definition: The FDIC Field Office where an institution is physically located.

name: FORM31
label: FFIEC Call Report 31 Filer
definition: A flag (1=yes,0=no) that indicates whether and institution filed an FFIEC 031 Call Report. Commercial banks with domestic and foreign offices are required to file such a report.

name: FREPO
label: Federal Funds Sold & Reverse Repurchase Agreements
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total federal funds sold and securities purchased under agreements to resell in domestic offices. Includes only federal Funds sold for TRF Reporters before March 1998.

name: FREPOFOR
label: Federal Funds Sold and Reverse Repurchase Agreements
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total federal funds sold and securities purchased under agreements to resell held in foreign offices. Includes only federal Funds sold for TRF Reporters before March 1998.

name: FREPOR
label: Federal Funds Sold & Reverse Repurchase Agreements
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total federal funds sold and securities purchased under agreements to resell in domestic offices. Includes only federal Funds sold for TRF Reporters before March 1998.

name: FREPP
label: Federal Funds Purchased and Repurchase Agreements
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total federal funds purchased and securities sold under agreements to repurchase in domestic offices. Thrift Financial Reports include only federal funds purchased.

name: FREPPFOR
label: Federal Funds Purchased and Repurchase Agreements Foreign Offices
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Federal funds purchased and securities sold under agreements to repurchase held in foreign offices.

name: FREPPR
label: Federal Funds Purchased and Repurchase Agreements Foreign Offices
definition: ( YTD, % ) Federal funds purchased and securities sold under agreements to repurchase held in foreign offices.

name: FRM_CERT
label: Previous FDIC Certificate #
definition: A unique NUMBER assigned by the FDIC used to identify institutions and for the issuance of insurance certificates.

name: FRM_CHARTAGENT
label: Previous Chartering Agency
definition: Previous Institution Chartering Agency - State and Federal, Comptroller of the Currency - Chartering authority for nationally chartered commercial banks and for federally chartered savings associations (The Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) before 7/21/11) State (includes U.S. Territories) - Chartering authority for institutions that are not chartered by the OCC or OTS

name: FRM_CLASS
label: Previous Bank Charter Class
definition: A code indicating the major groupings of institutions. N = FDIC Insured National Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; SM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; NM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, or a Mutual Commercial Bank; SI = FDIC Insured Stock and Mutual Savings Bank; MI = Mutually Insured; SB = FDIC Insured Savings Bank; SL = FDIC Insured Savings and Loan Association; OI = Other FDIC Insured Institution (International Banking Act); CU = Credit Union; NC = Non-insured Institution; NS = Non-insured Savings Association

name: FRM_CLCODE
label: Previous Numeric Code
definition: Numeric code which identifies the major and minor categories of an institution.

name: FRM_CNTYNAME
label: Previous County
definition: County where the institution is physically located (abbreviated if the county name exceeds 16 characters).

name: FRM_CNTYNUM
label: Previous County Number
definition: The federal information processing standards (FIPS) numeric code.

name: FRM_INSTNAME
label: Previous Institution Name
definition: The legal name of the institution.

name: FRM_OFFNAME
label: Previous Branch Name
definition: Previous name of a branch office.

name: FRM_OFFNUM
label: Previous Branch Number
definition: An institution branch office's previous number.

name: FRM_OFF_CHARTAGENT
label: Previous Office Chartering Agency
definition: Previous Office Chartering Agency - State and Federal, Comptroller of the Currency - Chartering authority for nationally chartered commercial banks and for federally chartered savings associations (The Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) before 7/21/11) State (includes U.S. Territories) - Chartering authority for institutions that are not chartered by the OCC or OTS

name: FRM_OFF_CLASS
label: Previous Office Class
definition: A code indicating the major groupings of institutions. N = FDIC Insured National Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; SM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; NM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, or a Mutual Commercial Bank; SI = FDIC Insured Stock and Mutual Savings Bank; MI = Mutually Insured; SB = FDIC Insured Savings Bank; SL = FDIC Insured Savings and Loan Association; OI = Other FDIC Insured Institution (International Banking Act); CU = Credit Union; NC = Non-insured Institution; NS = Non-insured Savings Association

name: FRM_OFF_CLCODE
label: Previous Office Numeric Code
definition: Numeric code which identifies the major and minor categories of an institution.

name: FRM_OFF_CNTYNAME
label: Previous Office County
definition: County where the branch was previously physically located (abbreviated if the county name exceeds 16 characters).

name: FRM_OFF_CNTYNUM
label: Previous County Number
definition: The federal information processing standards (FIPS) numeric code where the branch was previously physically located.

name: FRM_OFF_NAME
label: Previous Office Name
definition: Name of the branch.

name: FRM_OFF_NUM
label: Previous Office Number
definition: The branch's corresponding office number.

name: FRM_OFF_PADDR
label: Previous Physical Street Address
definition: Street address at which the institution or one of its branches was previously physically located.

name: FRM_OFF_PADDR2
label: Previous Physical Street Address Line 2
definition: Street address line 2 at which the institution or one of its branches was previously physically located.

name: FRM_OFF_PCITY
label: Previous City
definition: City in which an institution's main office or one of its branches was previously physically located. Either the entire name or part of the name of a specific city may be entered to produce an Institution List.

name: FRM_OFF_PSTALP
label: Previous State Alpha Code
definition: State in which an institution's main office or one of its branches were previously physically located. The FDIC Act defines state as any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, and any territory of the United States, Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, the Virgin Island, and the Northern Mariana Islands.

name: FRM_OFF_PZIP5
label: Previous Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code representing the previous physical location of the institution or one of its branches.

name: FRM_OFF_PZIP5_RAW
label: Previous 5-Digit Zip Code
definition: The 5-digit postal code of a branch office's previous physical location.

name: FRM_OFF_PZIPREST
label: Previous Zip Code Extension
definition: The 4-digit zip code extension of an institution or one of its branches previous physical location.

name: FRM_OFF_REGAGENT
label: Previous Regulator
definition: There are three federal regulators of banks and savings and loan institutions: Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation (FDIC) - primary federal regulator responsible for state-chartered banks not members of the Federal Reserve System and state chartered savings banks; Federal Reserve Board (FRB) - primary federal regulator responsible for state-chartered commercial bank members of the Federal Reserve System.; Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) - primary federal regulator responsible for nationally chartered commercial banks and federally chartered savings and loan associations. Before 7/21/11, Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) - primary federal regulator responsible for federally chartered savings and loan associations, federal savings banks and state-chartered savings and loan associations. FDIC insured depository institutions are members of the Deposit Insurance Fund (DIF).

name: FRM_OFF_SERVTYPE
label: Previous Service Type
definition: Types of services offered by an office. Full service offices accept deposits, make loans, open/close accounts, have a loan officer on site, have normal hours, and a full time staff.
