label: Commercial and Similar Letters of Credit
definition: ( YTD, % ) The amount of outstanding and unused commercial and similar letters of credit. Issued or confirmed commercial letters of credit, non-monetary travelers' letters of credit and similar non-standby letters of credit are issued specifically to facilitate trade or commerce.

name: LOCFPSB
label: Financial and Performance Standby Letters of Credit & Foreign Office Guarantees
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The amount of outstanding and unused financial standby letters of credit, foreign office guarantees, performance standby letters of credit, and foreign office guarantees (including cash collateral standby letters of credit).

name: LOCFPSBF
label: Unused Standby Letters of Credit Issued To Non-U.S. Addressees
definition: ( Qtly, % ) The amount of outstanding and unused standby letters of credit and foreign office guarantees (both financial and performance) issued to non-U.S. addressees. This item is only reported by banks with $1 billion or more in total assets.

name: LOCFPSBK
label: Standby Letters of Credit Amount Conveyed To Others
definition: ( YTD, $ ) That portion of financial standby letters of credit, foreign office guarantees, performance standby letters of credit, and foreign office guarantees (including cash collateral standby letters of credit) conveyed to others.

name: LOCFPSBKR
label: Standby Letters of Credit Amount Conveyed To Others
definition: ( YTD, % ) That portion of financial standby letters of credit, foreign office guarantees, performance standby letters of credit, and foreign office guarantees (including cash collateral standby letters of credit) conveyed to others.

name: LOCFPSBR
label: Financial and Performance Standby Letters of Credit & Foreign Office Guarantees
definition: ( YTD, % ) The amount of outstanding and unused financial standby letters of credit, foreign office guarantees, performance standby letters of credit, and foreign office guarantees (including cash collateral standby letters of credit).

name: LOCFSB
label: Financial Standby Letters of Credit & Foreign Office Guarantees
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The amount of outstanding and unused financial standby letters of credit and foreign office guarantees.

name: LOCFSBK
label: Amount Conveyed To Others
definition: ( YTD, $ ) That portion of financial standby letters of credit and foreign office guarantees conveyed to others.

name: LOCFSBKR
label: Amount Conveyed To Others
definition: ( YTD, % ) That portion of financial standby letters of credit and foreign office guarantees conveyed to others.

name: LOCFSBR
label: Financial Standby Letters of Credit & Foreign Office Guarantees
definition: ( YTD, % ) The amount of outstanding and unused financial standby letters of credit and foreign office guarantees.

name: LOCN_PHY_ST_NUM
label: State Number
definition: A unique number identifying the state in which a physical address is located. This number is provided by the Federal Information Processing System (FIPS).

name: LOCPSB
label: Performance Standby Letters of Credit
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The amount of outstanding and unused performance standby letters of credit. A performance standby letter of credit irrevocably obligates the financial institution to pay a third party beneficiary when a customer fails to perform some contractual nonfinancial obligation.

name: LOCPSBK
label: Letter of Credit Amount Conveyed To Others
definition: ( YTD, $ ) That portion of performance standby letters of credit and foreign office guarantees conveyed to others.

name: LOCPSBKR
label: Letter of Credit Amount Conveyed To Others
definition: ( YTD, % ) That portion of performance standby letters of credit and foreign office guarantees conveyed to others.

name: LOCPSBR
label: Performance Standby Letters of Credit
definition: ( YTD, % ) The amount of outstanding and unused performance standby letters of credit. A performance standby letter of credit irrevocably obligates the financial institution to pay a third party beneficiary when a customer fails to perform some contractual nonfinancial obligation.

name: LONGITUDE
label: Longitude
definition: The longitude of the physical address.

name: LOREGTY
label: Maximum recoverable portion of other real estate owned protected under FDIC loss share agreements
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Maximum amount recoverable of other real estate owned (ore) - protected portion of covered other real estate owned included in all the categories of ore ( CALL schedule RC-M 13b1 through 6) that are covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. available as of March 2011

name: LOREGTYR
label: Maximum recoverable portion of other real estate owned protected under FDIC loss share agreements
definition: ( YTD, % ) Maximum amount recoverable of other real estate owned (ore) - protected portion of covered other real estate owned included in all the categories of ore ( CALL schedule RC-M 13b1 through 6) that are covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. available as of March 2011

name: LOTH
label: All other loans and all leases
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Carrying amount of all other loans and leases that are covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Note: This item includes obligations (other than securities leases) of states and political subdivisions in the U.S. and loans to nondepository financial institutions. For TFR and form 041 filers, this item also includes loans to finance agricultural production and other loans farmers. Available as of March 2011

name: LOTHR
label: All other loans and all leases
definition: ( YTD, % ) Carrying amount of all other loans and leases that are covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Note: This item includes obligations (other than securities leases) of states and political subdivisions in the U.S. and loans to nondepository financial institutions. For TFR and form 041 filers, this item also includes loans to finance agricultural production and other loans farmers. Available as of March 2011

name: LREAG
label: Real estate loans secured by farmland
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Carrying amount of loans secured by farmland in domestic offices that are covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Available as of March 2011

name: LREAGR
label: Real estate loans secured by farmland
definition: ( YTD, % ) Carrying amount of loans secured by farmland in domestic offices that are covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Available as of March 2011

name: LRECONS
label: Real estate loans secured by construction, land development, and other land loans
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total carrying amount of construction, land development and other land loans secured by real estate in domestic offices that are covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. available as of March 2011

name: LRECONSR
label: Real estate loans secured by construction, land development, and other land loans
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total carrying amount of construction, land development and other land loans secured by real estate in domestic offices that are covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. available as of March 2011

name: LREMULT
label: Real estate loans secured by multifamily (5 or more) residential properties
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Carrying amount of multifamily (5 or more) residential properties held in domestic offices that are covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Available as of March 2011.

name: LREMULTR
label: Real estate loans secured by multifamily (5 or more) residential properties
definition: ( YTD, % ) Carrying amount of multifamily (5 or more) residential properties held in domestic offices that are covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Available as of March 2011.

name: LRENRES
label: Real estate loans secured by nonfarm nonresidential properties
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total carrying amount of loans secured by nonfarm nonresidential properties in domestic offices that are covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Available as of March 2011.

name: LRENRESR
label: Real estate loans secured by nonfarm nonresidential properties
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total carrying amount of loans secured by nonfarm nonresidential properties in domestic offices that are covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Available as of March 2011.

name: LRERES
label: Real estate loans secured by 1–4 family residential properties:
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total carrying amount of loans secured by 1-4 family residential properties in domestic offices that are covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Available as of March 2011

name: LRERESR
label: Real estate loans secured by 1–4 family residential properties:
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total carrying amount of loans secured by 1-4 family residential properties in domestic offices that are covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Available as of March 2011

name: LS
label: Lease Financing Receivables
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Lease financing receivables, net of unearned income.

name: LSALNLS
label: Carrying amount of covered of Loans and leases
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The carrying amount of the loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Available as of March 2010.

name: LSALNLSR
label: Carrying amount of covered of Loans and leases
definition: ( YTD, % ) The carrying amount of the loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Available as of March 2010.

name: LSAOA
label: Carrying amount of other assets
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The carrying amount of other assets covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Available as of March 2010.

name: LSAOAR
label: Carrying amount of other assets
definition: ( YTD, % ) The carrying amount of other assets covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Available as of March 2010.

name: LSAORE
label: Carrying amount of other real estate owned
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The carrying amount of Other Real Estate Owned covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Available as of March 2010.

name: LSAORER
label: Carrying amount of other real estate owned
definition: ( YTD, % ) The carrying amount of Other Real Estate Owned covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Available as of March 2010.

name: LSASCDBT
label: Carrying amount of debt securities
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The carrying amount of debt securities covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Available as of March 2010.

name: LSASCDBTR
label: Carrying amount of debt securities
definition: ( YTD, % ) The carrying amount of debt securities covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Available as of March 2010.

name: LSFOR
label: Lease Financing Receivables
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Lease financing receivables, net of unearned income, held in foreign offices.

name: LSFORR
label: Lease Financing Receivables
definition: ( YTD, % ) Lease financing receivables, net of unearned income, held in foreign offices.

name: LSNUS
label: Leases of Non-U.S. Addressees
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Lease financing receivables of non-U.S. addressees, net of unearned income.

name: LSR
label: Lease Financing Receivables
definition: ( YTD, % ) Lease financing receivables, net of unearned income.

name: MADDR
label: Mailing Street Address
definition: The street address portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MADDR2
label: Mailing Street Address Line 2
definition: The street address line 2 portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MAILING_ADDRESS
label: Mailing Address
definition: The mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MAILING_ADDRESS2
label: Mailing Address Line 2
definition: The mailing address line 2 of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MAILING_CITY
label: Mailing City
definition: The city portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MAILING_STALP
label: Mailing State Alpha Code
definition: The 2-digit state code portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MAILING_STATE
label: Mailing State
definition: The state portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MAILING_ZIP
label: Mailing Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MAILING_ZIP5_RAW
label: Mailing 5-Digit Zip Code
definition: The 5-digit postal code portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MAILING_ZIPREST
label: Mailing Zip Code Extension
definition: The 4-digit zip code extension portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MAINOFF
label: Main Office
definition: The main office for the institution.

name: MCITY
label: Mailing City
definition: The city portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MDI_STATUS_CODE
label: Minority Status Code
definition: A numeric flag used to indicate whether an institution is primarily a minority owned institution.

name: MDI_STATUS_DESC
label: Minority Status Description
definition: A descriptive flag used to indicate type of minority owned institution.

name: METROBR
label: METROBR
definition: An indicator for an area that contains a core urban area of 50,000 or more population

name: MICROBR
label: MICROBR
definition: An indicator for an area contains an urban core of at least 10,000 (but less than 50,000) population.

name: MMLFBAL
label: Outstanding balance of assets purchased under the Money Market Mutual Fund Liquidity Facility (MMLF).
definition: ( YTD, $ ) aggregate amount on a consolidated basis of the assets purchased under the Money Market Mutual Fund Liquidity Facility (MMLF) as reported by category on the balance sheet (FFIEC Call Schedule RC). The possible asset categories include Interest bearing balances due from depository institutions, Held-to-maturity securities, Available-for-sale securities, Trading assets, and Other assets. to prevent the disruption in the money markets from destabilizing the financial system, the board of governors of the federal reserve system authorized the federal reserve bank of Boston on March 19, 2020, to establish the money market mutual fund liquidity facility (MMLF) pursuant to section 13(3) of the federal reserve act (12 u.s.c. 343(3)). under the MMLF, the federal reserve bank of Boston will extend nonrecourse loans to eligible borrowers to purchase eligible assets from money market mutual funds, which will be posted as collateral to the Federal Reserve Bank of Boston. Data begins on June 30, 2020.

name: MMLFBALR
label: Outstanding balance of assets purchased under the Money Market Mutual Fund Liquidity Facility (MMLF).
definition: ( YTD, % ) The aggregate amount on a consolidated basis of the assets purchased under the Money Market Mutual Fund Liquidity Facility (MMLF) as reported by category on the balance sheet (FFIEC Call Schedule RC). The possible asset categories include Interest bearing balances due from depository institutions, Held-to-maturity securities, Available-for-sale securities, Trading assets, and Other assets. to prevent the disruption in the money markets from destabilizing the financial system, the board of governors of the federal reserve system authorized the federal reserve bank of Boston on March 19, 2020, to establish the money market mutual fund liquidity facility (mmlf) pursuant to section 13(3) of the federal reserve act (12 u.s.c. 343(3)). under the mmlf, the federal reserve bank of Boston will extend nonrecourse loans to eligible borrowers to purchase eligible assets from money market mutual funds, which will be posted as collateral to the Federal Reserve Bank of Boston. Data begins on June 30, 2020.

name: MOREINFO_CONSUMER
label: Consumer Assistance from Primary Federal Regulator
definition: ( Qtly, % ) A direct link to consumer assistance at the primary federal regulator of the intuition. The primary federal regulator is responsible for administering many of the federal laws that protect consumers in their dealings with financial institutions.

name: MSA
label: Metropolitan Statistical Area (MSA)
definition: The Metropolitan Statistical Areas based on Census Bureau data, as defined by the US Office of Management (OMB) prior to the year 2000.

name: MSABR
label: MSABR
definition: The Metropolitan Statistical Areas (MSABR) are based on the 2020 Census. These areas correspond to the state/county/CBSA relationships as defined by the Census Bureau. For the purpose of the Summary of Deposits, MSAs reflect only the CBSA codes for metropolitan areas (use MSABR for the Summary of Deposits).

name: MSANAMB
label: MSANAMB
definition: The Metropolitan Statistical Area name in which the branch is physically located.  The Metropolitan Statistical Areas (MSABR) are based on the 2020 Census. These areas correspond to the state/county/CBSA relationships as defined by the Census Bureau. For the purpose of the Summary of Deposits, MSAs reflect only the CBSA codes for metropolitan areas (use MSABR for the Summary of Deposits). The SOD Deposit Market Share Reports display the last updated MSA Name (MSANAMB) associated with the MSA number (MSABR) in all prior years for time series analysis. The data download file contains the historic record of the MSA name based delineations of the statistical areas shown in prior years. Official Metropolitan Statistical Areas information can be found by visiting the website of United State Census Bureau (https://www.census.gov/programs-surveys/metro-micro.html).

name: MSA_NO
label: Metropolitan Statistical Area Number
definition: The Metropolitan Statistical Area Number (MSA_NO) in which the institution is physically located. The Office of Management and Budget defines MSAs in terms of entire counties surrounding central cities, except in the six New England states where they are defined in terms of cities and towns within counties. Before 200 standards

name: MSRECE
label: Closed end 1-4 Family Residential Loans with recource,  Balance Serviced for Others
definition: ( YTD, $ ) 1-4 Family Residential Mortgages Serviced With Recourse Or Other Servicer-Provided Credit Enhancements. Outstanding Principal Balance Of Assets Serviced For Others 1-4 Family Residential Mortgages Serviced With Recourse Or Other Servicer-Provided Credit Enhancements.

name: MSRECER
label: Closed end 1-4 Family Residential Loans with recource,  Balance Serviced for Others
definition: ( YTD, % ) 1-4 Family Residential Mortgages Serviced With Recourse Or Other Servicer-Provided Credit Enhancements. Outstanding Principal Balance Of Assets Serviced For Others 1-4 Family Residential Mortgages Serviced With Recourse Or Other Servicer-Provided Credit Enhancements.

name: MSRESFCL
label: Serviced 1-4 Family Loans in Foreclosure
definition: ( YTD, $ ) outstanding principle balance of 1-4 family residential mortgages serviced for others that are in the process of Foreclosure at quarter end, including closed-end and open-end mortgages. the data begins in 2008. as of 2017 this information is not reported by banks filing a FFIEC Call Report 51.

name: MSRESFCLR
label: Serviced 1-4 Family Loans in Foreclosure
definition: ( YTD, % ) outstanding principle balance of 1-4 family residential mortgages serviced for others that are in the process of Foreclosure at quarter end, including closed-end and open-end mortgages. the data begins in 2008. as of 2017 this information is not reported by banks filing a FFIEC Call Report 51.

name: MSRNRECE
label: Closed end 1-4 Family Residential loans with no recource,  Balance Serviced for Others
definition: ( YTD, $ ) 1-4 Family Residential Mortgages Serviced With No Recourse Or Other Servicer-Provided Credit Enhancements. Outstanding Principal Balance Of Assets Serviced For Others 1-4 Family Residential Mortgages Serviced With No Recourse Or Other Servicer-Provided Credit Enhancements.

name: MSRNRECER
label: Closed end 1-4 Family Residential loans with no recource,  Balance Serviced for Others
definition: ( YTD, % ) 1-4 Family Residential Mortgages Serviced With No Recourse Or Other Servicer-Provided Credit Enhancements. Outstanding Principal Balance Of Assets Serviced For Others 1-4 Family Residential Mortgages Serviced With No Recourse Or Other Servicer-Provided Credit Enhancements.

name: MSTALP
label: Mailing State Alpha Code
definition: The 2-digit state code portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MSTATE
label: Mailing State
definition: The state portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MTGRES
label: Outstanding Principal Balance of Mortgages Transferred
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Outstanding principal of first lien 1-4 family residential mortgage loans transferred with recourse that have been treated as sold for Call Report purposes.

name: MTGRESRC
label: Amount of Recourse Exposure first lien 1-4 family
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Amount of recourse exposure on first lien 1-4 family residential mortgage loans transferred to others that have been sold for Call Report purposes.

name: MUTUAL
label: Ownership Type
definition: Banking institutions fall into one of two ownership types, stock or non-stock. A non-stock institution, or mutual institution, is owned and controlled solely by its depositors. A mutual does not issue capital stock. (1=Yes, a mutual) An institution which sells stock to raise capital is called a stock institution. It is owned by the shareholders who benefit from profits earned by the institution. (0=No, not a mutual.)

name: MZIP5
label: Mailing Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MZIP5_RAW
label: Mailing 5-Digit Zip Code
definition: The 5-digit postal code portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: MZIPREST
label: Mailing Zip Code Extension
definition: The 4-digit zip code extension portion of the mailing address of an institution or one of its branches.

name: NAAG
label: Loans for Agriculture and Other Loans To Farmers in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total loans to finance agricultural production and other loans to farmers that are no longer accruing interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: NAAGR
label: Loans for Agriculture and Other Loans To Farmers in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total loans to finance agricultural production and other loans to farmers that are no longer accruing interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: NAAGSM
label: Loans To Finance Agriculture and Other Loans To Farmers in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total loans to finance agricultural production and other loans to farmers that are no longer accruing interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: NAAGSMR
label: Loans To Finance Agriculture and Other Loans To Farmers in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total loans to finance agricultural production and other loans to farmers that are no longer accruing interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: NAASSET
label: Assets in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total assets, which are no longer accruing interest. Total assets include real estate loans, installment loans, credit cards and related loans, commercial and all other loans, lease financing receivables, debt securities and other assets.

name: NAAUTO
label: Nonaccrual Auto Loans
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Nonaccrual automobile loans to Individuals for household, family and other personal expenditures. Available as of March 2011

name: NAAUTOR
label: Nonaccrual Auto Loans
definition: ( YTD, % ) Nonaccrual automobile loans to Individuals for household, family and other personal expenditures. Available as of March 2011

name: NACDIR
label: Notional Amount of Credit Derivatives
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Notional amount of credit derivatives on which the institution is either the guarantor or beneficiary. Credit derivatives are off-balance sheet arrangements that allow one party (the beneficiary) to transfer the credit risk of an asset to another party (the guarantor). RIS definition: NACDIR= (CTDERGTY + CTDERBEN)

name: NACDIRR
label: Notional Amount of Credit Derivatives
definition: ( YTD, % ) Notional amount of credit derivatives on which the institution is either the guarantor or beneficiary. Credit derivatives are off-balance sheet arrangements that allow one party (the beneficiary) to transfer the credit risk of an asset to another party (the guarantor). RIS definition: NACDIR= (CTDERGTY + CTDERBEN)

name: NACI
label: Commercial and Industrial Loans in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total commercial and industrial loans that in nonaccrual status and are no longer accruing interest. Institutions with assets of less than $300 million include all other loans in this item.

name: NACINUS
label: Loans To Non-U.S. Addressees in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total construction and land development loans secured by real estate (with non-U.S. addressees) which are no longer accruing interest.

name: NACINUSR
label: Loans To Non-U.S. Addressees in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total construction and land development loans secured by real estate (with non-U.S. addressees) which are no longer accruing interest.

name: NACIR
label: Commercial and Industrial Loans in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total commercial and industrial loans that in nonaccrual status and are no longer accruing interest. Institutions with assets of less than $300 million include all other loans in this item.

name: NACON
label: Loans To Individuals in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total loans to Individuals for household, family, and other personal expenditures that in nonaccrual status and are no longer accruing interest.

name: NACONOTH
label: Nonaccrual-Other Consumer
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Nonaccrual other loans to Individuals for household, family and other personal expenditures on a consolidated basis. beginning March 2001, includes revolving credit plans other than credit cards. beginning March 2011, excludes automobile loans.

name: NACONOTHR
label: Nonaccrual-Other Consumer
definition: ( YTD, % ) Nonaccrual other loans to Individuals for household, family and other personal expenditures on a consolidated basis. beginning March 2001, includes revolving credit plans other than credit cards. beginning March 2011, excludes automobile loans.

name: NACONR
label: Loans To Individuals in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total loans to Individuals for household, family, and other personal expenditures that in nonaccrual status and are no longer accruing interest.

name: NACRCD
label: Credit Cards and Related Plans in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total credit cards loans to Individuals that are in nonaccrual status and no longer accruing interest. Prior to 2001, included revolving credit plans other than credit cards.

name: NACRCDR
label: Credit Cards and Related Plans in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total credit cards loans to Individuals that are in nonaccrual status and no longer accruing interest. Prior to 2001, included revolving credit plans other than credit cards.

name: NADEP
label: Loans To Depository Institutions in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total loans to depository institutions and acceptances of other banks, which are no longer accruing interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: NADEPNUS
label: Loans To Foreign Banks in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total loans to foreign depository institutions and acceptances of other foreign banks that are in nonaccrual status and no longer accruing interest.

name: NADEPNUSR
label: Loans To Foreign Banks in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total loans to foreign depository institutions and acceptances of other foreign banks that are in nonaccrual status and no longer accruing interest.

name: NADEPR
label: Loans To Depository Institutions in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total loans to depository institutions and acceptances of other banks, which are no longer accruing interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: NAFG
label: Loans To Foreign Governments and Official Institutions in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total loans to foreign governments and official institutions that are no longer accruing interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: NAFGR
label: Loans To Foreign Governments and Official Institutions in Nonaccrual Status
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total loans to foreign governments and official institutions that are no longer accruing interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: NAGTY
label: Nonaccrual Loans and Leases, Wholly Or Partially Guaranteed By Us Government
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The maximum amount recoverable from the U.S. Government loans and leases (excluding GNMA loans) that are guaranteed by the U.S. Government and are in nonaccrual status. The U.S. Government includes its agencies and its government-sponsored agencies, under the guarantee or insurance provisions applicable to the loans and leases. Listed here as memoranda only and is included in nonaccrual total assets. FRB Number: RFCD5617 RCON5617  Note: Prior to March 2011, included loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Prior to June 2005, included rebooked GNMA loans.

name: NAGTYGNM
label: Nonaccrual Rebooked Gnma Loans
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Nonaccrual rebooked GNMA loans that have been repurchased or are eligible for repurchase by seller-servicers of GNMA loans; and nonaccrual loans that have been purchased out of GNMA securitizations by servicers of GNMA loans that were not the transferors of the loans. GNMA loans are guaranteed by the Government National Mortgage Association commonly known as Ginnie Mae. Included in nonaccrual total assets. FRB Number: RCFDC868 or RECONC868  Note: Prior to March 2011, included loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements.

name: NAGTYGNMR
label: Nonaccrual Rebooked Gnma Loans
definition: ( YTD, % ) Nonaccrual rebooked GNMA loans that have been repurchased or are eligible for repurchase by seller-servicers of GNMA loans; and nonaccrual loans that have been purchased out of GNMA securitizations by servicers of GNMA loans that were not the transferors of the loans. GNMA loans are guaranteed by the Government National Mortgage Association commonly known as Ginnie Mae. Included in nonaccrual total assets. FRB Number: RCFDC868 or RECONC868  Note: Prior to March 2011, included loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements.

name: NAGTYPAR
label: Guaranteed Portion of Nonaccrual Loans and Leases, Excluding Gnma Loans
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Loans and leases for which repayment of principal is wholly or partially guaranteed or Insured by the U.S. Government, including its agencies and its government-sponsored agencies, that are in nonaccrual status a consolidated basis. Examples include loans guaranteed by the Small Business Administration, and the Federal Housing Administration. Excludes loans and leases guaranteed or Insured by state or local governments, state or local government agencies, foreign (non-U.S.) governments, and private agencies or organizations. Also excluded are loans and leases collateralized by securities issued by the U.S. Government, including its agencies and its government-sponsored agencies. Note: Included in nonaccrual total assets. FRB Number: RFCD5614 RCON5614 Note: Prior to March 2011, included loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements.

name: NAGTYPARR
label: Guaranteed Portion of Nonaccrual Loans and Leases, Excluding Gnma Loans
definition: ( YTD, % ) Loans and leases for which repayment of principal is wholly or partially guaranteed or Insured by the U.S. Government, including its agencies and its government-sponsored agencies, that are in nonaccrual status a consolidated basis. Examples include loans guaranteed by the Small Business Administration, and the Federal Housing Administration. Excludes loans and leases guaranteed or Insured by state or local governments, state or local government agencies, foreign (non-U.S.) governments, and private agencies or organizations. Also excluded are loans and leases collateralized by securities issued by the U.S. Government, including its agencies and its government-sponsored agencies. Note: Included in nonaccrual total assets. FRB Number: RFCD5614 RCON5614 Note: Prior to March 2011, included loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements.

name: NAGTYR
label: Nonaccrual Loans and Leases, Wholly Or Partially Guaranteed By Us Government
definition: ( YTD, % ) The maximum amount recoverable from the U.S. Government loans and leases (excluding GNMA loans) that are guaranteed by the U.S. Government and are in nonaccrual status. The U.S. Government includes its agencies and its government-sponsored agencies, under the guarantee or insurance provisions applicable to the loans and leases. Listed here as memoranda only and is included in nonaccrual total assets. FRB Number: RFCD5617 RCON5617  Note: Prior to March 2011, included loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Prior to June 2005, included rebooked GNMA loans.

name: NALAG
label: Farm loans in nonaccrual
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Carrying amount of Nonaccrual loans to finance agricultural production and other loans to farmers that are covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. reported separately for call 31 reporters only. call 41 reporters and TFR reporters include these loans in nonaccrual all other covered loans and leases days (NALOTH).Available as of March 2011.

name: NALAGR
label: Farm loans in nonaccrual
definition: ( YTD, % ) Carrying amount of Nonaccrual loans to finance agricultural production and other loans to farmers that are covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. reported separately for call 31 reporters only. call 41 reporters and TFR reporters include these loans in nonaccrual all other covered loans and leases days (NALOTH).Available as of March 2011.

name: NALCIR

definition: ( YTD, % ) Nonaccrual commercial and industrial loans that are covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Available as of March 2011

name: NALCON

name: NALCONR

definition: ( YTD, % ) Total nonaccrual consumer loans to Individuals for household, family and other personal expenditures that are covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements. Available as of March 2011

name: NALGTY
label: Total portion of covered nonaccrual loans protected by FDIC loss-sharing agreements
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The maximum amount recoverable from the FDIC under loss-sharing agreements, or total protected portion, covering the nonaccrual loans and leases. Is reported above and beyond the amount that has already been reflected in the measurement of the reporting bank’s indemnification asset, which represents the right to receive payments from the FDIC under the loss-sharing agreement. Available as of March 2011

name: NALGTYR
label: Total portion of covered nonaccrual loans protected by FDIC loss-sharing agreements
definition: ( YTD, % ) The maximum amount recoverable from the FDIC under loss-sharing agreements, or total protected portion, covering the nonaccrual loans and leases. Is reported above and beyond the amount that has already been reflected in the measurement of the reporting bank’s indemnification asset, which represents the right to receive payments from the FDIC under the loss-sharing agreement. Available as of March 2011

name: NALNLS

definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total loans and lease financing which are no longer accruing interest. Total loans include real estate loans, loans to depository institutions and acceptances of other banks, loans to finance agricultural production and other loans to farmers, commercial and industrial loans, loans to Individuals for household, family and other personal expenditures, loans to foreign governments and official institutions, and all other loans.

name: NALNSALER

name: NAME
label: Institution name
definition:
The legal title or name of the institution._x000D_
Note: If you are having trouble finding an FDIC-Insured Institution: Type only part of the name. By typing a part of the name -- for example, type NET instead of NET.B@NK -- you can list all institutions that have net anywhere in their name. Then, you can look at the list and select the bank or thrift you are seeking.

name: NAMEBR
label: NAMEBR
definition: Branch name.

name: NAMEFULL
label: Institution Name at Time of Reporting
definition: Institution Name at Time of Reporting

name: NAMEHCD
label: Holding Company (Regulatory Direct Owner)
definition: A holding company (regulatory direct owner) is any institution that: directly or indirectly owns, controls or has power to vote 25 percent or more of a bank's shares or controls in any manner the election of a majority of the directors or trustees of a bank or exercises a controlling influence over the management or policies of a bank. Bank holding companies are regulated by the Federal Reserve Board. Information on Thrift Holding Companies that own Savings Associations but do not own banks is not currently available in the ID System. Source: Federal Reserve Board National Information Center data base.

name: NAMEHCR
label: Name of Bank Holding Company (Regulatory Top Holder)
definition:
Regulatory top holder is assigned by the Federal Reserve Board based on ownership and control percentages. Note: Information on bank holding companies is only as of quarter-end. Regulatory top holder is any company that directly or indirectly owns, controls or has power to vote 25 percent or more of a bank's or direct holding company's shares or controls in any manner the election of a majority of the directors or trustees of a bank or direct holding company or exercises a controlling influence over the management or policies of a bank or direct holding company. Information on Thrift Holding Companies that own Savings Associations but do not own banks is not currently available in the ID System. Source: Federal Reserve Board National Information Center data base._x000D_
Note: Information on bank holding companies is only as of quarter-end. Regulatory top holder is any company that directly or indirectly owns, controls or has power to vote 25 percent or more of a bank's or direct holding company's shares or controls in any manner the election of a majority of the directors or trustees of a bank or direct holding company or exercises a controlling influence over the management or policies of a bank or direct holding company. Information on Thrift Holding Companies that own Savings Associations but do not own banks is not currently available in the ID System. Source: Federal Reserve Board National Information Center data base.

name: NCGTYPAR

definition: ( Qtly, % ) Loans and leases which are wholly or partially guaranteed by the US government past due 90 days or more and nonaccrual on a consolidated basis. Listed as memoranda only and is included in total noncurrent total assets. Important note: As of March 2011, no longer includes loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements.

name: NCLNLS

name: NCREMULR

name: NCRENRER

name: NECNAMB
label: NECNAMB

name: NECTABR
label: NECTABR
definition: Code of the New England City Town Areas based on the PLACENUM.

name: NETGNAST
label: Net Gains (Losses) on Sales of Other Assets (Excluding Securities)
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Reported net gains or (losses) on sales or other disposal of other assets (e.g. premises and fixed assets, personal property acquired for debts previously contracted (such as automobiles, boats, equipment, and appliances); and coins, art, and other similar assets. Prior to March 2001 this was reported as a memoranda item only and was included in all other noninterest income. Also, prior to March 2001, gains and losses on sale of premises and fixed assets were reported as either noninterest income or noninterest expense.

name: NETGNASTR
label: Net Gains (Losses) on Sales of Other Assets (Excluding Securities)
definition: ( YTD, % ) Reported net gains or (losses) on sales or other disposal of other assets (e.g. premises and fixed assets, personal property acquired for debts previously contracted (such as automobiles, boats, equipment, and appliances); and coins, art, and other similar assets. Prior to March 2001 this was reported as a memoranda item only and was included in all other noninterest income. Also, prior to March 2001, gains and losses on sale of premises and fixed assets were reported as either noninterest income or noninterest expense.

name: NETGNSLN
label: Net Gains (Losses) on Sales of Loans
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Reported net gains or (losses) on sales or other disposal of loans and leases. This includes unrealized losses on loans and leases held for sale. Prior to March 2001 this was reported as a memoranda item only and was included in all other noninterest income. Also prior to March 2001 gains or losses on the sale of loans were reported as either noninterest income or noninterest expense.

name: NETGNSLNR
label: Net Gains (Losses) on Sales of Loans
definition: ( YTD, % ) Reported net gains or (losses) on sales or other disposal of loans and leases. This includes unrealized losses on loans and leases held for sale. Prior to March 2001 this was reported as a memoranda item only and was included in all other noninterest income. Also prior to March 2001 gains or losses on the sale of loans were reported as either noninterest income or noninterest expense.

name: NETGNSRE
label: Net Gains (Losses) on Sales of Other Real Estate Owned
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Reported net gains or (losses) on the sales or other disposals of other real estate owned, increases and decreases in the valuation allowance for already foreclosed real estate, and write-downs charged to expense.

name: NETGNSRER
label: Net Gains (Losses) on Sales of Other Real Estate Owned
definition: ( YTD, % ) Reported net gains or (losses) on the sales or other disposals of other real estate owned, increases and decreases in the valuation allowance for already foreclosed real estate, and write-downs charged to expense.

name: NETIMIN
label: Net Income Attributable to Noncontrolling Interests
definition: ( YTD, $ ) NET income (loss) attributable to noncontrolling (minority) interests on a consolidated basis. note: available starting March 31, 2009.

name: NETIMINQ
label: Net Income Attributable to Noncontrolling Interests
definition: ( Qtly, $ ) NET income (loss) attributable to noncontrolling (minority) interests on a consolidated basis. note: available starting March 31, 2009.

name: NETIMINQR
label: Net Income Attributable to Noncontrolling Interests
definition: ( Qtly, % ) NET income (loss) attributable to noncontrolling (minority) interests on a consolidated basis. note: available starting March 31, 2009.

name: NETIMINR
label: Net Income Attributable to Noncontrolling Interests
definition: ( YTD, % ) NET income (loss) attributable to noncontrolling (minority) interests on a consolidated basis. note: available starting March 31, 2009.

name: NETINBM

definition: ( YTD, $ ) NET income (loss) attributable to the bank and noncontrolling (minority) interests on a consolidated basis. note: available beginning March 31, 2009.

name: NETINBMQ

name: NETINC

name: NETINCF

name: NETINCQ

name: NETINCR

name: NEWCERT

name: NFAA

definition: ( YTD, $ and % ) Total loans secured by 1-4 family residential properties that are no longer accruing interest held in domestic offices of the institution.

name: NIM
label: Net interest income
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total interest income minus total interest expense. It represents the difference between interest and dividends earned on interest-bearing assets and interest paid to depositors and other creditors.

name: NIMADNT
label: Net Interest Income Adjustments
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Net booking location adjustments for international operations (included in net interest income).

name: NIMF
label: Net Interest Income - Foreign
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total interest income allocable to international operations minus total interest expense allocable to international operations. It represents the difference between interest and dividends earned on interest-bearing assets and interest paid to depositors and other creditors.

name: NIMQ
label: Net interest income
definition: ( Qtly, $ ) Total interest income minus total interest expense. It represents the difference between interest and dividends earned on interest-bearing assets and interest paid to depositors and other creditors.

name: NIMQR
label: Net interest income
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total interest income minus total interest expense. It represents the difference between interest and dividends earned on interest-bearing assets and interest paid to depositors and other creditors.

name: NIMR
label: Net interest income
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total interest income minus total interest expense. It represents the difference between interest and dividends earned on interest-bearing assets and interest paid to depositors and other creditors.

name: NIMY
label: Net Interest Margin
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total interest income less total interest expense (annualized) as a percent of average earning assets .

name: NIMYQ
label: Net Interest Margin
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total interest income less total interest expense (annualized) as a percent of average earning assets .

name: NOIJ
label: Net Operating Income
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Net operating income adjusted on a consolidated basis includes: Income (loss) before discontinued operations, minus: gains (losses) on securities not held in trading accounts adjusted for applicable income taxes. Beginning in the 2018 reporting year, includes unrealized gains (losses) on equity securities for those that adopted ASU 2016-01 in the adjustment for securities gains (losses) This item also includes realized gains (losses) on equity securities. Prior to March 2016 defined as income (loss) before extraordinary items and other adjustments.

name: NOIJQ
label: Net Operating Income
definition: ( Qtly, $ ) Net operating income adjusted on a consolidated basis includes: Income (loss) before discontinued operations, minus: gains (losses) on securities not held in trading accounts adjusted for applicable income taxes. Beginning in the 2018 reporting year, includes unrealized gains (losses) on equity securities for those that adopted ASU 2016-01 in the adjustment for securities gains (losses) This item also includes realized gains (losses) on equity securities. Prior to March 2016 defined as income (loss) before extraordinary items and other adjustments.

name: NOIJQR
label: Net Operating Income
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Net operating income adjusted on a consolidated basis includes: Income (loss) before discontinued operations, minus: gains (losses) on securities not held in trading accounts adjusted for applicable income taxes. Prior to March 2016 defined as income (loss) before extraordinary items and other adjustments.

name: NOIJR

name: NOIJYQ

name: NONII

name: NONIIAYQ

name: NONIIY

name: NONIXAYQ

name: NONIXQR

definition: ( YTD and Qtly, % ) Noncurrent assets as a percent of total assets. Noncurrent assets are defined as assets that are past due 90 days or more plus assets placed in nonaccrual status plus other real estate owned (excluding direct and indirect investments in real estate).

name: NTGLFXAQ
label: Net Gains (Losses) on Sales of Other Assets (Excluding Securities)
definition: ( Qtly, $ ) Reported net gains or (losses) on sales or other disposal of other assets (e.g. premises and fixed assets, personal property acquired for debts previously contracted (such as automobiles, boats, equipment, and appliances); and coins, art, and other similar assets. Prior to March 2001 this was reported as a memoranda item only and was included in all other noninterest income. Also, prior to March 2001, gains and losses on sale of premises and fixed assets were reported as either noninterest income or noninterest expense.

name: NTGLFXAQR
label: Net Gains (Losses) on Sales of Other Assets (Excluding Securities)
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Reported net gains or (losses) on sales or other disposal of other assets (e.g. premises and fixed assets, personal property acquired for debts previously contracted (such as automobiles, boats, equipment, and appliances); and coins, art, and other similar assets. Prior to March 2001 this was reported as a memoranda item only and was included in all other noninterest income. Also, prior to March 2001, gains and losses on sale of premises and fixed assets were reported as either noninterest income or noninterest expense.

name: NTGLLNQ
label: Net Gains (Losses) on Sales of Loans
definition: ( Qtly, $ ) Reported net gains or (losses) on sales or other disposal of loans and leases. This includes unrealized losses on loans and leases held for sale. Prior to March 2001 this was reported as a memoranda item only and was included in all other noninterest income. Also prior to March 2001 gains or losses on the sale of loans were reported as either noninterest income or noninterest expense.

name: NTGLLNQR
label: Net Gains (Losses) on Sales of Loans

Prior to March 2001 this was reported as a memoranda item only and was included in all other noninterest income. Also prior to March 2001 gains or losses on the sale of loans were reported as either noninterest income or noninterest expense.

name: NTGLREQR

name: NTINCHPP

name: NTOTHERR
label: Other Loans
definition: ( YTD, % ) NET amount of all other loans that have been charged-off and debited to the allowance for loan and lease losses established by the institution. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: NTOTHQ

definition: ( Qtly, $ ) NET amount of all other loans that have been charged-off and debited to the allowance for loan and lease losses established by the institution. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

definition: ( Qtly, % ) NET amount of all other loans that have been charged-off and debited to the allowance for loan and lease losses established by the institution. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: NTR
label: Domestic nontransaction accounts
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The sum of the following accounts held in domestic offices: money market deposit accounts (MMDAs), other savings deposits and all time deposits. prior to 2004, This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: NTRCDSM
label: Amount ($) - Time Deposits Less Than $100,000
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total time deposits, both certificates of deposits and time open-accounts, under $100,000 held in domestic offices. note: although standard FDIC insurance coverage was temporarily raised from $100,000 to $250,000 in October 2008, institutions are required to report this item based on the $100,000 coverage limit through December 2009. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: NTRCDSMJ

definition: ( Qtly, % ) Time deposits less than or equal to the standard insurance limit held in domestic offices. available as of March 2010, this item is reported based on the temporary increase to the standard insurance limit of $250,000 which lagged behind the initial adoption of the temporary increase that became effective October 3, 2008.

name: NTRCDSMR

definition: ( YTD, % ) Total debt securities and other assets (excludes other real estate owned and other repossessed assets) which are no longer accruing interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: NTRCOMB

name: NTRCOMOT

name: NTRCOMOTR

name: NTRECOSR

definition: ( Qtly, $ ) The net amount of charged-off loans secured by real estate and held in foreign offices of the institution.

name: NTREFORR

name: NTRELNR

name: NTRFC

name: NTRFCFG

definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total nontransaction accounts of banks in foreign countries, foreign government and official institutions (includes foreign central banks) held in domestic offices.

name: NTRFCFGR

definition: ( YTD, % ) Total nontransaction accounts of banks in foreign countries, foreign government and official institutions (includes foreign central banks) held in domestic offices.

name: NTRFG

definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total nontransaction accounts of foreign governments and official institutions (includes foreign central banks) held in domestic offices. This item is not reported by institutions with less than $100 million in total assets.

name: NTRIPC
label: Individuals, Partnerships, and Corporations
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total nontransaction accounts of Individuals, partnerships and corporations (including certified and official checks) held in domestic offices. This item is not available for TFR Reporters with assets less than $300 million and risk-based capital ratios in excess of 12 percent. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: NTRIPCR
label: Individuals, Partnerships, and Corporations
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total nontransaction accounts of Individuals, partnerships and corporations (including certified and official checks) held in domestic offices. This item is not available for TFR Reporters with assets less than $300 million and risk-based capital ratios in excess of 12 percent. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: NTRMMEDR
label: Amount of Time Deposits of $250,000 or less (or $100,000 through December 2009)
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total time deposits, both certificates of deposits and time open-accounts, $100,000 to $250,000 held in domestic offices. NOTE: Although standard FDIC insurance coverage was temporarily raised from $100,000 to $250,000 in October 2008, institutions were required to report this item based on the $100,000 coverage limit through December 2009

name: NTRMUNI
label: States and Political Subdivisions in The U.S.
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total nontransaction accounts of states, counties, municipalities, and local housing authorities (or political subdivisions) held in domestic offices. Included are deposits of funds advanced to states and political subdivisions by the U.S. government and corporations, and deposits of withheld income taxes of states and political subdivisions.

name: NTRMUNIR
label: States and Political Subdivisions in The U.S.
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total nontransaction accounts of states, counties, municipalities, and local housing authorities (or political subdivisions) held in domestic offices. Included are deposits of funds advanced to states and political subdivisions by the U.S. government and corporations, and deposits of withheld income taxes of states and political subdivisions.

name: NTRR
label: Domestic nontransaction accounts
definition: ( YTD, % ) The sum of the following accounts held in domestic offices: money market deposit accounts (MMDAs), other savings deposits and all time deposits. prior to 2004, This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: NTRSMMDA
label: Money Market Deposit Accounts (MMDAS)
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total money market deposit accounts held in domestic offices. Prior to 2004, this item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: NTRSMMDAR
label: Money Market Deposit Accounts (MMDAS)
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total money market deposit accounts held in domestic offices. Prior to 2004, this item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: NTRSOTH
label: Other Savings Deposits (Excluding MMDAS)
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total savings deposits held in domestic offices, aside from money market deposit accounts. prior to 2004, This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: NTRSOTHR
label: Other Savings Deposits (Excluding MMDAS)
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total savings deposits held in domestic offices, aside from money market deposit accounts. prior to 2004, This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: NTRTIME
label: Total Time Deposits
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total nontransaction time deposits held in domestic offices. Prior to 2004, this item is not available for TFR reporters with assets less than $300 million and risk-based capital ratios in excess of 12 percent.

name: NTRTMLG
label: Amount ($) - Time Deposits of $100,000 Or More
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Time certificates of deposit and open-account time deposits with balances of $100,000 or more held in domestic offices. Listed as memoranda only and is included in total non-transaction accounts.   NOTE: Although standard FDIC insurance coverage was temporarily raised from $100,000 to $250,000 in October 2008, institutions are required to report this item based on the $100,000 coverage limit through December 2009 For TFR Reporters :   - Prior to December 2006, includes all deposits (not just time deposits) with balances greater than $100,000.   - In December 2006, includes all non-retirement deposits with balances greater than $100,000 and all retirement deposit accounts with balances greater than $250,000.   - Beginning in 2007, this item only includes time deposits with balances greater than $100,000.

name: NTRTMLGJ
label: Time Deposits Over The insurance Limit of $250,000
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Time deposits over the standard $250,000 insurance limit held in domestic offices. available as of March 2010, this item is reported based on the temporary increase to the standard insurance limit of $250,000 which lagged behind the initial adoption of the temporary increase that became effective October 3, 2008. Not available for Insured U.S. branches of a foreign chartered institutions (IBA)

name: NTRTMLGJR
label: Time Deposits Over The insurance Limit of $250,000
definition: ( YTD, % ) Time deposits over the standard $250,000 insurance limit held in domestic offices. available as of March 2010, this item is reported based on the temporary increase to the standard insurance limit of $250,000 which lagged behind the initial adoption of the temporary increase that became effective October 3, 2008. Not available for Insured U.S. branches of a foreign chartered institutions (IBA)

name: NTRTMMED
label: Amount ($) - Time Deposits $100,000 To $250,000
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total time deposits, both certificates of deposits and time open-accounts, $100,000 to $250,000 held in domestic offices. note: although standard FDIC insurance coverage was temporarily raised from $100,000 to $250,000 in October 2008, institutions are required to report this item based on the $100,000 coverage limit through December 2009 This item is not available for TFR Reporters, which ended in Dec. 2011.The last TFR Reports were filed December 2011.

name: NTRUSDEP
label: Other Depository Institutions in U.S.
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total nontransaction accounts of other depository institutions in the U.S., other than commercial banks, held in domestic offices.

name: NTRUSGOV
label: U.S. Government
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total nontransaction accounts of federal public funds made by or for the account of the U.S. government which are held in domestic offices.

name: NTRUSGOVR
label: U.S. Government
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total nontransaction accounts of federal public funds made by or for the account of the U.S. government which are held in domestic offices.

name: NUMEMP
label: Total Employees (full-time equivalent)
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The number of full-time employees on the payroll of the bank and its subsidiaries at the end of the quarter.

name: OAIENC
label: Income Earned, Not Collected on Loans
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Loan income earned but not collected which is also included in other assets. For TFR Reporters, includes accrued interest receivables on securities from March 1984 through December 1989.

name: OAKAR
label: Oakar Institutions
definition: ( Qtly, % ) A member of one insurance fund that acquired deposits insured by the other fund, where that portion of the buyer's deposits remained insured by, and assessable by, the other fund.

name: OALIFGEN
label: General Account Life insurance Assets
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The amount of the bank’s holdings of life insurance assets associated with general account insurance policies. In a general account life insurance policy, the general assets of the insurance company issuing the policy support the policy’s cash surrender value. also included is the portion of the carrying value of: (1) separate account policies that represents general account claims on the insurance company, such as realizable deferred acquisition costs and mortality reserves; and (2) hybrid account policies that represents general account claims on the insurance company, such as any shortfall in the value of the separate account assets supporting the cash surrender value of the policies.

name: OALIFGENR
label: General Account Life insurance Assets
definition: ( YTD, % ) The amount of the bank’s holdings of life insurance assets associated with general account insurance policies. In a general account life insurance policy, the general assets of the insurance company issuing the policy support the policy’s cash surrender value. also included is the portion of the carrying value of: (1) separate account policies that represents general account claims on the insurance company, such as realizable deferred acquisition costs and mortality reserves; and (2) hybrid account policies that represents general account claims on the insurance company, such as any shortfall in the value of the separate account assets supporting the cash surrender value of the policies.

name: OALIFHYB
label: Hybrid Life insurance Assets
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The amount of the bank’s holdings of life insurance assets associated with hybrid account insurance policies. A hybrid account insurance policy combines features of both general and separate account insurance products. Similar to a general account life insurance policy, a hybrid policy offers a guaranteed minimum crediting rate, does not carry market value risk, and does not require stable value protection. However, like a separate account life insurance policy, a hybrid policy’s cash surrender value is supported by assets segregated from the general assets of the insurance carrier. Because they are legally segregated, the assets of each separate account are not subject to claims on the insurer that arise out of any other business of the insurance company. Additionally, the bank holding the hybrid account life insurance policy is able to select the investment strategy in which the insurance premiums are invested. Under such an arrangement, the policyholder neither owns the underlying separate account created by the insurance carrier on its behalf nor controls investment. data available as of March 2011 for call reports only, not available for TFR filers.

name: OALIFHYBR
label: Hybrid Life insurance Assets
definition: ( YTD, % ) The amount of the bank’s holdings of life insurance assets associated with hybrid account insurance policies. A hybrid account insurance policy combines features of both general and separate account insurance products. Similar to a general account life insurance policy, a hybrid policy offers a guaranteed minimum crediting rate, does not carry market value risk, and does not require stable value protection. However, like a separate account life insurance policy, a hybrid policy’s cash surrender value is supported by assets segregated from the general assets of the insurance carrier. Because they are legally segregated, the assets of each separate account are not subject to claims on the insurer that arise out of any other business of the insurance company. Additionally, the bank holding the hybrid account life insurance policy is able to select the investment strategy in which the insurance premiums are invested. Under such an arrangement, the policyholder neither owns the underlying separate account created by the insurance carrier on its behalf nor controls investment. data available as of March 2011 for call reports only, not available for TFR filers.

name: OALIFINS
label: Life insurance Assets
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The amount of the bank’s holdings of life insurance assets. Include the cash surrender value of life insurance reported by the insurance carrier, less any applicable surrender charges not reflected by the carrier in this reported value, on all forms of permanent life insurance policies owned by the bank, its consolidated subsidiaries, and grantor (rabbi) trusts established by the bank or its consolidated subsidiaries, regardless of the purposes for acquiring the insurance and regardless of whether the insurance is a general account obligation of the insurer or a separate account obligation of the insurer. also included are the bank’s interest in insurance policies under split-dollar life insurance arrangements with directors, officers, and employees under both the endorsement and collateral assignment methods.

name: OALIFINSR
label: Life insurance Assets
definition: ( YTD, % ) The amount of the bank’s holdings of life insurance assets. Include the cash surrender value of life insurance reported by the insurance carrier, less any applicable surrender charges not reflected by the carrier in this reported value, on all forms of permanent life insurance policies owned by the bank, its consolidated subsidiaries, and grantor (rabbi) trusts established by the bank or its consolidated subsidiaries, regardless of the purposes for acquiring the insurance and regardless of whether the insurance is a general account obligation of the insurer or a separate account obligation of the insurer. also included are the bank’s interest in insurance policies under split-dollar life insurance arrangements with directors, officers, and employees under both the endorsement and collateral assignment methods.

name: OALIFSEP
label: Separate Account Life insurance Assets
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The amount of the bank’s holdings of life insurance assets associated with separate account insurance policies. In a separate account policy, the policy’s cash surrender value is supported by assets segregated from the general assets of the insurance carrier. Under such an arrangement, the policyholder neither owns the underlying separate account created by the insurance carrier on its behalf nor controls investment decisions in the underlying account, but does assume all investment and price risk. separate accounts are employed by life insurers to meet specific investment objectives of policyholders. the accounts are often maintained as separate accounting and reporting entities for pension plans as well as fixed benefit, variable annuity, and other products. investment income and investment gains and losses generally accrue directly to such policyholders and are not accounted for on the general accounts of the insurer. on the books of the insurer, the carrying values of separate account assets and liabilities usually approximate each other with little associated capital. Because they are legally segregated, the assets of each separate account are not subject to claims on the insurer that arise out of any other business of the insurance company. Data available as of March 2011 for Call Reports only, not available for TFR filers.

name: OALIFSEPR
label: Separate Account Life insurance Assets
definition: ( YTD, % ) The amount of the bank’s holdings of life insurance assets associated with separate account insurance policies. In a separate account policy, the policy’s cash surrender value is supported by assets segregated from the general assets of the insurance carrier. Under such an arrangement, the policyholder neither owns the underlying separate account created by the insurance carrier on its behalf nor controls investment decisions in the underlying account, but does assume all investment and price risk. separate accounts are employed by life insurers to meet specific investment objectives of policyholders. the accounts are often maintained as separate accounting and reporting entities for pension plans as well as fixed benefit, variable annuity, and other products. investment income and investment gains and losses generally accrue directly to such policyholders and are not accounted for on the general accounts of the insurer. on the books of the insurer, the carrying values of separate account assets and liabilities usually approximate each other with little associated capital. Because they are legally segregated, the assets of each separate account are not subject to claims on the insurer that arise out of any other business of the insurance company. Data available as of March 2011 for Call Reports only, not available for TFR filers.

name: OBSDIR
label: Derivatives
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Represents the sum of the following: interest-rate contracts (as defined as the notional value of interest-rate swap, futures, forward and option contracts), foreign-exchange-rate contracts, commodity contracts and equity contracts (defined similarly to interest-rate contracts). Futures and forward contracts are contracts in which the buyer agrees to purchase and the seller agrees to sell, at a specified future date, a specific quantity of underlying at a specified price or yield. These contracts exist for a variety of underlying's, including traditional agricultural or physical commodities, as well as currencies and interest rates. Futures contracts are standardized and are traded on organized exchanges which set limits on counterparty credit exposure. Forward contracts do not have standardized terms and are traded over the counter. Option contracts are contracts in which the buyer acquires the right to buy from or sell to another party some specified amount of underlying at a stated price (strike price) during a period or on a specified future date, in return for compensation (such as a fee or premium). The seller is obligated to purchase or sell the underlying at the discretion of the buyer of the contract. Swaps are obligations between two parties to exchange a series of cash flows at periodic intervals (settlement dates) for a specified period. The cash flows of a swap are either fixed or determined for each settlement date by multiplying the quantity of the underlying instrument (notional principal) by specified reference rates or prices. Except for currency swaps, the notional principal is used to calculate each payment but is not exchanged. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

label: Derivatives
definition: ( YTD, % ) Represents the sum of the following: interest-rate contracts (as defined as the notional value of interest-rate swap, futures, forward and option contracts), foreign-exchange-rate contracts, commodity contracts and equity contracts (defined similarly to interest-rate contracts). Futures and forward contracts are contracts in which the buyer agrees to purchase and the seller agrees to sell, at a specified future date, a specific quantity of underlying at a specified price or yield. These contracts exist for a variety of underlying's, including traditional agricultural or physical commodities, as well as currencies and interest rates. Futures contracts are standardized and are traded on organized exchanges which set limits on counterparty credit exposure. Forward contracts do not have standardized terms and are traded over the counter. Option contracts are contracts in which the buyer acquires the right to buy from or sell to another party some specified amount of underlying at a stated price (strike price) during a period or on a specified future date, in return for compensation (such as a fee or premium). The seller is obligated to purchase or sell the underlying at the discretion of the buyer of the contract. Swaps are obligations between two parties to exchange a series of cash flows at periodic intervals (settlement dates) for a specified period. The cash flows of a swap are either fixed or determined for each settlement date by multiplying the quantity of the underlying instrument (notional principal) by specified reference rates or prices. Except for currency swaps, the notional principal is used to calculate each payment but is not exchanged. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: OCCDIST
label: Office of the Comptroller
definition: The Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) District in which the institution is physically located. The six OCC Districts and their respective states are: Northeast - Connecticut, Delaware, District of Columbia, Maine, Maryland, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Puerto Rico, Rhode Island, Vermont, U.S. Virgin Islands Southeast - Alabama, Florida, Georgia, Mississippi, North Carolina, South Carolina, Tennessee, Virginia, West Virginia Central - Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio, Wisconsin Midwest - Iowa, Kansas, Minnesota, Missouri, Nebraska, North Dakota, South Dakota Southwest - Arkansas, Louisiana, New Mexico, Oklahoma, Texas West - Alaska, American Samoa, Arizona, California, Colorado, Guam, Hawaii, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, Oregon, States of Micronesia, Utah, Washington, Wyoming

name: OCC_CHARTNUM

definition: ( Qtly, % ) A unique identification number assigned to the bank by the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) in order to identify and track. The OCC charters, regulates, and supervises all national banks and federal savings associations. It also supervises the Insured Branches and Agencies (IBA's) of foreign banks.

name: OFFDOM
label: Number of Domestic Offices
definition: The number of domestic offices (including headquarters) operated by active institutions in the 50 states of the U.S.A.

name: OFFFOR
label: Number of Foreign Offices
definition: The number of foreign offices (outside the U.S. and U.S.territories) operated by active FDIC Insured institutions.

name: OFFICES
label: Office
definition: ( Qtly, % ) A branch/office is any location, or facility, of a financial institution, including its main office, where deposit accounts are opened, deposits are accepted, checks paid, and loans granted. Some branches include, but are not limited to, brick and mortar locations, detached or attached drive-in facilities, seasonal offices, offices on military bases or government installations, paying/receiving stations or units, nondeposit offices, Internet and Phone Banking locations where a customer can open accounts, make deposits and borrow money. A branch does not include Automated Teller Machines (ATM), Consumer Credit Offices, Contractual Offices, Customer Bank Communication Terminals (CBCT), Electronic Fund Transfer Units (EFTU), and Loan Production Offices Summary of Deposits information is required for each insured office located in any State, the District of Columbia, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico or any U.S. territory or possession such as Guam or the U.S. Virgin Islands, without regard to the location of the main office.

name: OFFOA

name: OFF_CNTYNUM

Limited service offices offer some, but not all of these services. 11 = Full service brick and mortar office; 12 = Full service retail office; 13 = Full service cyber office; 21 = Limited service administrative office; 22 = Limited service military facility; 23 = Limited service drive through or detached facility; 24 = Limited service loan production office; 25 = Limited service consumer credit office; 26 = Limited service contractual office; 27 = Limited service messenger office; 28 = Limited service retail office; 29 = Limited service mobile office; 30 = Limited service trust office

name: OFF_SERVTYPE_DESC

definition: Service Type Description

name: ONETIMEHLP
label: One Time Assessment Credit
definition: ( Qtly, % ) The Federal Deposit Insurance Reform Act of 2005 allows eligible Insured depository institutions to share a one-time assessment credit pool of approximately $4.7 billion. The FDIC issued a final rule (HTML | PDF – 100kb) on the One-Time Assessment Credit published on October 18, 2006. (71 Fed. Reg. 61374) (See also FDIC FIL-93-2006) As indicated in the final rule, the FDIC will provide a preliminary Statement of One-Time Credit to all eligible institutions as soon as practicable after October 18, 2006.To be eligible for the one-time credit, an institution must have been in existence on December 31, 1996, and have paid a deposit insurance assessment prior to that date, or be a “successor” to such an institution. The final rule defines a “successor” as the resulting institution in a merger or consolidation or the acquiring institution under a de facto rule.  The de facto rule recognizes a transfer of at least 90 percent of an institution’s assets and deposit liabilities as a substantial divestiture of the transferring institution’s business. An institution deemed a “successor” under this rule acquires a pro rata share of the transferring institution’s assessment base ratio, for purposes of determining the one-time assessment credit.  Each eligible institution's share of the assessment credit pool will be calculated by dividing its December 31, 1996 assessment base by the combined assessment base of all eligible institutions. Assessment credits will be applied to reduce deposit insurance assessments payable after the one-time credit regulations become effective. The FDIC assessment credit search tool allows an institution to view its preliminary 1996 assessment base and preliminary, estimated credit amount based on the final rule and data (including merger information) in the FDIC's records. An institution that participated in one or more mergers or consolidations (referred to as acquisitions on the report form) also may view the preliminary 1996 assessment base and preliminary, estimated credit amount attributable to such mergers or consolidations.  Please note that this search tool does not include transactions that might qualify under the de facto rule. An institution may request a review of its One-Time Assessment Credit by December 18, 2006.  If an institution does not submit such a request by that date, it will be barred from doing so in the future. This deadline includes claims for credits under the de facto rule. Information provided through this search tool is preliminary and estimated. The information is subject to change and might increase or decrease based on adjustments to institutions' eligibility for credits, including adjustments made under the de facto rule. If you have any questions about your 1996 assessment base, preliminary estimated credit amount, or any transaction in which your institution was involved that might affect your credit, please contact the FDIC at 1-800-759-6596.

name: ORE
label: Other Real Estate Owned
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total other real estate owned on a consolidated basis. Includes direct and indirect investments in real estate. The amount is reflected net of valuation allowances. For Thrift Financial Reporters, the valuation allowances include allowances for other repossessed assets.

Also, the subcategories of property (1-4 family, Multifamily, etc...) acquired through foreclosure are reported gross of valuation allowances while this total is reported net of valuation allowances.
