name: OTBFH1L
label: FHLB Advances Maturity Or Repricing: One Year Or Less
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Other borrowed money: fixed rate Federal Home Loan Bank advances with a remaining maturity of one year or less, and floating rate Federal Home Loan Bank advances with a next repricing date in one year or less. All overnight advances are included in this item. remaining maturity is amount of time remaining from the report date until the final contractual maturity of an advance or another borrowing without regard to the advance’s or the borrowing’s repayment schedule, if any. next repricing date is (a) the date the interest rate on an advance or other borrowing with a floating rate can next change under the terms or (b) the contractual maturity date of the advance or other borrowing, whichever is earlier.

name: OTBFH1LR
label: FHLB Advances Maturity Or Repricing: One Year Or Less
definition: ( YTD, % ) Other borrowed money: fixed rate Federal Home Loan Bank advances with a remaining maturity of one year or less, and floating rate Federal Home Loan Bank advances with a next repricing date in one year or less. All overnight advances are included in this item. remaining maturity is amount of time remaining from the report date until the final contractual maturity of an advance or another borrowing without regard to the advance’s or the borrowing’s repayment schedule, if any. next repricing date is (a) the date the interest rate on an advance or other borrowing with a floating rate can next change under the terms or (b) the contractual maturity date of the advance or other borrowing, whichever is earlier.

name: OTBFH1T3

definition: ( YTD, $ ) Other borrowed money: fixed rate Federal Home Loan Bank advances with a remaining maturity of over one year through three years, and floating rate Federal Home Loan Bank advances with a next repricing date in over one year through three years.

name: OTBFHOV5

definition: ( YTD, $ ) Other borrowed money: fixed rate Federal Home Loan Bank advances with a remaining maturity of over five years, and floating rate Federal Home Loan Bank advances with a next repricing date in over five years.

name: OTBFHOV5R
label: FHLB Advances Maturity Or Repricing: Over Five Years
definition: ( YTD, % ) Other borrowed money: fixed rate Federal Home Loan Bank advances with a remaining maturity of over five years, and floating rate Federal Home Loan Bank advances with a next repricing date in over five years.

name: OTBFHSTA
label: FHLB Structured Advances
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Other borrowed money: structured Federal Home Loan Bank advances outstanding. Structured advances are advances containing options. structured advances include (1) callable advances, i.e., fixed rate advances that the fhlb has the option to call after a specified amount of time, (2) convertible advances, i.e., fixed rate advances that the FHLB has the option to convert to floating rate after a specified amount of time, and (3) puttable advances, i.e., fixed rate advances that the bank has the option to prepay without penalty on a specified date or dates. Any other advances that have caps, floors, or other embedded derivatives are structured advances.

name: OTBFHSTAR
label: FHLB Structured Advances
definition: ( YTD, % ) Other borrowed money: structured Federal Home Loan Bank advances outstanding. Structured advances are advances containing options. structured advances include (1) callable advances, i.e., fixed rate advances that the fhlb has the option to call after a specified amount of time, (2) convertible advances, i.e., fixed rate advances that the FHLB has the option to convert to floating rate after a specified amount of time, and (3) puttable advances, i.e., fixed rate advances that the bank has the option to prepay without penalty on a specified date or dates. Any other advances that have caps, floors, or other embedded derivatives are structured advances.

name: OTHBFH1L

definition: ( Qtly, % ) Other borrowed money: all Federal Home Loan Bank advances with a remaining maturity of one year or less. Both fixed rate and floating rate advances with a remaining maturity of one year or less are included. this amount is part of the total federal home loan bank advances with a remaining maturity or next repricing date of one year or less (above). the difference is that floating rate advances with a next repricing date of one year or less, yet have a remaining maturity of over one year, are excluded in this amount.

name: OTHBORF
label: Other Borrowed Funds
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Other borrowed money held in foreign offices. Beginning in March 1997, this item includes mortgage indebtedness and obligations under capitalized leases.

name: OTHBOT1L
label: Other Borrowings With A Remaining Maturity of One Year Or Less
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Other borrowed money: all other borrowings with a remaining maturity of one year or less. Both fixed rate and floating rate borrowings with a remaining maturity of one year or less are included. This amount is part of the total other borrowings with a remaining maturity or next repricing date of one year or less (above). the difference is that floating rate advances with a next repricing date of one year or less, yet have a remaining maturity of over one year, are excluded.

name: OTHBOT1LR
label: Other Borrowings With A Remaining Maturity of One Year Or Less
definition: ( YTD, % ) Other borrowed money: all other borrowings with a remaining maturity of one year or less. Both fixed rate and floating rate borrowings with a remaining maturity of one year or less are included. This amount is part of the total other borrowings with a remaining maturity or next repricing date of one year or less (above). the difference is that floating rate advances with a next repricing date of one year or less, yet have a remaining maturity of over one year, are excluded.

name: OTHBRF
label: Other borrowed funds
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Other borrowed money (excludes deposits, federal funds purchased, securities sold under agreements to repurchase in domestic offices of the bank, and trading liabilities).  RIS definition: OTHBRF = SUM(TTL, OTHBOR, MTGLS, 0) Reporting notes: As of March 1997, includes mortgage indebtedness and obligations under capitalized leases. TTL ended December 2000 and MTGLS ended December 1996.

name: OTHBRFR
label: Other borrowed funds
definition: ( YTD, % ) Other borrowed money (excludes deposits, federal funds purchased, securities sold under agreements to repurchase in domestic offices of the bank, and trading liabilities).  RIS definition: OTHBRF = SUM(TTL, OTHBOR, MTGLS, 0) Reporting notes: As of March 1997, includes mortgage indebtedness and obligations under capitalized leases. TTL ended December 2000 and MTGLS ended December 1996.

name: OTHFFC
label: Futures and Forward Contracts commodities
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Notional amount of futures and forward contracts such as stock index and commodity (precious metals, wheat, cotton, livestock contracts) and contracts on other commodities and equities. This item is not available prior to March 1995.

name: OTHFFCR
label: Futures and Forward Contracts commodities
definition: ( YTD, % ) Notional amount of futures and forward contracts such as stock index and commodity (precious metals, wheat, cotton, livestock contracts) and contracts on other commodities and equities. This item is not available prior to March 1995.

name: OTHNII
label: Other Non-Interest Income
definition: ( Qtly, % ) All operating income of the bank not required to be reported elsewhere, including (but exclusive to): income and fees from the rental of safe deposit boxes; income and fees from the sale of checks, money orders, cashiers' checks, and travelers' checks; Income and fees from the use of the bank's ATMs;Income from performing data processing services for others; Earnings on or other increases in the value of the cash surrender value of bank-owned life insurance policies; Rent and other income from Real Estate Owned.

name: OTHNVS
label: Notional Value of Other Swaps
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The notional amount of all outstanding equity, equity index and all other swap agreements not reportable as interest rate or foreign exchange contracts. This item is not available prior to March 1995.

name: OTHNVSR
label: Notional Value of Other Swaps
definition: ( YTD, % ) The notional amount of all outstanding equity, equity index and all other swap agreements not reportable as interest rate or foreign exchange contracts. This item is not available prior to March 1995.

name: OTHOFFBS
label: All Other off-Balance Sheet Liabilities
definition: ( YTD, $ ) All other off-balance sheet liabilities (excluding off-balance sheet derivatives).

name: OTHOFFBSR
label: All Other off-Balance Sheet Liabilities
definition: ( YTD, % ) All other off-balance sheet liabilities (excluding off-balance sheet derivatives).

name: OTHPOC
label: Purchased Option Contracts Equity Instrument
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The contract amount of option contracts where the reporting institution has, for a fee, purchased the right to purchase or sell an equity instrument, equity index, a commodity or product not reportable as interest rate or foreign exchange contracts. This item is not available prior to March 1995.

name: OTHPOCR
label: Purchased Option Contracts Equity Instrument
definition: ( YTD, % ) The contract amount of option contracts where the reporting institution has, for a fee, purchased the right to purchase or sell an equity instrument, equity index, a commodity or product not reportable as interest rate or foreign exchange contracts. This item is not available prior to March 1995.

name: OTHWOC
label: Written Option Contracts equity instrument prior to March 1995
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The gross amount of contracts where the reporting institution has obligated itself, for compensation, to purchase or sell an equity instrument, equity index, a commodity or product not reportable as interest rate or foreign exchange contracts. This item is not available prior to March 1995.

name: OTHWOCR
label: Written Option Contracts equity instrument prior to March 1995
definition: ( YTD, % ) The gross amount of contracts where the reporting institution has obligated itself, for compensation, to purchase or sell an equity instrument, equity index, a commodity or product not reportable as interest rate or foreign exchange contracts. This item is not available prior to March 1995.

name: OTSREGNM
label: Office of Thrift Supervision Region - pre 07/21/11
definition: The Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) Region in which the institution was physically located. The OTS merged into the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) on July 21, 2011. The five OTS Regions and their respective states were: Northeast - Connecticut, Delaware, Maine, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island, Vermont, West Virginia Southeast - Alabama, District of Columbia, Florida, Georgia, Maryland, North Carolina, Puerto Rico, South Carolina, U.S. Virgin Islands, Virginia Central - Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio, Tennessee, Wisconsin Midwest - Arkansas, Colorado, Iowa, Kansas, Louisiana, Minnesota, Mississippi, Missouri, Nebraska, New Mexico, North Dakota, Oklahoma, South Dakota, Texas West - Alaska, American Samoa, Arizona, California, Guam, Hawaii, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, States of Micronesia, Oregon, Utah, Washington, Wyoming

name: OUT_CERT
label: Outgoing FDIC Certificate #
definition: A unique number of the outgoing institution assigned by the FDIC used to identify institutions and for the issuance of insurance certificates.

name: OUT_CHARTAGENT
label: Outgoing Chartering Agency
definition: The chartering agency of the outgoing institution - State and Federal, and the Comptroller of the Currency - Chartering authority for nationally chartered commercial banks and for federally chartered savings associations (The Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) before 7/21/11) State (includes U.S. Territories) - Chartering authority for institutions that are not chartered by the OCC or OTS

name: OUT_CHARTER
label: Outgoing OCC Charter Number
definition: A unique number of the outgoing institution assigned by the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) used to identify institutions that it has chartered and regulates (i.e. national banks).

name: OUT_CLASS
label: Outgoing Class
definition: A code of the outgoing institution indicating the its major grouping. N = FDIC Insured National Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; SM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; NM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, or a Mutual Commercial Bank; SI = FDIC Insured Stock and Mutual Savings Bank; MI = Mutually Insured; SB = FDIC Insured Savings Bank; SL = FDIC Insured Savings and Loan Association; OI = Other FDIC Insured Institution (International Banking Act); CU = Credit Union; NC = Non-insured Institution; NS = Non-insured Savings Association

name: OUT_CLCODE
label: Outgoing Class Code
definition: Numeric code which identifies the major and minor categories of an outgoing institution.

name: OUT_CNTYNAME
label: Outgoing County Name
definition: County where the outgoing institution is physically located (abbreviated if the county name exceeds 16 characters).

name: OUT_CNTYNUM
label: Outgoing County Number
definition: The federal information processing standards (FIPS) numeric code of the outgoing institution.

name: OUT_FDICREGION
label: Outgoing FDIC Supervisory Region Number
definition: A two-digit number indicating the FDIC Supervisory Division or Region of the outgoing institution. 02 = New York; 05 = Atlanta; 09 = Chicago; 11 = Kansas City; 13 = Dallas; 14 = San Francisco; 16 = Office of Complex Financial Institutions (CFI)

name: OUT_FDICREGION_DESC
label: Outgoing Supervisory Region Description
definition: A description associated with the name of an FDIC supervisory region of the outgoing institution

name: OUT_INSAGENT1
label: Outgoing Insurance Fund Membership
definition: Fund membership of the outgoing institution; Deposit Insurance Fund (DIF), Bank Insurance Fund (BIF), Savings Association Insurance Fund (SAIF)

name: OUT_INSAGENT2
label: Outgoing Secondary Insurance Fund
definition: As a result of the establishment of a single Deposit Insurance Fund (DIF) effective April 1, 2006, the Secondary Insurance fund is no longer applicable. Before April 1,2006, Bank Insurance Fund (BIF) or Savings Association Insurance Fund (SAIF)

name: OUT_INSTNAME
label: Outgoing Institution name
definition: The legal name of the outgoing institution.

name: OUT_MADDR
label: Outgoing Mailing Street Address
definition: Street address portion of the mailing address of the outgoing institution.

name: OUT_MADDR2
label: Outgoing Mailing Street Address Line 2
definition: Street address line 2 portion of the mailing address of the outgoing institution.

name: OUT_MCITY
label: Outgoing Mailing City
definition: City portion of the mailing address of the outgoing institution. Either the entire name or part of the name of a specific city may be entered to produce an Institution List.

name: OUT_MSTALP
label: Outgoing Mailing State Alpha Code
definition: 2-digit state alpha code portion of the mailing address of the outgoing institution.

name: OUT_MSTATE
label: Outgoing Mailing State
definition: State name portion of the mailing address of the outgoing institution.

name: OUT_MZIP5
label: Outgoing Mailing Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code portion of the mailing address of the outgoing institution.

name: OUT_MZIP5_RAW
label: Outgoing Mailing 5-Digit Zip Code
definition: The 5-digit postal code portion of the mailing address of the outgoing institution.

name: OUT_MZIPREST
label: Outgoing Mailing Zip Code Extension
definition: The 4-digit zip code extension portion of the mailing address of the outgoing institution.

name: OUT_PADDR
label: Outgoing Physical Street Address
definition: Street address at which the outgoing institution is physically located.

name: OUT_PADDR2
label: Outgoing Physical Street Address Line 2
definition: Street address line 2 at which the outgoing institution is physically located.

name: OUT_PCITY
label: Outgoing Physical City
definition: City in which the outgoing institution is physically located. Either the entire name or part of the name of a specific city may be entered to produce an Institution List.

name: OUT_PSTALP
label: Outgoing Physical State Alpha Code
definition: State in which the outgoing institution is physically located. The FDIC Act defines state as any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, and any territory of the United States, Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, the Virgin Island, and the Northern Mariana Islands.

name: OUT_PZIP5
label: Outgoing Physical Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code in which the outgoing institution is physically located.

name: OUT_PZIP5_RAW
label: Outgoing Physical 5-Digit Zip Code
definition: The postal code (e.g. ZIP Code for the U.S.) in which the outgoing institution is physically located.

name: OUT_PZIPREST
label: Outgoing Physical Zip Code Extension
definition: The 4-digit zip code extension in which the outgoing institution is physically located.

name: OUT_REGAGENT
label: Outgoing Chartering Agency
definition: The chartering agency of the outgoing institution - State and Federal, and the Comptroller of the Currency - Chartering authority for nationally chartered commercial banks and for federally chartered savings associations (The Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) before 7/21/11) State (includes U.S. Territories) - Chartering authority for institutions that are not chartered by the OCC or OTS

name: OUT_TRUST
label: Outgoing Trust Power
definition: The trust power status of the outgoing institution.

name: OUT_UNINUM
label: Outgoing FDIC Unique Number
definition: The FDIC unique identifier number of the outgoing institution. This value maps to the main office for the divesting Institution in a merger, acquisition, etc.

name: OWNCRCI
label: Recoveries on Loan Amounts C&I Loans Securitized
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Commercial & Industrial Loans Recoveries On Loans That Are Part OF The Carrying Value Of The Reporting Bank's Ownership (Or Seller's) Interest In Securitization Structures - Commercial And Industrial Loans.

name: OWNCRCRD
label: Recoveries on Loan Amounts Credit Cards Receivables
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Credit Cards Receivables Recoveries On Loans That Are Part OF The Carrying Value Of The Reporting Bank's Ownership (Or Seller's) Interest In Securitization Structures - Credit Card Receivables.

name: OWNCRHEL
label: Recoveries on Loan Amounts Home Equity Lines Securitized
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Home Equity Lines Recoveries On Loans That Are Part OF The Carrying Value Of The Reporting Bank's Ownership (Or Seller's) Interest In Securitization Structures - Home Equity Lines.

name: OWNDRCI
label: Charge-offs on Loan Amounts C&I Loans Securitized
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Commercial & Industrial Loans Charge-Offs On Loans That Are Part Of The Carrying Value Of The Reporting Bank's Ownership (Or Seller's) Interest In Securitization Structures - Commercial And Industrial Loans.

name: OWNDRCRD
label: Charge-offs on Loan Amounts Credit Cards Securitized Receivables
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Credit Cards Receivables Charge-Offs On Loans That Are Part Of The Carrying Value Of The Reporting Bank's Ownership (Or Seller's) Interest In Securitization Structures - Cards Card Receivables.

name: OWNDRHEL
label: Charge-offs on Loan Amounts Securitized Home Equity Lines_x000D_
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Home Equity Lines Charge-Offs On Loans That Are Part Of The Carrying Value Of The Reporting Bank's Ownership (Or Seller's) Interest In Securitization Structures - Home Equity Lines.

name: OWNLNCI
label: Amount of Ownership (Seller's) Interest, Carried As Loans C&I Loans Securitized
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Commercial & Industrial Loans Carrying Value Of Seller's Interest Not In Form Of A Security. Seller's Interest Are To Be Reported As Loans And Included In Schedule RC-C - Loans And Lease Financing Receivables - Commercial And Industrial Loans.

name: OWNLNCRD
label: Amount of Ownership (Seller's), Interest Carried As Loans Credit Cards Securitized Rec.
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Credit Cards Receivables Carrying Value Of Seller's Interest Not In Form Of A Security. Seller's Interest Are To Be Reported As Loans And Included In Schedule RC-C - Loans And Lease Financing Receivables - Credit Card Loans.

name: OWNLNHEL
label: Amount of Ownership (Seller's), Interest Carried As Loans Home Equity Lines
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Home Equity Lines Carrying Value Of Seller's Interest Not In Form Of A Security. Seller's Interest Are To Be Reported As Loans And Included In Schedule RC-C - Loans And Lease Financing Receivables - Home Equity Loans.

name: OWNP3CI
label: Past Due Loan Amounts 30-89 Days: C&I Loans
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Commercial & Industrial Loans Outstanding Principal Balance Of Loans 30-89 Days Past Due That Are Part Of The Carrying Value Of The Reporting Bank’s Ownership (Or Seller's) Interest In Securitization Structures - Commercial And Industrial Loans.

name: OWNP3CRD
label: Past Due Loan Amounts 30-89 Credit Cards Receivables
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Credit Cards Receivables Outstanding Principal Balance Of Loans 30-89 Days Past Due That Are Part Of The Carrying Value Of The Reporting Bank's Ownership (Or Seller's) Interest In Securitization Structures - Credit Card Receivables.

name: OWNP3HEL
label: Past Due Loan Amounts 30-89 Days: Home Equity Lines
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Home Equity Lines Outstanding Principal Balance Of Loans 30-89 Days Past Due That Are Part Of The Carrying Value Of The Reporting Bank's Ownership (Or Seller's) Interest In Securitization Structures - Home Equity Loans.

name: OWNP9CI
label: Past Due Loan Amounts 90+ Days C&I Loans Securitized
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Commercial & Industrial Loans Outstanding Principal Balance Of Loans 90+ Days Past Due That Are Part Of The Carrying Value Of The Reporting Bank's Ownership (Or Seller's) Interest In Securitization Structures - Commercial And Industrial Loans.

name: OWNP9CRD
label: Past Due Loan Amounts 90+ Days Credit Cards Securitized Receivables
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Credit Cards Receivables Outstanding Principal Balance Of Loans 90+ Days Past Due That Are Part Of The Carrying Value Of The Reporting Bank's Ownership (Or Seller's) Interest In Securitization Structures - Credit Card Receivables.

name: OWNP9HEL
label: Past Due Loan Amounts 90+ Days Home Equity Lines Securitized
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Home Equity Lines Outstanding Principal Balance Of Loans 90+ Days Past Due That Are Part Of The Carrying Value Of The Reporting Bank's Ownership (Or Seller's) Interest In Securitization Structures - Home Equity Loans.

name: OWNSCCI
label: Amount of Ownership (Seller's), Interest C&I Loans Securitized
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Commercial & Industrial Loans Carrying Value Of Seller's Interest Not In Form Of A Security. Seller's Interest Are To Be Reported As Loans And Included In Schedule RC-C - Loans And Lease Financing Receivables - Commercial And Industrial Loans.

name: OWNSCCRD
label: Amount of Ownership (Seller's), Interest Credit Cards Securitized Receivables
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Credit Cards Receivables Carrying Value Of Seller's Interest Not In Form Of A Security That Is Included As Available-For-Sale Or Held-To-Maturity Securities In Schedule RC-B - Securities Or As Trading Securities In Schedule RC, Item 5, Trading Assets - Underlying Asset Is Credit Cards Receivables.

name: OWNSCHEL
label: Amount of Ownership (Seller's), Interest Home Equity Lines Securitized
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Home Equity Lines Carrying Value Of Seller's Interest Not In Form Of A Security That Is Included As Available-For-Sale Or Held-To-Maturity Securities In Schedule RC-B - Securities Or As Trading Securities In Schedule RC, Item 5, Trading Assets - Underlying Asset Is Home Equity Loans.

name: P3AG
label: Loans To Finance Agricultural Production and Other Loans To Farmers, Past Due 30
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total rloans to finance agricultural production and other loans to farmers past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: P3AGR
label: Loans To Finance Agricultural Production and Other Loans To Farmers, Past Due 30
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total rloans to finance agricultural production and other loans to farmers past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: P3AGSM
label: Loans for Agricultural Production and Other Loans To Farmers, Past Due 30-89Days
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total rloans to finance agricultural production and other loans to farmers past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest. Institutions with assets less than $300 million report this item only if their agricultural production loans exceed 5 percent of total loans. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: P3AGSMR
label: Loans for Agricultural Production and Other Loans To Farmers, Past Due 30-89Days
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total rloans to finance agricultural production and other loans to farmers past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest. Institutions with assets less than $300 million report this item only if their agricultural production loans exceed 5 percent of total loans. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: P3ASSET
label: Assets Past Due 30-89 Days
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total assets past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest. Total assets include real estate loans, installment loans, credit cards and related plan loans, commercial, and all other loans, lease financing receivables, debt securities and other assets. Note: The FFIEC considers 30 through 89 day past due data on an institution level confidential.

name: P3ASSETR
label: Assets Past Due 30-89 Days
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total assets past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest. Total assets include real estate loans, installment loans, credit cards and related plan loans, commercial, and all other loans, lease financing receivables, debt securities and other assets. Note: The FFIEC considers 30 through 89 day past due data on an institution level confidential.

name: P3AUTO
label: 30-89 Days P/D Auto Loans
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Automobile loans to Individuals for household, family and other personal expenditures past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest. available as of March 2011

name: P3AUTOR
label: 30-89 Days P/D Auto Loans
definition: ( YTD, % ) Automobile loans to Individuals for household, family and other personal expenditures past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest. available as of March 2011

name: P3CI
label: Commercial and Industrial Loans, Past Due 30 - 89 Days
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total commercial and industrial loans past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest, which are held in domestic offices of the institution. Institutions with assets of less than $300 million include all other loans in this item.

name: P3CINUS
label: Construction Loans To Non-U.S. Addressees, Past Due 30 - 89 Days
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total construction and land development loans secured by real estate (with non-U.S. addressees) past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest.

name: P3CINUSR
label: Construction Loans To Non-U.S. Addressees, Past Due 30 - 89 Days
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total construction and land development loans secured by real estate (with non-U.S. addressees) past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest.

name: P3CIR
label: Commercial and Industrial Loans, Past Due 30 - 89 Days
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total commercial and industrial loans past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest, which are held in domestic offices of the institution. Institutions with assets of less than $300 million include all other loans in this item.

name: P3CON
label: Loans To Individuals, Past Due 30 - 89 Days
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total loans to Individuals for household, family, and other personal expenditures past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest.

name: P3CONOTH
label: 30-89 Days P/D-Other Consumer
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Other loans to Individuals for household, family and other personal expenditures past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest on a consolidated basis (excludes credit cards). Note: This information on an institution level is considered confidential by the FFIEC. beginning March 2001, includes revolving credit plans other than credit cards. beginning March 2011, excludes automobile loans.

name: P3CONOTHR
label: 30-89 Days P/D-Other Consumer
definition: ( YTD, % ) Other loans to Individuals for household, family and other personal expenditures past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest on a consolidated basis (excludes credit cards). Note: This information on an institution level is considered confidential by the FFIEC. beginning March 2001, includes revolving credit plans other than credit cards. beginning March 2011, excludes automobile loans.

name: P3CONR
label: Loans To Individuals, Past Due 30 - 89 Days
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total loans to Individuals for household, family, and other personal expenditures past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest.

name: P3CRCD
label: Credit Cards and Related Plans, Past Due 30 - 89 Days
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total credit card loans to Individuals past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest. Prior to 2001, included revolving credit plans other than credit cards.

name: P3CRCDR
label: Credit Cards and Related Plans, Past Due 30 - 89 Days
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total credit card loans to Individuals past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest. Prior to 2001, included revolving credit plans other than credit cards.

name: P3DEP
label: Loans To Depository Institutions, Past Due 30 - 89 Days
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total rloans to depository institutions and acceptances of other banks past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: P3DEPNUS
label: Loans To Foreign Banks, Past Due 30 - 89 Days
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Loans To Foreign Banks, Past Due 30 - 89 Days

name: P3DEPNUSR
label: Loans To Foreign Banks, Past Due 30 - 89 Days
definition: ( YTD, % ) Loans To Foreign Banks, Past Due 30 - 89 Days

name: P3DEPR
label: Loans To Depository Institutions, Past Due 30 - 89 Days
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total rloans to depository institutions and acceptances of other banks past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: P3FG
label: Loans To Foreign Governments and Official Institutions, Past Due 30 - 89 Days
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total rloans to foreign governments and official institutions past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: P3FGR
label: Loans To Foreign Governments and Official Institutions, Past Due 30 - 89 Days
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total rloans to foreign governments and official institutions past due 30 through 89 days and still accruing interest. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: P3GTY
label: Guaranteed Portion of Loans and Leases, Excluding Gnma Loans, 30-89Days Past Due
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The maximum amount recoverable from the U.S. Government for past due 30 to 89 day loans and leases (excluding GNMA loans) that are guaranteed by the U.S. Government and still accruing interest on a consolidated basis. The U.S. Government includes its agencies and its government-sponsored agencies, under the guarantee or insurance provisions applicable to the loans and leases. Listed as memoranda only and is included in total loans and leases past due 30-89 days or more and still accruing interest. GNMA loans are guaranteed by the Government National Mortgage Association or commonly know as Ginnie Mae. FRB MDRM Number: RFCD5615 or RCON5615  Note: Prior to March 2011, included loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements.

name: P3GTYGNM
label: Rebooked Gnma Loans Repurchased Or Are Eligible for Repurchase, 30-89 Days Past due
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Past due 30-89 day rebooked GNMA loans that have been repurchased or are eligible for repurchase by seller-servicers of GNMA loans; and past due 30-89 day loans that have been purchased out of GNMA securitizations by servicers of GNMA loans that were not the transferors of the loans. GNMA loans are guaranteed by the Government National Mortgage Association or commonly know as Ginnie Mae. Listed here as memoranda only and is included in total loans and leases past due 30-89 days and still accruing interest. FRB Number: RCFDC866 or RCONC866 Note: Prior to March 2011, included loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements.

name: P3GTYGNMR
label: Rebooked Gnma Loans Repurchased Or Are Eligible for Repurchase, 30-89 Days Past due
definition: ( YTD, % ) Past due 30-89 day rebooked GNMA loans that have been repurchased or are eligible for repurchase by seller-servicers of GNMA loans; and past due 30-89 day loans that have been purchased out of GNMA securitizations by servicers of GNMA loans that were not the transferors of the loans. GNMA loans are guaranteed by the Government National Mortgage Association or commonly know as Ginnie Mae. Listed here as memoranda only and is included in total loans and leases past due 30-89 days and still accruing interest. FRB Number: RCFDC866 or RCONC866 Note: Prior to March 2011, included loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements.

name: P3GTYPAR
label: Loans and Leases 30-89 Past Due, U.S. Government Wholly Or Partially Guaranteed
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Loans and leases for which repayment of principal is wholly or partially guaranteed or Insured by the U.S. Government, including its agencies and its government-sponsored agencies that are past due 30 to 89 days and are still accruing interest on a consolidated basis. Examples include loans guaranteed by the Small Business Administration, and the Federal Housing Administration. Excluded are loans and leases guaranteed or Insured by state or local governments, state or local government agencies, foreign (non-U.S.) governments, and private agencies or organizations. Also excluded are loans and leases collateralized by securities issued by the U.S. Government, including its agencies and its government-sponsored agencies. Included in total loans and leases past due 30-89 days or more and are still accruing interest. FRB MDRM numbers: RFCD5612 or RCON5612 important note: prior to March 2011, included loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements.

name: P3GTYPARR
label: Loans and Leases 30-89 Past Due, U.S. Government Wholly Or Partially Guaranteed
definition: ( YTD, % ) Loans and leases for which repayment of principal is wholly or partially guaranteed or Insured by the U.S. Government, including its agencies and its government-sponsored agencies that are past due 30 to 89 days and are still accruing interest on a consolidated basis. Examples include loans guaranteed by the Small Business Administration, and the Federal Housing Administration. Excluded are loans and leases guaranteed or Insured by state or local governments, state or local government agencies, foreign (non-U.S.) governments, and private agencies or organizations. Also excluded are loans and leases collateralized by securities issued by the U.S. Government, including its agencies and its government-sponsored agencies. Included in total loans and leases past due 30-89 days or more and are still accruing interest. FRB MDRM numbers: RFCD5612 or RCON5612 important note: prior to March 2011, included loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements.

name: P3GTYR
label: Guaranteed Portion of Loans and Leases, Excluding Gnma Loans, 30-89Days Past Due
definition: ( YTD, % ) The maximum amount recoverable from the U.S. Government for past due 30 to 89 day loans and leases (excluding GNMA loans) that are guaranteed by the U.S. Government and still accruing interest on a consolidated basis. The U.S. Government includes its agencies and its government-sponsored agencies, under the guarantee or insurance provisions applicable to the loans and leases. Listed as memoranda only and is included in total loans and leases past due 30-89 days or more and still accruing interest. GNMA loans are guaranteed by the Government National Mortgage Association or commonly know as Ginnie Mae. FRB MDRM Number: RFCD5615 or RCON5615  Note: Prior to March 2011, included loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements.

label: Guaranteed Portion of Loans and Leases, Excluding Gnma Loans, 90 Days Past Due
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The maximum amount recoverable from the U.S. Government from past due 90 days or more loans and leases (excluding GNMA loans) that are guaranteed by the U.S. Government and still accruing interest on a consolidated basis. The U.S. Government includes its agencies and its government-sponsored agencies, under the guarantee or insurance provisions applicable to the loans and leases. Listed here as memoranda only and is included in total loans and leases past due 90 days or more days and still accruing interest. GNMA loans are guaranteed by the Government National Mortgage Association or commonly know as Ginnie Mae. FRB Number: RFCD5616 or RCON5616 Note: Prior to March 2011, included loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements.

name: P9GTYGNM
label: Rebooked Gnma Loans That Have Been Repurchased Or Are Eligible for Repurchase,
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Past due 90 or more day rebooked GNMA loans that have been repurchased or are eligible for repurchase by seller-servicers of GNMA loans and past due 90 days or more loans that have been purchased out of GNMA securitizations by servicers of GNMA loans that were not the transferors of the loans. GNMA loans are guaranteed by the Government National Mortgage Association, commonly know as Ginnie Mae. Included in total loans and leases past due 90 or more days and still accruing interest. FRB Number: RCFDC867 or RCONC867  Note: Prior to March 2011, included loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements.

name: P9GTYGNMR
label: Rebooked Gnma Loans That Have Been Repurchased Or Are Eligible for Repurchase,
definition: ( YTD, % ) Past due 90 or more day rebooked GNMA loans that have been repurchased or are eligible for repurchase by seller-servicers of GNMA loans and past due 90 days or more loans that have been purchased out of GNMA securitizations by servicers of GNMA loans that were not the transferors of the loans. GNMA loans are guaranteed by the Government National Mortgage Association, commonly know as Ginnie Mae. Included in total loans and leases past due 90 or more days and still accruing interest. FRB Number: RCFDC867 or RCONC867  Note: Prior to March 2011, included loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements.

name: P9GTYPAR
label: Loans and Leases, Wholly Or Partially Guaranteed By The U.S. Government-90+Pd
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Loans and leases for which repayment of principal is wholly or partially guaranteed or Insured by the U.S. Government, including its agencies and its government-sponsored agencies, that are past due 90 days or more and are still accruing interest on a consolidated basis. Examples include loans guaranteed by the Small Business Administration, and the Federal Housing Administration. Excluded are loans and leases guaranteed or Insured by state or local governments, state or local government agencies, foreign (non-U.S.) governments, and private agencies or organizations. Also excluded are loans and leases collateralized by securities issued by the U.S. Government, including its agencies and its government-sponsored agencies. Listed here as memoranda only and is included in total loans and leases past due 90+ days or more and still accruing interest. FRB Number: RFCD5613 RCON5613   Note: As of March 2011, no longer includes loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements.

name: P9GTYPARR
label: Loans and Leases, Wholly Or Partially Guaranteed By The U.S. Government-90+Pd
definition: ( YTD, % ) Loans and leases for which repayment of principal is wholly or partially guaranteed or Insured by the U.S. Government, including its agencies and its government-sponsored agencies, that are past due 90 days or more and are still accruing interest on a consolidated basis. Examples include loans guaranteed by the Small Business Administration, and the Federal Housing Administration. Excluded are loans and leases guaranteed or Insured by state or local governments, state or local government agencies, foreign (non-U.S.) governments, and private agencies or organizations. Also excluded are loans and leases collateralized by securities issued by the U.S. Government, including its agencies and its government-sponsored agencies. Listed here as memoranda only and is included in total loans and leases past due 90+ days or more and still accruing interest. FRB Number: RFCD5613 RCON5613   Note: As of March 2011, no longer includes loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements.

name: P9GTYR

definition: ( YTD, % ) The maximum amount recoverable from the U.S. Government from past due 90 days or more loans and leases (excluding GNMA loans) that are guaranteed by the U.S. Government and still accruing interest on a consolidated basis. The U.S. Government includes its agencies and its government-sponsored agencies, under the guarantee or insurance provisions applicable to the loans and leases. Listed here as memoranda only and is included in total loans and leases past due 90 days or more days and still accruing interest. GNMA loans are guaranteed by the Government National Mortgage Association or commonly know as Ginnie Mae. FRB Number: RFCD5616 or RCON5616 Note: Prior to March 2011, included loans and leases covered by FDIC loss-sharing agreements.

name: P9LGTY

definition: ( YTD, $ ) The maximum amount recoverable from the FDIC under loss-sharing agreements, or total protected portion, covering the loans and leases past due 90 days or more and still accruing interest. Is reported above and beyond the amount that has already been reflected in the measurement of the reporting bank’s indemnification asset, which represents the right to receive payments from the FDIC under the loss-sharing agreement. Available as of March 2011.

name: P9LGTYR

definition: ( YTD, % ) The maximum amount recoverable from the FDIC under loss-sharing agreements, or total protected portion, covering the loans and leases past due 90 days or more and still accruing interest. Is reported above and beyond the amount that has already been reflected in the measurement of the reporting bank’s indemnification asset, which represents the right to receive payments from the FDIC under the loss-sharing agreement. Available as of March 2011.

name: PADDR
label: Physical Street Address
definition: Street address at which the institution or one of its branches is physically located.

name: PADDR2
label: Physical Street Address Line 2
definition: Street address line 2 at which the institution or one of its branches is physically located.

name: PARCERT
label: Directly owned by another bank (CERT)
definition: The PARCERT number identifies the subsidiary institutions parent certificate number. Beginning in March 1997, both the Thrift Financial Reports and Call Reports are completed on a fully consolidated basis. Previously, the consolidation of subsidiary depository institutions was prohibited. Now, parent institutions are required to file consolidated reports, while their subsidiary financial institutions are still required to file separate reports.

name: PARTACQU
label: Participations Acquired By Reporting Bank
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Participations in acceptances acquired by the reporting (nonaccepting) bank that are outstanding. This information is not available after December 31, 2014.

name: PARTCONV
label: Participations Conveyed To Others By Reporting Bank
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Participations in acceptances conveyed to others by the institution that are outstanding. not reported as of March 2006

name: PARTCONVR
label: Participations Conveyed To Others By Reporting Bank
definition: ( YTD, % ) Participations in acceptances conveyed to others by the institution that are outstanding. not reported as of March 2006

name: PCITY
label: Physical City
definition: City in which an institution's main office or one of its branches is physically located. Either the entire name or part of the name of a specific city may be entered to produce an Institution List.

name: PCTOFASSETS
label: Percent of Assets
definition: ( Qtly, % )  Balance sheet values displayed as a percent of total assets.

name: PCTOFAVGASSETS
label: Percent of Average Assets
definition: ( Qtly, % )  Annualized income displayed as a percent of average total assets. Year-to-date income is displayed as a percent of Average assets, year-to-date. Quarterly income is displayed as a percent of Average assets, quarterly.

name: PLACENUM
label: PLACENUM
definition: The FIPS MCD (Minor Civil Division) code refers to a subsection of the U.S. Census county subdivision statistics. The county subdivision FIPS includes census data for county divisions, census subareas, minor civil divisions, unorganized territories, and incorporated areas.

name: PPPLF1LS
label: Outstanding balance under the PPPLF with a remaining maturity of one year or less
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The outstanding amount as of the report date of borrowings from a Federal Reserve Bank under the PPPLF with a remaining maturity of one year or less. The remaining maturity is the amount of time remaining from the report date until the final contractual maturity of the borrowing without regard to the borrowing’s repayment schedule, if any. the borrowings included in this item have also been included in line items that correspond on the Memoranda Report related to the repricing for other borrowings and secured other borrowings (ffiec call schedule rc-m item 5.b. and 10.b)Data begins 0n June 30, 2020.

name: PPPLF1LSR
label: Outstanding balance under the PPPLF with a remaining maturity of one year or less
definition: ( YTD, % ) The outstanding amount as of the report date of borrowings from a Federal Reserve Bank under the PPPLF with a remaining maturity of one year or less. The remaining maturity is the amount of time remaining from the report date until the final contractual maturity of the borrowing without regard to the borrowing’s repayment schedule, if any. the borrowings included in this item have also been included in line items that correspond on the Memoranda Report related to the repricing for other borrowings and secured other borrowings (ffiec call schedule rc-m item 5.b. and 10.b)Data begins 0n June 30, 2020.

name: PPPLFOV1
label: Outstanding balance under the PPPLF with a remaining maturity of more than one year
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The outstanding amount as of the report date of borrowings by the reporting institution from a Federal Reserve Bank under the PPPLF with a remaining maturity of more than one year. The remaining maturity is the amount of time remaining from the report date until the final contractual maturity of the borrowing without regard to the borrowing’s repayment schedule, if any. the borrowings included in this item also have also been included in line items that correspond on supplemental reports related to the repricing for other borrowings and secured other borrowings (ffiec call schedule rc-m item 5b. and 10.b) Data begins on June 30, 2020.

name: PPPLFOV1R
label: Outstanding balance under the PPPLF with a remaining maturity of more than one year
definition: ( YTD, % ) The outstanding amount as of the report date of borrowings by the reporting institution from a Federal Reserve Bank under the PPPLF with a remaining maturity of more than one year. The remaining maturity is the amount of time remaining from the report date until the final contractual maturity of the borrowing without regard to the borrowing’s repayment schedule, if any. the borrowings included in this item also have also been included in line items that correspond on supplemental reports related to the repricing for other borrowings and secured other borrowings (ffiec call schedule rc-m item 5b. and 10.b) Data begins on June 30, 2020.

name: PPPLNBAL
label: Outstanding Balance of PPP Loans
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The aggregate amount at which Paycheck Protection Program (PPP) loans held for investment, held for sale, and held for trading as of the report date. These loans are included within the balances on the Net Loans and Leases Report (FFIEC Call Report Schedule RC-C.) the paycheck protection program (ppp) was established by section 1102 of the 2020 coronavirus aid, relief, and economic security act, which was enacted on March 27, 2020 and amended on June 5, 2020. ppp covered loans (ppp loans) are fully guaranteed as to principal and accrued interest by the U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA). Data begins on June 30, 2020.

name: PPPLNBALR
label: Outstanding Balance of PPP Loans
definition: ( YTD, % ) The aggregate amount at which Paycheck Protection Program (PPP) loans held for investment, held for sale, and held for trading as of the report date. These loans are included within the balances on the Net Loans and Leases Report (FFIEC Call Report Schedule RC-C.) the paycheck protection program (ppp) was established by section 1102 of the 2020 coronavirus aid, relief, and economic security act, which was enacted on March 27, 2020 and amended on June 5, 2020. ppp covered loans (ppp loans) are fully guaranteed as to principal and accrued interest by the U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA). Data begins on June 30, 2020.

name: PPPLNNUM
label: Number of PPP Loans Outstanding
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Number of U.S. Small Business Administration (PPP) loans held by the reporting institution as of the report date. the paycheck protection program (ppp) was established by section 1102 of the 2020 coronavirus aid, relief, and Economic Security Act, which was enacted on March 27, 2020 and amended on June 5, 2020. PPP covered loans (PPP loans) are fully guaranteed as to principal and accrued interest by the U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA). Data begins on June 30, 2020.

name: PPPLNNUMR
label: Number of PPP Loans Outstanding
definition: ( YTD, % ) Number of U.S. Small Business Administration (PPP) loans held by the reporting institution as of the report date. the paycheck protection program (ppp) was established by section 1102 of the 2020 coronavirus aid, relief, and Economic Security Act, which was enacted on March 27, 2020 and amended on June 5, 2020. PPP covered loans (PPP loans) are fully guaranteed as to principal and accrued interest by the U.S. Small Business Administration (SBA). Data begins on June 30, 2020.

name: PPPLNPLG
label: Outstanding Balance of PPP Loans Pledged To The PPPLF
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The aggregate amount of Paycheck Protection Program (PPP) loans pledged to the Federal Reserve PPP Liquidity Facility (PPPLF). These loans are included as applicable in the balances on the Net Loans and Leases Report (FFIEC Call Report Schedule RC-C) and as Pledged Loans and Leases/Pledged Loans (Schedule RC-D). The Federal Reserve PPP liquidity facility (PPPLF) was authorized by the board of governors of the federal reserve system on April 8, 2020, under section 13(3) of the federal reserve act (12 u.s.c. 343(3)). under the PPPLF, the Federal Reserve banks will extend non-recourse loans to eligible lenders, with the extensions of credit secured by SBA-guaranteed PPP loans that the lenders have originated or purchased. Data begins on June 30, 2020.

name: PPPLNPLGR
label: Outstanding Balance of PPP Loans Pledged To The PPPLF
definition: ( YTD, % ) The aggregate amount of Paycheck Protection Program (PPP) loans pledged to the Federal Reserve PPP Liquidity Facility (PPPLF). These loans are included as applicable in the balances on the Net Loans and Leases Report (FFIEC Call Report Schedule RC-C) and as Pledged Loans and Leases/Pledged Loans (Schedule RC-D). The Federal Reserve PPP liquidity facility (PPPLF) was authorized by the board of governors of the federal reserve system on April 8, 2020, under section 13(3) of the federal reserve act (12 u.s.c. 343(3)). under the PPPLF, the Federal Reserve banks will extend non-recourse loans to eligible lenders, with the extensions of credit secured by SBA-guaranteed PPP loans that the lenders have originated or purchased. Data begins on June 30, 2020.

definition: A date indicating when an institution's change/event is processed.

name: PSTALP
label: Physical State Alpha Code
definition: State in which the main office is physically located. The FDIC Act defines state as any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, and any territory of the United States, Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, the Virgin Island, and the Northern Mariana Islands.

name: PTAXNETINC

definition: ( YTD, $ ) Pre-tax Net Operating Income

label: Pre-tax Net Operating Income
