name: PZIP5
label: Physical Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code representing in which the institution or one of its branches is physically located.

name: PZIPREST
label: Physical Zip Code Extension
definition: The 4-digit zip code extension in which the institution or one of its branches is physically located.

name: QBPRCOML
label: Quarterly Banking Profile Commercial Bank Region
definition: The Quarterly Banking Profile (QBP) Commercial Bank Region in which the institution is physically located. The six QBP Regions and their respective states are: Northeast - Connecticut, Delaware, District of Columbia, Maine, Maryland, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Puerto Rico, Rhode Island, Vermont, U.S. Virgin Islands Southeast - Alabama, Florida, Georgia, Mississippi, North Carolina, South Carolina, Tennessee, Virginia, West Virginia Central - Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio, Wisconsin Midwest - Iowa, Kansas, Minnesota, Missouri, Nebraska, North Dakota, South Dakota Southwest - Arkansas, Louisiana, New Mexico, Oklahoma, Texas West - Alaska, American Samoa, Arizona, California, Colorado, Guam, Hawaii, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, Oregon, States of Micronesia, Utah, Washington, Wyoming

name: RB2LNRES
label: Loan Loss Allowance Included in Tier 2 Capital
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Loan Loss Allowance Included in Tier 2 Capital

name: RB2LNRESR
label: Loan Loss Allowance Included in Tier 2 Capital
definition: ( YTD, % ) Loan Loss Allowance Included in Tier 2 Capital

name: RBC1AAJ
label: Leverage (Core Capital) Ratio
definition: ( YTD, % ) Tier 1 (core) capital as a percent of average total assets minus ineligible intangibles. beginning in March 2020, the leverage ratio is used in the community bank leverage ratio (CBLR) framework. Tier 1 (core) capital includes: common equity plus noncumulative perpetual preferred stock plus minority interests in consolidated subsidiaries less goodwill and other ineligible intangible assets. The amount of eligible intangibles (including mortgage servicing rights) included in core capital is limited in accordance with supervisory capital regulations. Total assets for the leverage ratio is average total consolidated assets, less deductions from common equity tier 1 capital & additional tier 1 capital, less other deductions defined by regulatory capital rules of the bank’s primary federal supervisor. As of March 2015, all institutions began reporting the amended CALL schedule RC-R Part I and Part II which incorporates risk-based capital rules based on the Basel III framework and section 939A of the Dodd-Frank Act. Some designated institutions began reporting based on the updated requirements as of March 2014. (See: FDIC Financial Institutions Letter FIL-24-2012)

name: RBC1AAQR
label: Leverage (Core Capital) Ratio
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Leverage (Core Capital) Ratio

name: RBC1RWAJ
label: Tier 1 Risk-Based Capital Ratio
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Tier 1 (core) capital as a percent of risk-weighted assets as defined by the appropriate federal regulator for prompt corrective action during that time period. beginning March 2020, an institution that has elected the Community Bank leverage Ratio (CBLR) framework is not required to calculate this ratio. As of March 2015, all institutions began reporting the amended CALL schedule RC-R Part I and Part II which incorporates risk-based capital rules based on the Basel III framework and section 939A of the Dodd-Frank Act. Some designated institutions began reporting based on the updated requirements as of March 2014. (See: FDIC Financial Institutions Letter FIL-24-2012)

name: RBCRWAJ
label: Total Risk-Based Capital Ratio
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total Risk-Based Capital Ratio (No Cblr Electors)***

name: RBCRWAQR
label: Total Risk-Based Capital Ratio
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total Risk-Based Capital Ratio (No Cblr Electors)***

name: RBCT1CER
label: Common Equity Tier 1 Capital Ratio
definition: ( Qtly, % ) The ratio of common equity tier 1 capital to risk-weighted assets. Beginning March 2020, this ratio in not required nor available for an institution that has elected the Community Bank leverage Ratio (CBLR) framework.   Common equity tier 1 capital includes common stock instruments and related surplus, retained earnings, accumulated other comprehensive income (AOCI), and limited amounts of common equity tier 1 minority interest, minus applicable regulatory adjustments and deductions. Items that are fully deducted from common equity tier 1 capital include goodwill, other intangible assets (excluding mortgage servicing assets) and certain deferred tax assets; items that are subject to limits in common equity tier 1 capital include mortgage servicing assets, eligible deferred tax assets, and certain significant investments. As of March 2015, all institutions began reporting the amended CALL schedule RC-R Part I and Part II which incorporates risk-based capital rules based on the Basel III framework and section 939A of the Dodd-Frank Act. Some designated institutions began reporting based on the updated requirements as of March 2014. (See: FDIC Financial Institutions Letter FIL-24-2012)  Available beginning March 2014.

name: RBCT1J
label: Tier One (Core) Capital
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Tier 1 (core) capital includes: common equity plus noncumulative perpetual preferred stock plus minority interests in consolidated subsidiaries less goodwill and other ineligible intangible assets. The amount of eligible intangibles (including mortgage servicing rights) included in core capital is limited in accordance with supervisory capital regulations.   As of March 2015, all institutions began reporting the amended CALL schedule RC-R Part I and Part II which incorporates Advanced Approaches Risk-Based Capital Rule based on the Basel III framework and section 939A of the Dodd-Frank Act. Some designated institutions began reporting based on the updated requirements as of March 2014. (See: FDIC Financial Institutions Letter FIL-24-2012)

name: RBCT1JR
label: Tier One (Core) Capital
definition: ( YTD, % ) Tier 1 (core) capital includes: common equity plus noncumulative perpetual preferred stock plus minority interests in consolidated subsidiaries less goodwill and other ineligible intangible assets. The amount of eligible intangibles (including mortgage servicing rights) included in core capital is limited in accordance with supervisory capital regulations.   As of March 2015, all institutions began reporting the amended CALL schedule RC-R Part I and Part II which incorporates Advanced Approaches Risk-Based Capital Rule based on the Basel III framework and section 939A of the Dodd-Frank Act. Some designated institutions began reporting based on the updated requirements as of March 2014. (See: FDIC Financial Institutions Letter FIL-24-2012)

name: RBCT2
label: Tier 2 Risk-Based Capital
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Risk-based capital-tier 2 is based on the risk-based capital definitions for prompt corrective action (PCA). beginning March 2020, an institution that has elected the Community Bank Leverage Ratio (CBLR) framework is not required to calculate tier 2 capital nor make any deductions that would have been taken from tier 2 capital. Includible Tier 2 capital components consist of, but are not limited to, limited subordinated debt, cumulative perpetual preferred stock, allowance for loan and lease losses, total mandatory convertible debt and a portion of unrealized gains on available-for-sale equity securities. The maximum amount of supplementary capital elements that qualify as Tier 2 capital is limited to 100 percent of Tier 1 capital. In addition, the combined maximum amount of subordinated debt and intermediate-term preferred stock that qualifies as Tier 2 capital is limited to 50 percent of Tier 1 capital. As of March 2015, all institutions began reporting the amended CALL schedule RC-R Part I and Part II which incorporates Advanced Approaches Risk-Based Capital Rule based on the Basel III framework and section 939A of the Dodd-Frank Act. Some designated institutions began reporting based on the updated requirements as of March 2014. (See: FDIC Financial Institutions Letter FIL-24-2012)

name: RBCT2R
label: Tier 2 Risk-Based Capital
definition: ( YTD, % ) Risk-based capital-tier 2 is based on the risk-based capital definitions for prompt corrective action (PCA). beginning March 2020, an institution that has elected the Community Bank Leverage Ratio (CBLR) framework is not required to calculate tier 2 capital nor make any deductions that would have been taken from tier 2 capital. Includible Tier 2 capital components consist of, but are not limited to, limited subordinated debt, cumulative perpetual preferred stock, allowance for loan and lease losses, total mandatory convertible debt and a portion of unrealized gains on available-for-sale equity securities. The maximum amount of supplementary capital elements that qualify as Tier 2 capital is limited to 100 percent of Tier 1 capital. In addition, the combined maximum amount of subordinated debt and intermediate-term preferred stock that qualifies as Tier 2 capital is limited to 50 percent of Tier 1 capital. As of March 2015, all institutions began reporting the amended CALL schedule RC-R Part I and Part II which incorporates Advanced Approaches Risk-Based Capital Rule based on the Basel III framework and section 939A of the Dodd-Frank Act. Some designated institutions began reporting based on the updated requirements as of March 2014. (See: FDIC Financial Institutions Letter FIL-24-2012)

name: REGAGENT
label: Primary Regulator
definition: A code indicating the federal regulatory agency that provides primary supervision over an institution. OCC=Office of the Comptroller of Currency; FDIC=Federal Deposit Insurance Corporation; FRB=Federal Reserve Board; NCUA=National Credit Union Association; OTS=Office of Thrift Supervision.

name: REGAGENT2
label: Secondary Regulator
definition: A code indicating the federal regulatory agency that provides secondary supervision over an institution. CFPB = Consumer Financial Protection Bureau; OTS=Office of Thrift Supervision.

name: REGAGNT
label: Primary Federal Regulator (Institution)
definition: There are now three federal regulators of banks and savings and loan institutions. Before July 21, 2011, there were four federal regulators: federal deposit insurance corporation (FDIC) - primary federal regulator responsible for state-chartered banks not members of the Federal Reserve System, state chartered savings banks and as of July 21, 2011, state chartered thrifts. Federal Reserve Board (FRB) - Primary federal regulator responsible for state-chartered commercial and savings bank members of the Federal Reserve System. Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) - Primary federal regulator responsible for nationally chartered commercial banks, and as of July 21, 2011, federally chartered thrifts. Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) - Prior to its merger into the OCC on July 21, 2011, primary federal regulator responsible for federally chartered savings and loan associations, federal savings banks and state-chartered savings and loan associations. FDIC Insured depository institutions are members of the Deposit Insurance Fund (DIF).

name: REGNBKDN
label: Regional Breakdown
definition: Atlanta - Alabama, Florida, Georgia, North Carolina, South Carolina, Virginia, West Virginia Chicago - Illinois, Indiana, Kentucky, Michigan, Ohio, Wisconsin Dallas - Arkansas, Colorado, Louisiana, Mississippi, New Mexico, Oklahoma, Tennessee, Texas Kansas City - Iowa, Kansas, Minnesota, Missouri, North Dakota, Nebraska, South Dakota New York - Connecticut, District of Columbia, Delaware, Maine, Maryland, Massachusetts, New Hampshire, New Jersey, New York, Pennsylvania, Puerto Rico, Rhode Island, Vermont, U.S. Virgin Islands San Francisco - Alaska, American Samoa, Arizona, California, States of Micronesia, Hawaii, Idaho, Montana, Nevada, Oregon, Utah, Washington, Wyoming, Guam, States of Micronesia

name: REPDTE
label: Report Date
definition: The last day of the financial reporting period selected.

name: REPOPURF
label: Reverse Repo Agreements Purchased - Foreign Offices
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Securities sold under agreements to repurchase held in foreign offices. Available starting in 2002.

name: REPORT_TYPE
label: Report Type
definition: Type of Report

name: REPOSLDF
label: Reverse Repo Agreements Sold - Foreign Offices
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Securities purchased under agreements to resell held in foreign offices. Available starting in 2002.

name: RERNASTR
label: Earning Assets To Total Assets
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Interest earning assets as a percent of total assets

name: RISDATE
label: Report Date
definition: The financial reporting period selected in CCYYMM format.

name: RISUPDDATE
label: The Date That The Financial Data Was Updated for That Quarter in Ccyymmdd Format
definition: The date that the financial data was run in the Research Information System (RIS) database in CCYYMMDD format. For example 20080206 is February 6, 2008.

name: ROA
label: Return on Assets
definition: ( YTD, % ) Net income after taxes and extraordinary items (annualized) as a percent of average total assets. Bank net income (including gains or losses on securities and extraordinary items) as a percentage of average total (consolidated) assets. The basic yardstick of bank profitability.

name: ROAPTX
label: Pretax Return on Assets
definition: ( YTD, % ) Annualized pre-tax net income as a percent of average assets. Note: Includes extraordinary items and other adjustments, net of taxes.

name: ROAPTXQ
label: Pretax Return on Assets
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Quarterly pre-tax net income as a percent of average assets. Note: Includes extraordinary items and other adjustments, net of taxes.

name: ROAQ
label: Return on Assets
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Quarterly net income after taxes and extraordinary items as a percent of average total assets.

name: ROE
label: Return on Equity
definition: ( YTD, % ) Annualized bank net income ((including gains or losses on securities and extraordinary items) as a percent of average total equity on a consolidated basis. Note: If retained earnings are negative, the ratio is shown as NA.

name: ROEINJR
label: Retained Earnings to Average Equity (YTD only)
definition: ( YTD, % ) Net income (year-to-date, annualized), less cash dividends declared (year-to-date, annualized), as a percent of average total equity capital. If retained earnings are negative, the ratio is shown as NA. This ratio is presented on a year-to-date basis only.

name: ROEQ
label: Return on Equity
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Quarterly net income (including gains or losses on securities and extraordinary items) as a percentage of average total equity capital.

name: RSCI
label: C&I Loans Restructured and in Compliance
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Commercial and industrial loans restructured and in compliance with modified terms on a consolidated basis. available as of March 2011

name: RSCONS
label: Construction Loans Restructured and in Compliance
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total construction, land development and other land loans secured by real estate which are restructured and in compliance with modified terms in domestic offices.  Available as of March 2011

name: RSLNLS
label: Loans Secured by Non 1-4 Family Residential in Compliance with their Modified Terms
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Loans and lease financing receivables, other then 1-4 family, restructured and in compliance with modified terms on a consolidated basis. notes: (1) excludes loans secured by 1-4 family properties in domestic offices and loans to Individuals for household, family and other personal expenditures for CALL report filers. (2) Listed as memoranda only and is included in loans and leases, gross. (3) For TFR filers, includes restructured real estate held for investment.

name: RSLNLSR
label: Loans Secured by Non 1-4 Family Residential in Compliance with their Modified Terms
definition: ( YTD, % ) Loans and lease financing receivables, other then 1-4 family, restructured and in compliance with modified terms on a consolidated basis. notes: (1) excludes loans secured by 1-4 family properties in domestic offices and loans to Individuals for household, family and other personal expenditures for CALL report filers. (2) Listed as memoranda only and is included in loans and leases, gross. (3) For TFR filers, includes restructured real estate held for investment.

name: RSLNLTOT
label: Restructured Loans and Leases
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total rloans and leases restructured and in compliance with modified terms. note: beginning March 2008, includes loans secured by 1-4 family residential properties for CALL report filers

name: RSLNREFM
label: Loans Secured by 1-4 Family Residential in Compliance with their Modified Terms
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Loans secured by 1-4 family residential properties in domestic offices which are restructured and in compliance with the modified terms. Available for Call reporters as of March 2008 and TFR reporters as of March 2011.

name: RSLNREFMR
label: Loans Secured by 1-4 Family Residential in Compliance with their Modified Terms
definition: ( YTD, % ) Loans secured by 1-4 family residential properties in domestic offices which are restructured and in compliance with the modified terms. Available for Call reporters as of March 2008 and TFR reporters as of March 2011.

name: RSLNTOTR
label: Restructured Loans and Leases
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total rloans and leases restructured and in compliance with modified terms. note: beginning March 2008, includes loans secured by 1-4 family residential properties for CALL report filers

name: RSMULT
label: Multifamily Loans Restructured and in Compliance
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Loans secured by multifamily (5 or more) residential properties which are restructured and in compliance with modified terms in domestic offices.  Available as of March 2011

name: RSNRES
label: Nonfarm Nonresidential Loans Restructured and in Compliance
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total rloans secured by nonfarm nonresidential properties which are restructured and in compliance with modified terms in domestic offices. available as of March 2011

name: RSOTH
label: All Other Loans and Leases restructured
definition: ( Qtly, % ) All other loans and lease financing receivables restructured and in compliance with modified terms. This excludes loans to Individuals for household, family, and other personal expenditures.

name: RSOTHER
label: All Other Loans Restructured and in Compliance
definition: ( Qtly, % ) All other loans (includes loans to Individuals for household, family, and other personal expenditures) restructured and in compliance with modified terms. Beginning in March 2011, this item includes loans secured by farmland in domestic offices, loans to depository institutions and acceptances of other banks, loans to finance agricultural production and other loans to farmers, loans to Individuals for household, family and other personal expenditures, loans to foreign governments and official institutions, loans secured by real estate in foreign offices and other loans.

name: RSRE
label: Loans Secured By Real Estate
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total loans secured by real estate restructured and in compliance with modified terms.

name: RSRENUS
label: Secured by real estate to Non-U.S. Addressees
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total loans secured by real estate (other than those secured by 1-4 family residential properties) restructured and in compliance with modified terms to non-U.S. addressees.

name: RSSDHCR
label: RSSDID - High Regulatory Holder
definition: The unique number assigned by the Federal Reserve Board to the regulatory high holding company of the institution.

name: RSSDID
label: Federal Reserve ID Number
definition: A unique number assigned by the Federal Reserve Board as the entity's unique identifier (RSSDID) for the Regulatory top holder. note: information on bank holding companies is only as of quarter-end. Regulatory top holder is any company that directly or indirectly owns, controls or has power to vote 25 percent or more of a bank's or direct holding company's shares or controls in any manner the election of a majority of the directors or trustees of a bank or direct holding company or exercises a controlling influence over the management or policies of a bank or direct holding company. Information on Thrift Holding Companies that own Savings Associations but do not own banks is not currently available in the ID System. Source: Federal Reserve Board National Information Center data base.

name: RT
label: Interest Rate Contracts
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Notional amount of all interest rate contracts.

name: RTFFC
label: Futures and Forward Contracts interest rate contracts
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Futures and forward interest rate contracts (gross amounts) in which the predominant risk characteristic is interest rate risk. These contracts represent agreements for delayed delivery of financial instruments or commodities in which the buyer agrees to purchase and the seller agrees to deliver, at a specified future date, a specified instrument or commodity at a specified price or yield. Futures contracts are standardized and are traded on organized exchanges that act as the counterpart to each contract. Forward contracts are not traded on organized exchanges and their contractual terms are not standardized.

name: RTFFCR
label: Futures and Forward Contracts interest rate contracts
definition: ( YTD, % ) Futures and forward interest rate contracts (gross amounts) in which the predominant risk characteristic is interest rate risk. These contracts represent agreements for delayed delivery of financial instruments or commodities in which the buyer agrees to purchase and the seller agrees to deliver, at a specified future date, a specified instrument or commodity at a specified price or yield. Futures contracts are standardized and are traded on organized exchanges that act as the counterpart to each contract. Forward contracts are not traded on organized exchanges and their contractual terms are not standardized.

name: RTNVS
label: Notional Value of Interest Rate Swaps
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The notional amount of all outstanding interest rate and basis swaps whose predominant characteristic is interest rate risk. The notional amount is the underlying principal amount upon which the exchange of interest is based.

name: RTNVSR
label: Notional Value of Interest Rate Swaps
definition: ( YTD, % ) The notional amount of all outstanding interest rate and basis swaps whose predominant characteristic is interest rate risk. The notional amount is the underlying principal amount upon which the exchange of interest is based.

name: RTPOC
label: Purchased Option Contracts Interest Rate
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The par value of a financial instrument underlying a futures contract whose underlying risk characteristic is interest rate risk. Also included are contracts known as caps, floors, collars, corridors, and swaps.

name: RTPOCR
label: Purchased Option Contracts Interest Rate
definition: ( YTD, % ) The par value of a financial instrument underlying a futures contract whose underlying risk characteristic is interest rate risk. Also included are contracts known as caps, floors, collars, corridors, and swaps.

name: RTR
label: Interest Rate Contracts
definition: ( YTD, % ) Notional amount of all interest rate contracts.

name: RTWOC
label: Written Option Interest Rate Contracts
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Written option interest rate contracts (gross amounts) that the reporting institution has obligated itself to either purchase or sell that are outstanding as of the report date. It includes both written exchange-traded and written over-the-counter (OTC) interest rate options. Exchange-traded option contracts obligate the reporting institution to either purchase or sell an interest rate futures contract whose predominant risk characteristic is interest rate risk. OTC interest rate options include options to purchase and sell interest bearing financial instruments whose predominant risk characteristic is interest rate risk as well as contracts known as caps, floors, collars, corridors, and swaptions.

name: RTWOCR
label: Written Option Interest Rate Contracts
definition: ( YTD, % ) Written option interest rate contracts (gross amounts) that the reporting institution has obligated itself to either purchase or sell that are outstanding as of the report date. It includes both written exchange-traded and written over-the-counter (OTC) interest rate options. Exchange-traded option contracts obligate the reporting institution to either purchase or sell an interest rate futures contract whose predominant risk characteristic is interest rate risk. OTC interest rate options include options to purchase and sell interest bearing financial instruments whose predominant risk characteristic is interest rate risk as well as contracts known as caps, floors, collars, corridors, and swaptions.

name: RUNDATE
label: Run Date
definition: The day the institution information was updated.

name: RWAJ
label: Total Risk Weighted Assets
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Risk weighted assets-adjusted is based on the risk-based capital definitions for prompt corrective action (PCA) and includes: Call reporters: gross risk weighted assets (includes mkt risk equivalent assets) - disallowed ln&ls allowance (allowance for LN&LS MINUS RBC-LIMITED LN&LS ALLOWANCE) - ALLOCATED TRANSFER RISK RESERVE + UNREALIZED LOSS ON EQUITY SECURITIES and for Thrift Financial Reporters: Total RISK BASED CAPITAL + FULLY CAPITALIZED ITEMS * 12.5 - UNREALIZED HOLDING GAINS OR LOSSES ON AVAILABLE-FOR-SALE SECURITIES ADJUSTED ACCORDING TO FASB 115

name: RWAJT
label: Total Risk Weighted Assets Adjusted
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total risk weighted assets are assets adjusted for risk-based capital definitions which include on-balance-sheet as well as off-balance-sheet items multiplied by specified risk-weights. A conversion factor is used to assign a balance sheet equivalent amount for selected off-balance-sheet accounts.  As of March 2015, all institutions began reporting the amended CALL schedule RC-R Part I and Part II which incorporates the risk-based capital rules based on the Basel III framework and section 939A of the Dodd-Frank Act. Some designated Advanced Approaches institutions began reporting RC-R Part 1a based on the updated requirements as of March 2014. (See: FDIC Financial Institutions Letter FIL-24-2012) RIS data note: Prior to March 2014, this variable is the same as RIS variable RWAJ

name: RWAJTR
label: Total Risk Weighted Assets Adjusted
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total risk weighted assets are assets adjusted for risk-based capital definitions which include on-balance-sheet as well as off-balance-sheet items multiplied by specified risk-weights. A conversion factor is used to assign a balance sheet equivalent amount for selected off-balance-sheet accounts.  As of March 2015, all institutions began reporting the amended CALL schedule RC-R Part I and Part II which incorporates the risk-based capital rules based on the Basel III framework and section 939A of the Dodd-Frank Act. Some designated Advanced Approaches institutions began reporting RC-R Part 1a based on the updated requirements as of March 2014. (See: FDIC Financial Institutions Letter FIL-24-2012) RIS data note: Prior to March 2014, this variable is the same as RIS variable RWAJ

name: SASSER
label: Sasser Institutions
definition: ( Qtly, % ) OTS supervised savings associations that converted their charter to that of a commercial or savings bank. Converted associations remain members of the SAIF, but they become subject to supervision by one of the three federal banking agencies. Not Applicable as of March 31, 2006.

name: SC
label: Total Securities
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total rsecurities: the sum of held-to-maturity securities at amortized cost, available-for-sale debt securities at fair value and equity securities with readily determinable fair values not held for trading on a consolidated basis. prior to December 2020, defined as the sum of held-to-maturity securities at amortized cost, available-for-sale securities at fair value and equity securities with readily determinable fair values not held for trading on a consolidated basis. As of March 2019, for institutions that have adopted CECL Methodology (ASU 2016-13) securities are reported net of allowances for credit losses. The full implementation of FASB 115 became effective as of January 1, 1994. Beginning on that date, a portion of banks' securities portfolios are reported based upon fair (market) values; previously, all securities not held in trading accounts were reported at either amortized cost or the lower of cost or market. A negative total securities amount indicates a TFR Reporter with assets held in trading accounts that exceed total securities.

name: SC1LES
label: Fixed and floating rate debt securities (included above) with remaining maturity of one year or less
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Fixed and floating rate debt securities with a remaining maturity of one year or less. Held-to-maturity debt securities are reported at amortized cost. Available-for-sale debt securities are reported at fair value. This excludes debt securities that are in nonaccrual status. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: SC1LESR
label: Fixed and floating rate debt securities (included above) with remaining maturity of one year or less
definition: ( YTD, % ) Fixed and floating rate debt securities with a remaining maturity of one year or less. Held-to-maturity debt securities are reported at amortized cost. Available-for-sale debt securities are reported at fair value. This excludes debt securities that are in nonaccrual status. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: SCABS
label: Asset Backed Securities
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The amortized cost of held-to-maturity and the fair value of available-for-sale for all asset-backed securities (other than mortgage-backed securities), including asset-backed commercial paper, not held for trading. Prior to 2001, asset backed securities are included in “Other domestic debt securities” for those institutions that file a FFIEC Call Report. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: SCABSR
label: Asset Backed Securities
definition: ( YTD, % ) The amortized cost of held-to-maturity and the fair value of available-for-sale for all asset-backed securities (other than mortgage-backed securities), including asset-backed commercial paper, not held for trading. Prior to 2001, asset backed securities are included in “Other domestic debt securities” for those institutions that file a FFIEC Call Report. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: SCAF
label: Available for Sale (Fair Market Value)
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total available-for-sale debt securities at fair value on a consolidated basis. Prior to December 2020, defined as:   total available-for-sale securities at fair value on a consolidated basis. Beginning March 2018, does not include equity securities for institutions that have adopted ASU2016-01. For IBA reporters, this is a memo item only. For TFR filers (prior to 2012) this is estimated available-for-sale securities.

name: SCAFR
label: Available for Sale (Fair Market Value)
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total available-for-sale debt securities at fair value on a consolidated basis. Prior to December 2020, defined as:   total available-for-sale securities at fair value on a consolidated basis. Beginning March 2018, does not include equity securities for institutions that have adopted ASU2016-01. For IBA reporters, this is a memo item only. For TFR filers (prior to 2012) this is estimated available-for-sale securities.

name: SCAFSRES
label: Credit Loss Allowance for Available for Sale Debt Securities
definition: ( Qtly, % ) The balance of the allowance for credit losses on available-for-sale debt securities at the end of the current reporting period. begins in March 2019 and is reported only by institutions that have adopted cecl methodology (asu 2016-13).

name: SCAGE
label: U.S. Government Agency Obligations
definition: ( Qtly, $ and % ) The amortized cost and fair value of all U.S. Government agency obligations not held for trading. Excludes commercial mortgage-backed securities as of June 2009. for TFR reporters, this includes the aforementioned securities held in trading accounts, u.s. treasury securities, and mortgage derivatives securities.

name: SCAOT
label: U.S. Government Agencies
definition: ( Qtly, % ) U.S. government agency and corporation obligations (excluding mortgage-backed securities) issued by U.S. government agencies held-to-maturity at amortized cost and available-for-sale at fair value on a consolidated basis. Prior to 1994, this item was reported on a domestic basis. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: SCASPNSUM
label: Non-mortgage Backed: Issued by U.S. Government Agencies or Sponsored (GSEs)
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The total amount of securities borrowed on both a collateralized and uncollateralized basis.

name: SCASPNSUMR
label: Non-mortgage Backed: Issued by U.S. Government Agencies or Sponsored (GSEs)
definition: ( YTD, % ) The total amount of securities borrowed on both a collateralized and uncollateralized basis.

name: SCBORROW
label: Securities Borrowed
definition: ( Qtly, % ) The total amount of securities borrowed on both a collateralized and uncollateralized basis.

name: SCCMMB
label: Other commercial mortgage-backed securities
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Total commercial mortgage-backed securities on a consolidated basis.    Note: Data begins in June 2009. This item is not available for TFR Reporters .   Modification starting March 2011: SCCMMB= SUM (SCCMMB,-SCCPTG,-SCCMOG)

name: SCCMOG
label: Commercial Mortgage-Backed Other Securities Issued or Guaranteed by U.S. Government Agencies or Sponsored Agencies
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Other commercial mortgage-backed securities issued or guaranteed by U.S. Government agencies or sponsored agencies - the amortized cost and fair value of all CMOs, REMICs, CMO and REMIC residuals, stripped mortgage-backed securities, and commercial paper backed by loans secured by properties other than 1-4 family residential properties that have been issued by U.S. Government agencies or U.S. Government-sponsored agencies. TFR Reporters.

name: SCCMOGR
label: Commercial Mortgage-Backed Other Securities Issued or Guaranteed by U.S. Government Agencies or Sponsored Agencies
definition: ( YTD, % ) Other commercial mortgage-backed securities issued or guaranteed by U.S. Government agencies or sponsored agencies - the amortized cost and fair value of all CMOs, REMICs, CMO and REMIC residuals, stripped mortgage-backed securities, and commercial paper backed by loans secured by properties other than 1-4 family residential properties that have been issued by U.S. Government agencies or U.S. Government-sponsored agencies. TFR Reporters.

name: SCCMOS
label: Other Commercial Mortgage-Backed Securities
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Other Commercial Mortgage-Backed Securities

name: SCCMOSR
label: Other Commercial Mortgage-Backed Securities
definition: ( YTD, % ) Other Commercial Mortgage-Backed Securities

name: SCCMOT
label: Other Commercial Mortgage Securities
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Other commercial mortgage-backed securities (issued by U.S. government-sponsored agencies or by others) on a consolidated basis note: data back to 2009

name: SCCMOTR
label: Other Commercial Mortgage Securities
definition: ( YTD, % ) Other commercial mortgage-backed securities (issued by U.S. government-sponsored agencies or by others) on a consolidated basis note: data back to 2009

name: SCCMPT
label: Commercial Mortgage Pass-Through Securities
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Commercial mortgage pass-through securities (issued by U.S. government-sponsored agencies or by others) on a consolidated basis    Note: Data begins in June 2009

name: SCCMPTR
label: Commercial Mortgage Pass-Through Securities
definition: ( YTD, % ) Commercial mortgage pass-through securities (issued by U.S. government-sponsored agencies or by others) on a consolidated basis    Note: Data begins in June 2009

name: SCCOL
label: Collateralized Mortgage Obligations (CMOs) and REMICS Issued by U.S. Government Agencies or Sponsored Agencies
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Mortgage-backed securities – Collateralized Mortgage Obligations. The amortized cost and fair value of all classes of CMOs and REMICs, CMO and REMIC residuals, and stripped mortgage-backed securities issued or guaranteed by U.S. Government agencies or U.S. Government-sponsored agencies that are backed by loans secured by 1-4 family residential properties. Also includes REMICs issued by the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) that are backed by 1-4 family residential mortgages in this item. Includes privately issued CMOs and REMICS (including securities held in trading accounts) for TFR reporters. u.s. government agencies include, but are not limited to, such agencies as the government national mortgage association (gnma), the federal deposit insurance corporation (FDIC), and the national credit union administration (ncua). u.s. government-sponsored agencies include, but are not limited to, such agencies as the federal home loan mortgage corporation (fhlmc) and the federal national mortgage association (fnma). beginning January 1, 1994, this item consists of both securities designated as held-to-maturity, reported at amortized cost, and securities designated as available-for-sale, reported at fair market value.

name: SCCOLR
label: Collateralized Mortgage Obligations (CMOs) and REMICS Issued by U.S. Government Agencies or Sponsored Agencies
definition: ( YTD, % ) Mortgage-backed securities – Collateralized Mortgage Obligations. The amortized cost and fair value of all classes of CMOs and REMICs, CMO and REMIC residuals, and stripped mortgage-backed securities issued or guaranteed by U.S. Government agencies or U.S. Government-sponsored agencies that are backed by loans secured by 1-4 family residential properties. Also includes REMICs issued by the U.S. Department of Veterans Affairs (VA) that are backed by 1-4 family residential mortgages in this item. Includes privately issued CMOs and REMICS (including securities held in trading accounts) for TFR reporters. u.s. government agencies include, but are not limited to, such agencies as the government national mortgage association (gnma), the federal deposit insurance corporation (FDIC), and the national credit union administration (ncua). u.s. government-sponsored agencies include, but are not limited to, such agencies as the federal home loan mortgage corporation (fhlmc) and the federal national mortgage association (fnma). beginning January 1, 1994, this item consists of both securities designated as held-to-maturity, reported at amortized cost, and securities designated as available-for-sale, reported at fair market value.

name: SCCPTG
label: Commercial Mortgage-Backed Pass-Through Securities Issued or Guaranteed by F.N.M.A., F.H.L.M.C., or G.N.M.A.
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Commercial mortgage pass-through securities issued or guaranteed by FNMA, FHLMC, or GNMA held-to-maturity at amortized cost and available-for-sale at fair value on a consolidated basis. note: data begins in March 2011 and is not available for TFR reporters.

name: SCCPTGR
label: Commercial Mortgage-Backed Pass-Through Securities Issued or Guaranteed by F.N.M.A., F.H.L.M.C., or G.N.M.A.
definition: ( YTD, % ) Commercial mortgage pass-through securities issued or guaranteed by FNMA, FHLMC, or GNMA held-to-maturity at amortized cost and available-for-sale at fair value on a consolidated basis. note: data begins in March 2011 and is not available for TFR reporters.

name: SCDOMO
label: Other Domestic Debt Securities
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total rother domestic debt securities. For TFR Reporters, this item includes securities held in trading accounts but excludes privately-issued collateralized mortgage obligations (including REMICs). Institutions with less than $100 million in total assets include 'foreign debt securities' in 'other domestic debt securities'. Includes securities held in trading accounts for TFR reporters.

name: SCDOMOR
label: Other Domestic Debt Securities
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total rother domestic debt securities. For TFR Reporters, this item includes securities held in trading accounts but excludes privately-issued collateralized mortgage obligations (including REMICs). Institutions with less than $100 million in total assets include 'foreign debt securities' in 'other domestic debt securities'. Includes securities held in trading accounts for TFR reporters.

name: SCEQ
label: Equity Securities Available for Sale
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total requity securities available-for-sale at fair value not held in trading. This item ends in 2020. See equity securities readily determinable fair values (SCEQFV) Beginning in March 2018, does not include equity securities with readily determinable fair values not held for trading (FASB ASU 2016-01 adopters). Before March 2012, includes securities held in trading accounts for TFR Reporters.

name: SCEQFV
label: Equity Readily Determinable Fair Values
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Equity securities with readily determinable fair values not held for trading. Includes all investments in mutual funds and other equity securities with easy to determine fair values that are not held for trading. Such securities include, but are not limited to, money market mutual funds, mutual funds that invest solely in U.S. Government securities, common stock, and perpetual preferred stock. begins in March 2018 and is reported by institutions that have adopted fasb accounting standards update no. 2016-01 (asu 2016-01) for fiscal years beginning after December 15, 2017.

name: SCEQFVR
label: Equity Readily Determinable Fair Values
definition: ( YTD, % ) Equity securities with readily determinable fair values not held for trading. Includes all investments in mutual funds and other equity securities with easy to determine fair values that are not held for trading. Such securities include, but are not limited to, money market mutual funds, mutual funds that invest solely in U.S. Government securities, common stock, and perpetual preferred stock. begins in March 2018 and is reported by institutions that have adopted fasb accounting standards update no. 2016-01 (asu 2016-01) for fiscal years beginning after December 15, 2017.

name: SCEQNFT
label: Equity Securities Not Held for Trading
definition: ( YTD, $ ) All other equity securities available-for-sale at fair value. This item includes equity securities without readily determinable fair values at historical cost. Available for Call Reporters only.

name: SCEQO
label: Other Equity Securities
definition: ( Qtly, % ) All other equity securities available-for-sale at fair value. This item includes equity securities without readily determinable fair values at historical cost. Available for Call Reporters only.

name: SCFMN
label: Residential Mortgage Backed: Pass-Through Issued by F.N.M.A. and F.H.L.M.C.
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Mortgage-backed pass-through securities issued by FNMA and FHLMC held-to-maturity at amortized cost and available-for-sale at fair value on a consolidated basis. Beginning January 1, 1994, this item consists of both securities designated as held-to-maturity, reported at amortized cost, and securities designated as available-for-sale, reported at fair market value  As of June 2018 this also includes Mortgage-backed pass-through securities issued by GNMA for banks filing a FFIEC Call Report 51 , or banks with domestic offices only and Total Assets < $1 billion.  This item is not available for TFR Reporters. The last TFR was filed in December 2011.

name: SCFMNR
label: Residential Mortgage Backed: Pass-Through Issued by F.N.M.A. and F.H.L.M.C.
definition: ( YTD, % ) Mortgage-backed pass-through securities issued by FNMA and FHLMC held-to-maturity at amortized cost and available-for-sale at fair value on a consolidated basis. Beginning January 1, 1994, this item consists of both securities designated as held-to-maturity, reported at amortized cost, and securities designated as available-for-sale, reported at fair market value  As of June 2018 this also includes Mortgage-backed pass-through securities issued by GNMA for banks filing a FFIEC Call Report 51 , or banks with domestic offices only and Total Assets < $1 billion.  This item is not available for TFR Reporters. The last TFR was filed in December 2011.

name: SCFORD
label: Foreign Debt Securities
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total rof all foreign debt securities held-to-maturity at amortized cost and available-for-sale at fair value. Prior to 2001, institutions that filed a FFIEC Call Report and had less than less than $100 million in total assets included 'foreign debt securities' in 'other domestic debt securities'. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: SCFORDR
label: Foreign Debt Securities
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total rof all foreign debt securities held-to-maturity at amortized cost and available-for-sale at fair value. Prior to 2001, institutions that filed a FFIEC Call Report and had less than less than $100 million in total assets included 'foreign debt securities' in 'other domestic debt securities'. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: SCGNM
label: Residential Mortgage Backed: Pass-Through Issued by G.N.M.A.
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Mortgage-backed pass-through securities guaranteed by GNMA held-to-maturity at amortized cost and available-for-sale at fair value on a consolidated basis. Beginning January 1, 1994, this item consists of both securities designated as held-to-maturity, reported at amortized cost, and securities designated as available-for-sale, reported at fair market value. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: SCGNMR
label: Residential Mortgage Backed: Pass-Through Issued by G.N.M.A.
definition: ( YTD, % ) Mortgage-backed pass-through securities guaranteed by GNMA held-to-maturity at amortized cost and available-for-sale at fair value on a consolidated basis. Beginning January 1, 1994, this item consists of both securities designated as held-to-maturity, reported at amortized cost, and securities designated as available-for-sale, reported at fair market value. This item is not available for TFR Reporters. TFR Reports ended in December 2011.

name: SCGTY
label: Certificates of Participation in Pools of Residential Mortgages Issued or Guaranteed by U.S.
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Mortgage-backed pass-through securities held-to-maturity at amortized cost and available-for-sale at fair value, which are issued or guaranteed through FNMA, FHLMC, or GNMA. This includes securities held in trading accounts for TFR Reporters.

name: SCGTYR
label: Certificates of Participation in Pools of Residential Mortgages Issued or Guaranteed by U.S.
definition: ( YTD, % ) Mortgage-backed pass-through securities held-to-maturity at amortized cost and available-for-sale at fair value, which are issued or guaranteed through FNMA, FHLMC, or GNMA. This includes securities held in trading accounts for TFR Reporters.

name: SCHA
label: Held to Maturity (Book Value)
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Total securities designated to be held to maturity, reported at amortized cost (book value).

name: SCHAR
label: Held to Maturity (Book Value)
definition: ( YTD, % ) Total securities designated to be held to maturity, reported at amortized cost (book value).

name: SCHTMRES
label: Less: Allowance for Credit Losses on Held to Maturity Debt Securities
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Credit Loss Allowance for Held to Maturity Debt Securities The balance of the allowance for credit losses on held-to-maturity debt securities at the end of the current reporting period. as of March 2019, reported only by institutions that have adopted cecl methodology ( (ASU 2016-13)

name: SCHTMRESR
label: Less: Allowance for Credit Losses on Held to Maturity Debt Securities
definition: ( YTD, % ) Credit Loss Allowance for Held to Maturity Debt Securities The balance of the allowance for credit losses on held-to-maturity debt securities at the end of the current reporting period. as of March 2019, reported only by institutions that have adopted cecl methodology ( (ASU 2016-13)

name: SCLENT
label: Securities Lent
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Either the book value of securities lent on both a collateralized and uncollateralized basis or the market value of customers' securities lent, where the customer is indemnified against loss by the institution.

name: SCLENTR
label: Securities Lent
definition: ( YTD, % ) Either the book value of securities lent on both a collateralized and uncollateralized basis or the market value of customers' securities lent, where the customer is indemnified against loss by the institution.

name: SCMA1LES
label: Fixed and Floating Rate Debt Securities - One Year Or Less
definition: ( Qtly, % ) Fixed and floating rate debt securities with a remaining maturity of one year or less. Held-to-maturity debt securities are reported at amortized cost. Available-for-sale debt securities are reported at fair value. This excludes debt securities that are in nonaccrual status.

name: SCMTGBK
label: Mortgage-Backed Securities
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Mortgage-backed securities include: US Government agency and corporation issued obligations or guaranteed certificates of participation in pools of residential mortgages. US Government agency and corporation obligations and collateralized mortgage obligations issued by the Federal National Mortgage Association and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (including real estate mortgage investment conduits - REMICS). Other domestic debt private securities - (i.e., non-government issued or guaranteed) and certificates of participation in pools of residential mortgages. Other domestic debt securities that are privately issued and collateralized mortgage obligations (including REMICS). Effective March 1994, the full implementation of FASB 115 states that a portion of banks' mortgage-backed securities portfolio is now reported based upon fair (market) values; previously, all mortgage-backed securities not held in trading accounts were reported at either amortized cost or lower of cost or market.

name: SCMTGBKR
label: Mortgage-Backed Securities
definition: ( YTD, % ) Mortgage-backed securities include: US Government agency and corporation issued obligations or guaranteed certificates of participation in pools of residential mortgages. US Government agency and corporation obligations and collateralized mortgage obligations issued by the Federal National Mortgage Association and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation (including real estate mortgage investment conduits - REMICS). Other domestic debt private securities - (i.e., non-government issued or guaranteed) and certificates of participation in pools of residential mortgages. Other domestic debt securities that are privately issued and collateralized mortgage obligations (including REMICS). Effective March 1994, the full implementation of FASB 115 states that a portion of banks' mortgage-backed securities portfolio is now reported based upon fair (market) values; previously, all mortgage-backed securities not held in trading accounts were reported at either amortized cost or lower of cost or market.

name: SCMUNI
label: Securities Issued by States & Political Subdivisions
definition: ( YTD, $ )  The amortized cost and fair value of all securities issued by states and political subdivisions in the U.S. not held for trading. Includes securities held in trading accounts for TFR Reporters.

name: SCMUNIR
label: Securities Issued by States & Political Subdivisions
definition: ( YTD, % )  The amortized cost and fair value of all securities issued by states and political subdivisions in the U.S. not held for trading. Includes securities held in trading accounts for TFR Reporters.

name: SCNM1T3
label: Debt Securities Maturity Or Repricing: Over One Year Through Three Years
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Fixed and floating rate debt securities with a remaining maturity or next repricing date of over one year through three years. This includes securities that are issued by the U.S. Treasury, U.S. government agencies, and states and political subdivisions; other non-mortgage debt securities, and mortgage pass-through securities other than those backed by closed-end first lien 1-4 family residential mortgages. This excludes debt securities that are in nonaccrual status. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: SCNM1T3R
label: Debt Securities Maturity Or Repricing: Over One Year Through Three Years
definition: ( YTD, % ) Fixed and floating rate debt securities with a remaining maturity or next repricing date of over one year through three years. This includes securities that are issued by the U.S. Treasury, U.S. government agencies, and states and political subdivisions; other non-mortgage debt securities, and mortgage pass-through securities other than those backed by closed-end first lien 1-4 family residential mortgages. This excludes debt securities that are in nonaccrual status. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: SCNM3LES
label: Debt Securities Maturity Or Repricing: Three Months Or Less
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Fixed and floating rate debt securities with a remaining maturity or next repricing date of three months or less. This includes securities that are issued by the U.S. Treasury, U.S. government agencies, and states and political subdivisions; other non-mortgage debt securities, and mortgage pass-through securities other than those backed by closed-end first lien 1-4 family residential mortgages. This excludes debt securities that are in nonaccrual status. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: SCNM3LESR
label: Debt Securities Maturity Or Repricing: Three Months Or Less
definition: ( YTD, % ) Fixed and floating rate debt securities with a remaining maturity or next repricing date of three months or less. This includes securities that are issued by the U.S. Treasury, U.S. government agencies, and states and political subdivisions; other non-mortgage debt securities, and mortgage pass-through securities other than those backed by closed-end first lien 1-4 family residential mortgages. This excludes debt securities that are in nonaccrual status. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: SCNM3T12
label: Debt Securities Maturity Or Repricing: Over Three Months Through Twelve Months
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Fixed and floating rate debt securities with a remaining maturity or next repricing date of over three months through twelve months. This includes securities that are issued by the U.S. Treasury, U.S. government agencies, and states and political subdivisions; other non-mortgage debt securities, and mortgage pass-through securities other than those backed by closed-end first lien 1-4 family residential mortgages. This excludes debt securities that are in nonaccrual status. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: SCNM3T12R
label: Debt Securities Maturity Or Repricing: Over Three Months Through Twelve Months
definition: ( YTD, % ) Fixed and floating rate debt securities with a remaining maturity or next repricing date of over three months through twelve months. This includes securities that are issued by the U.S. Treasury, U.S. government agencies, and states and political subdivisions; other non-mortgage debt securities, and mortgage pass-through securities other than those backed by closed-end first lien 1-4 family residential mortgages. This excludes debt securities that are in nonaccrual status. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: SCNM3T5
label: Debt Securities Maturity Or Repricing: Over Three Years Through Five Years
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Fixed and floating rate debt securities with a remaining maturity or next repricing date of over three years through five years. This includes securities that are issued by the U.S. Treasury, U.S. government agencies, and states and political subdivisions; other non-mortgage debt securities, and mortgage pass-through securities other than those backed by closed-end first lien 1-4 family residential mortgages. This excludes debt securities that are in nonaccrual status. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: SCNM3T5R
label: Debt Securities Maturity Or Repricing: Over Three Years Through Five Years
definition: ( YTD, % ) Fixed and floating rate debt securities with a remaining maturity or next repricing date of over three years through five years. This includes securities that are issued by the U.S. Treasury, U.S. government agencies, and states and political subdivisions; other non-mortgage debt securities, and mortgage pass-through securities other than those backed by closed-end first lien 1-4 family residential mortgages. This excludes debt securities that are in nonaccrual status. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: SCNM5T15
label: Debt Securities Maturity Or Repricing: Over Five Years Through Fifteen Years
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Fixed and floating rate debt securities with a remaining maturity or next repricing date of over five years through fifteen years. This includes securities that are issued by the U.S. Treasury, U.S. government agencies, and states and political subdivisions; other non-mortgage debt securities, and mortgage pass-through securities other than those backed by closed-end first lien 1-4 family residential mortgages. This excludes debt securities that are in nonaccrual status. This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: SCNM5T15R
label: Debt Securities Maturity Or Repricing: Over Five Years Through Fifteen Years
definition: ( YTD, % ) Fixed and floating rate debt securities with a remaining maturity or next repricing date of over five years through fifteen years. name: SCSNHAA
label: Structured Notes Amortized Cost
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Amortized cost of structured notes (included in the held-to-maturity and available-for-sale accounts). Structured notes are debt securities that combine straight fixed or variable rate instruments and derivative products that are issued by corporations and government sponsored enterprises including FNMA, the Federal Home Loan Bank and the Federal Home Loan Mortgage Corporation. Structured notes generally contain embedded options such as call options, interest rate caps and interest rate floors. They typically have cash flows that are linked to indices such as interest rates, foreign exchange rates, commodity prices and other financial variables. The cash flows of structured notes can vary in the timing and amount of cash received by the investor throughout the life of the security. This makes the valuation of cash flows and risks of these securities a complex process. prior to 2004, This item is not available for TFR Reporters.TFR reports ended December 2011.

name: STATNOTES
label: Notes on Statistical Capability and Averages:
definition: Notes on statistical capability: Ranking and Statistics report: This report can be selected from the ‘Create and modify reports’ page. It starts as a two column report with an institution in one column and a custom peer group that includes that institution in the other. (This report is best printed in Landscape orientation.) The report is generated with the institution values, rank and percent rank along with the peer’s non weighted average, minimum, maximum, median and standard deviation values for each line item. The rank is a tie-high ordering of the data from largest to smallest, largest being equal to number 1. The Percent<= is based on the Rank and indicates the percentage of the others in the group that have a value less than or equal to the institution’s value. Quarterly and year-to-date income items are available. Custom peer groups can be comprised of institutions and/or bank holding companies. Notes on Averages: Standard peer averages ID/SDI are weighted averages . The ratio is created from the sum of the numerators and sum of the dominators. The percent of the (sum all numerators) divided by the (sum of all denominators). Size differences can be a factor in weighted averages. 2.Custom Peers also allow non-weighted averages, which is the average of all the ratios in the peer. Each ratio is individually calculated for the bank and then the ratios for the peers are averaged. In a non-weighed peer each ratio is treated as one equal member of the group.

name: SUBCHAPS
label: Subchapter S Corporations
definition: The Small Business Job Protection Act of 1996 changed the Internal Revenue Code to allow financial institutions to elect Subchapter S corporation status, beginning in 1997. Banks are required to indicate on the Call Report whether there is currently in effect an election to file under Subchapter S. Thrifts have a similar requirement as of March 1998. The most important IRS requirements to elect and maintain Subchapter S status are: There can be no more than 75 eligible shareholders and no more than one class of stock. (In general, shareholders can only be individuals, estates, and certain types of trusts. Certain retirement plans and charitable organizations will be eligible in 1998.) All shareholders must consent. Banks and thrifts converting to Subchapter S status must use the specific charge-off method for tax purposes rather than the reserve method of accounting for bad debts and recapture tax bad debt reserves over a period of six years, if the reserve method had been used prior to conversion. (Note: even though the specific charge-off method is required for tax purposes, an adequate allowance for loan and lease losses must still be maintained on the financial statements and Call Reports.) Banks and thrifts are subject to a built-in gains (BIG) tax, if the aggregate fair market value of assets is greater than their aggregate adjusted bases on the date of conversion to Subchapter S status. [Banks are required to indicate separately on the Call Report in December of each year, the deferred portion of income taxes reported in net income. For Subchapter S banks, some or all of their deferred tax assets and liabilities may be eliminated upon conversion to Subchapter S status; however, deferred taxes related to the BIG tax and the recapture of bad debt reserves must be recognized.]. A Subchapter S corporation is treated as a pass-through entity, similar to a partnership, for federal income tax purposes. It is generally not subject to any federal income taxes at the corporate level. Its taxable income flows through to its shareholders in proportion to their stock ownership, and the shareholders generally pay federal income taxes on their share of this taxable income. This can have the effect of reducing institutions' reported income tax expense and increasing their after-tax earnings.. The election of Subchapter S status may result in an increase in shareholders' personal tax liabilities. Therefore, S corporations typically increase the amount of earnings distributed as dividends to compensate for higher personal taxes.

definition: The chartering agency of the surviving institution - State and Federal, and the Comptroller of the Currency - Chartering authority for nationally chartered commercial banks and for federally chartered savings associations (The Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) before 7/21/11) State (includes U.S. Territories) - Chartering authority for institutions that are not chartered by the OCC or OTS

name: SUR_CHARTER
label: Surviving OCC Charter Number
definition: A unique number of the surviving institution assigned by the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency (OCC) used to identify institutions that it has chartered and regulates (i.e. national banks).

name: SUR_CLASS
label: Surviving Class
definition: A code of the surviving institution indicating the its major grouping. N = FDIC Insured National Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; SM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, Federal Reserve Member; NM = FDIC Insured State Commercial Bank, or a Mutual Commercial Bank; SI = FDIC Insured Stock and Mutual Savings Bank; MI = Mutually Insured; SB = FDIC Insured Savings Bank; SL = FDIC Insured Savings and Loan Association; OI = Other FDIC Insured Institution (International Banking Act); CU = Credit Union; NC = Non-insured Institution; NS = Non-insured Savings Association

name: SUR_CLCODE
label: Surviving Class Code
definition: Numeric code which identifies the major and minor categories of a surviving institution.

name: SUR_CNTYNAME
label: Surviving County
definition: County where the surviving institution is physically located (abbreviated if the county name exceeds 16 characters).

name: SUR_CNTYNUM
label: Surviving County Number
definition: The federal information processing standards (FIPS) numeric code of the surviving institution.

name: SUR_FDICREGION
label: Surviving Supervisory Region Number
definition: A two-digit number indicating the FDIC Supervisory Division or Region of the surviving institution. 02 = New York; 05 = Atlanta; 09 = Chicago; 11 = Kansas City; 13 = Dallas; 14 = San Francisco; 16 = Office of Complex Financial Institutions (CFI)

name: SUR_FDICREGION_DESC
label: Surviving Supervisory Region Description
definition: A description associated with the name of an FDIC supervisory region of the surviving institution

name: SUR_INSAGENT1
label: Surviving Insurance Fund Membership
definition: Fund membership of the surviving institution; Deposit Insurance Fund (DIF), Bank Insurance Fund (BIF), Savings Association Insurance Fund (SAIF)

name: SUR_INSAGENT2
label: Surviving Secondary Insurance Fund
definition: As a result of the establishment of a single Deposit Insurance Fund (DIF) effective April 1, 2006, the Secondary Insurance fund is no longer applicable. Before April 1,2006, Bank Insurance Fund (BIF) or Savings Association Insurance Fund (SAIF)

name: SUR_INSTNAME
label: Surviving Institution Name
definition: The legal name of the surviving institution.

name: SUR_MADDR
label: Surviving Mailing Street Address
definition: Street address portion of the mailing address of the surviving institution.

name: SUR_MADDR2
label: Surviving Mailing Street Address Line 2
definition: Street address line 2 portion of the mailing address of the surviving institution.

name: SUR_MCITY
label: Survivng Mailing City
definition: City portion of the mailing address of the surviving institution. Either the entire name or part of the name of a specific city may be entered to produce an Institution List.

name: SUR_MSTALP
label: Surviving Mailing State Alpha Code
definition: 2-digit state alpha code portion of the mailing address of the surviving institution.

name: SUR_MSTATE
label: Surviving Mailing State
definition: State name portion of the mailing address of the surviving institution.

name: SUR_MZIP5
label: Surviving Mailing Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code portion of the mailing address of the surviving institution.

name: SUR_MZIP5_RAW
label: Surviving Mailing 5-Digit Zip Code
definition: The 5-digit postal code portion of the mailing address of the surviving institution.

name: SUR_PADDR
label: Surviving Physical Street Address
definition: Street address at which the surviving institution is physically located.

name: SUR_PADDR2
label: Surviving Physical Street Address Line 2
definition: Street address line 2 at which the surviving institution is physically located.

name: SUR_PCITY
label: Surviving Physical City
definition: City in which the surviving institution is physically located. Either the entire name or part of the name of a specific city may be entered to produce an Institution List.

name: SUR_PSTALP
label: Surviving Physical State Alpha Code
definition: State in which the surviving institution is physically located. The FDIC Act defines state as any State of the United States, the District of Columbia, and any territory of the United States, Puerto Rico, Guam, American Samoa, the Trust Territory of the Pacific Islands, the Virgin Island, and the Northern Mariana Islands.

name: SUR_PZIP5
label: Surviving Physical Zip Code
definition: The first three, four, or five digits of the full postal zip code in which the surviving institution is physically located.

name: SUR_PZIP5_RAW
label: Surviving Physical 5-Digit Zip Code
definition: The postal code (e.g. ZIP Code for the U.S.) in which the surviving institution is physically located.

name: SUR_PZIPREST
label: Surviving Physical Zip Code Extension
definition: The 4-digit zip code extension in which the surviving institution is physically located.

name: SUR_REGAGENT
label: Surviving Chartering Agency
definition: The chartering agency of the surviving institution - State and Federal, and the Comptroller of the Currency - Chartering authority for nationally chartered commercial banks and for federally chartered savings associations (The Office of Thrift Supervision (OTS) before 7/21/11) State (includes U.S. Territories) - Chartering authority for institutions that are not chartered by the OCC or OTS

name: SUR_TRUST
label: Surviving Trust Power
definition: The trust power status of the surviving institution.

name: SYS_LST_DTETME
label: System Last Datetime
definition: Last update to a record

name: TCAMA
label: Corporate Trust and Agency Accounts, Managed Assets
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The market value of assets held by the institution for all corporate trust and agency accounts held in managed fiduciary accounts. note: reported quarterly by institutions with total fiduciary assets of greater than $250 million (prior December) or with fiduciary and related services income of greater than 10% of revenue (net interest income plus noninterest income) for the prior calendar year. All others only report in December.

name: TCAMANUM
label: Corporate Trust and Agency, Number of Managed Accounts
definition: ( YTD, $ and % ) The number of accounts held by the institution for all corporate trust and agency accounts held in managed fiduciary accounts. note: reported quarterly by institutions with total fiduciary assets of greater than $250 million (prior December) or with fiduciary and related services income of greater than 10% of revenue (net interest income plus noninterest income) for the prior calendar year. All others only report in December.

name: TCANMA
label: Corporate Trust and Agency Accounts, Non-Managed Assets
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The market value of assets held by the institution for all corporate trust and agency accounts held in non-managed fiduciary accounts. note: reported quarterly by institutions with total fiduciary assets of greater than $250 million (prior December) or with fiduciary and related services income of greater than 10% of revenue (net interest income plus noninterest income) for the prior calendar year. All others only report in December.

name: TCANMNUM
label: Corporate Trust and Agency, Number of Non-Managed Accounts
definition: ( YTD, $ and % ) The number of accounts held by the institution for all corporate trust and agency accounts held in non-managed fiduciary accounts. note: reported quarterly by institutions with total fiduciary assets of greater than $250 million (prior December) or with fiduciary and related services income of greater than 10% of revenue (net interest income plus noninterest income) for the prior calendar year. All others only report in December.

name: TCANUM
label: Number of Corporate and Municipal Trusteeships Account Issues
definition: ( YTD, $ and % ) The total number of corporate and municipal issues, as well as other debt issues such as unit investment trust for which the institution serves as a trustee. only reported in December.

name: TCANUMD
label: Number of Corporate Trust and Agency Account Issues in Default
definition: ( YTD, $ and % ) Total rNumber Of Corporate And Municipal Issues, Including Equity Securities Such As Trust Preferred Securities And Other Debt Issues Such As Unit Investment Trusts, For Which The Institution Serves As Trustee And Are In Default. note: 1.) reported as a memoranda item in the December quarter only

name: TCAPAO
label: Principal Amount Outstanding Corporate Trust and Agency Accounts
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The total par value of outstanding debt securities for the issues reported in the number of corporate and municipal trusteeships for which the institution serves as trustee. only reported in December.

name: TCAPAOD
label: Amount of Corporate Trust and Agency Account Issues in Default
definition: ( YTD, $ ) Amount of corporate trust and agency account issues in default Note: Reported as a memoranda item in December only.

name: TCATNUM
label: Number of Transfer Agent, Registrar, Paying Agent and Other Corporate Agency Issues
definition: ( YTD, $ and % ) The total number of issues for which the institution acts in a corporate agency capacity. only reported in December.

name: TCDEMV
label: Domestic Equity, Collective Investment & Common Trust Funds Total Market Value
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The market value of collective investment funds (CIF's) and common trust funds operated by reporting institution - domestic equity. only reported in December.

name: TCDENUM
label: Domestic Equity, Collective Investment & Common Trust Funds Number
definition: ( YTD, $ and % ) The number of collective investment funds (CIF's) and common trust funds operated by reported institution – domestic equity. only reported in December.

name: TCIEMV
label: International/Global Equity, Collective Investment & Common Trust Funds Total Market Value
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The market value of collective investment funds (CIF's) and common trust funds operated by reporting institution - international/global equity. only reported in December.

name: TCIENUM
label: International/Global Equity, Collective Investment & Common Trust Funds Number
definition: ( YTD, $ and % )  International/Global Equity The number of collective investment funds (CIF's) and common trust funds operated by reporting institution - international/global equity. only reported in December.

name: TCMBMV
label: Municipal Bond, Collective Investment & Common Trust Funds Total Market Value
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The market value of collective investment funds (CIF's) and common trust funds operated by reporting institution - municipal bond. only reported in December.

name: TCMBNUM
label: Municipal Bond, Collective Investment & Common Trust Funds Number
definition: ( YTD, $ and % ) The number of collective investment funds (CIF's) and common trust funds operated by reporting institution - municipal bond. only reported in December.

name: TCSBMV
label: Stock/Bond Blend. Collective Investment & Common Trust Funds Total Market Value
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The market value of collective investment funds (CIF's) and common trust funds operated by reporting institution - stock/bond blend. only reported in December.

name: TCSBNUM
label: Stock/Bond Blend, Collective Investment & Common Trust Funds Number
definition: ( YTD, $ and % ) The number of collective investment funds (CIF's) and common trust funds operated by reporting institution - stock/bond blend. only reported in December.

name: TCSNMA
label: Custody and Safekeeping Accounts, Non-Managed Assets
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The market value of personal and institutional custody and safekeeping accounts held by the institution. Safekeeping and custody accounts are a type of agency account in which the reporting institution performs one or more specified agency functions but the institution is not a trustee and also is not responsible for managing the asset selection for account assets note: reported quarterly by institutions with total fiduciary assets of greater than $250 million (prior December) or with fiduciary and related services income of greater than 10% of revenue (net interest income plus noninterest income) for the prior calendar year. all others only report in December.

name: TCSNMNUM
label: Memo: Number of Non-Managed Custody and Safekeeping Accounts
definition: ( YTD, $ and % ) The number of personal and institutional custody and safekeeping accounts held by the institution. Safekeeping and custody accounts are a type of agency account in which the reporting institution performs one or more specified agency functions but the institution is not a trustee and also is not responsible for managing the the asset selection for account assets. note: reported quarterly by institutions with total fiduciary assets of greater than $250 million (prior December) or with fiduciary and related services income of greater than 10% of revenue (net interest income plus noninterest income) for the prior calendar year. all others only report in December.

name: TCSOMV
label: Specialty/Other, Collective Investment & Common Trust Funds Total Market Value
definition: ( YTD, $ ) The market value of collective investment funds (CIF's) and common trust funds operated by reporting institution - specialty/other. only reported in December.

name: TCSONUM

definition: ( YTD, $ and % ) The number of collective investment funds (CIF's) and common trust funds operated by reporting institution - specialty/other. only reported in December.

name: TCSTMV

definition: ( YTD, $ ) The market value of collective investment funds (CIF's) and common trust funds operated by reporting institution - short term investments/money market. only reported in December.

name: TCSTNUM
label: Short-Term investments/Money Market, Collective Investment & Common Trust Funds Number
definition: ( YTD, $ and % ) The number of collective investment funds (CIF's) and common trust funds operated by reporting institution - short term investments/money market. only reported in December.

name: TCTBMV

name: TE010N528
